// Version: 1
// Generated on: 2026-05-05 23:22:32

Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Water Utilities - Version 1'
}

Table treatment.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for facility']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Water treatment plants are cost centers in utility financial systems. Required for monthly variance reporting, rate case cost allocation, GASB facility-level expense reporting, and budget performance ']

  Note: 'Master record for each Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility. Captures facility identity, design capacity (MGD), treatment technology type (conventional, membrane, RO, UV, GAC), regulatory permit numbers, NPDES permit references, operational status, geographic location, and service area. This is the authoritative facility master for the treatment domain.'
}

Table treatment.process_unit {
  process_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for process_unit']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Treatment process units (filters, clarifiers, UV reactors, membranes) are installed via capital projects. Required for asset handover, tracking which project delivered each unit, warranty management, ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Treatment processes (filtration, disinfection, clarification) are often sub-cost centers for detailed expense tracking. Enables process-level cost allocation for rate case justification, efficiency an']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Treatment process units (filters, clarifiers, chemical feed systems) consume spare parts, filter media, membranes, and other materials tracked in material master. Essential for maintenance planning, i']

  Note: 'Defines the treatment process units and stages configured at each WTP/WWTP facility. Captures process unit type (coagulation, flocculation, sedimentation, filtration, chlorination, UV disinfection, RO, UF, MF, GAC, ozonation, biological treatment), process sequence order, design flow rate (GPM/MGD), operational parameters, and associated SCADA tag references in OSIsoft PI Historian. Serves as the process configuration master for treatment operations.'
}

Table treatment.process_reading {
  process_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each process reading record in the silver-layer curated telemetry dataset. Primary key for the process_reading data product.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Process readings during construction/commissioning track new equipment performance against project acceptance criteria. Substantial completion requires demonstration that installed equipment meets des']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed treatment plant operator who reviewed, approved, or manually entered/substituted this process reading. Applicable primarily to manually entered readings or operator-approved substitutions. Required for regulatory accountability under state operator certification requirements. Null for fully automated SCADA readings with Good quality flag.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant) where this process reading was generated. Links to the facility master record for site-level aggregation and regulatory reporting.']
  instrument_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset (e.g., flow meter, pressure transducer, turbidimeter, pH probe, chlorine analyzer) that generated this reading. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo for calibration tracking, maintenance history, and instrument lifecycle management.']
  meter_installation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Treatment process readings at flow measurement points reference the master meter installation measuring flow at that process stage. SCADA systems integrate process readings with meter installations fo']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed treatment plant operator who reviewed, approved, or manually entered/substituted this process reading. Applicable primarily to manually entered readings or operator-approved substitutions. Required for regulatory accountability under state operator certification requirements. Null for fully automated SCADA readings with Good quality flag.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Treatment process readings at clearwell and discharge points are monitored in context of destination pressure zone. Essential for CT compliance verification, pressure management, and SCADA alarming. O']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., clarifier, filter, UV reactor, chlorination chamber, RO train, GAC contactor) within the facility from which this reading originates. Enables process-unit-level performance analysis.']
  quality_mcl_exceedance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.exceedance. Business justification: Treatment process alarms (MCL trips, CT failures, turbidity spikes) trigger quality exceedance records for regulatory reporting. Links operational alarm event to compliance exceedance record for MOR/D']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset (e.g., flow meter, pressure transducer, turbidimeter, pH probe, chlorine analyzer) that generated this reading. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo for calibration tracking, maintenance history, and instrument lifecycle management.']
  scada_tag_id varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian sensor tag identifier (PI tag name) that uniquely identifies the process control point or instrument from which this reading was captured. Follows the site-specific PI tag naming convention (e.g., WTP01.FLW.INFLUENT.001). Maps directly to the PI Point name in the OSIsoft PI Historian system.']
  shift_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational shift during which this process reading was captured. Links to the workforce shift schedule for operator accountability, shift-based performance reporting, and MOR (Monthly Operating Report) compilation. Enables shift-level process performance analysis.']
  treatment_process_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., clarifier, filter, UV reactor, chlorination chamber, RO train, GAC contactor) within the facility from which this reading originates. Enables process-unit-level performance analysis.']
  alarm_state varchar [note: 'The SCADA alarm state associated with this reading at the time of capture. Reflects the process control alarm condition: normal (within operational bounds), low_alarm (below low setpoint), high_alarm (above high setpoint), low_low_alarm (critical low condition), high_high_alarm (critical high condition), sensor_fault (instrument malfunction detected). Used for operational response and incident tracking.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The data pipeline batch or job run identifier associated with the ingestion of this process reading into the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer. Used for ETL traceability, reprocessing identification, and data pipeline audit. Enables identification of all records loaded in a specific pipeline execution.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the instrument generating this reading is due for its next calibration verification. Sourced from the IBM Maximo asset management calibration schedule. Readings captured after the calibration_due_date may require additional quality review. Supports instrument reliability and regulatory compliance for monitoring equipment.']
  compression_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OSIsoft PI Historian exception and compression algorithms were applied to this data point before archiving. When True, the stored value may represent a compressed/exception-filtered point rather than a raw scan value. Important for understanding data density and potential gaps in high-frequency process data.']
  ct_required double [note: 'The minimum CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value in mg·min/L required by the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) for the applicable disinfectant, temperature, and pH conditions at this facility. Used to calculate CT compliance ratio (ct_value / ct_required) for regulatory reporting. Null for non-disinfection parameters.']
  ct_value double [note: 'The calculated CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value in mg·min/L for disinfection process readings. CT is the product of disinfectant residual concentration (mg/L) and contact time (minutes), used to demonstrate compliance with the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) inactivation requirements for Giardia and viruses. Applicable only to disinfection process stage readings; null for non-disinfection parameters.']
  dmr_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this process reading is designated for inclusion in the Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR) submitted to the EPA or state agency under the facility\'s NPDES permit. True for effluent quality readings at WWTPs that must be reported on the DMR. Enables automated DMR data extraction.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measurement associated with the measured_value field. Follows industry-standard engineering unit notation used in SCADA and PI Historian configurations. Common values include MGD (Million Gallons per Day), GPM (Gallons per Minute), PSI (Pounds per Square Inch), NTU (Nephelometric Turbidity Units), pH, mg/L (milligrams per Liter), and °C (degrees Celsius). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MGD|GPM|PSI|NTU|pH|mg/L|°C|%|mV|kW|gal|ft|ft/s|ppm|ppb|mS/cm|MPN/100mL — promote to reference product]']
  high_range_limit double [note: 'The maximum operational range limit for this process parameter as configured in the SCADA/PI Historian system. Values above this threshold may indicate sensor malfunction, process upset, or out-of-range conditions. Used in data quality validation and alarm management.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this process reading record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer from the source system (OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA). Distinct from reading_timestamp (the real-world event time). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring and ETL audit trails.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this process reading was manually entered by a licensed operator rather than automatically captured by the SCADA/PI Historian system. True for manual readings (e.g., grab sample results, field measurements, backup readings during SCADA outages). Required for data integrity auditing and regulatory compliance documentation under EPA SDWA.']
  is_regulatory_exceedance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured_value exceeds the applicable regulatory_limit_value for this process parameter. True when the reading constitutes a regulatory violation requiring notification, corrective action, and reporting under SDWA or NPDES permit conditions. Critical flag for compliance monitoring and MOR/DMR generation.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent calibration verification performed on the instrument generating this reading. Sourced from IBM Maximo calibration records. Used to assess measurement reliability and support regulatory documentation of monitoring equipment maintenance.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'A human-readable description of the physical location within the treatment facility where this measurement point is installed (e.g., \'Filter Gallery - Filter 3 Effluent\', \'Clearwell Inlet\', \'UV Reactor Train 2 Outlet\'). Supplements the tag_id with a plain-language location reference for operator use and regulatory documentation.']
  low_range_limit double [note: 'The minimum operational range limit for this process parameter as configured in the SCADA/PI Historian system. Values below this threshold may indicate sensor malfunction, process upset, or out-of-range conditions. Used in data quality validation and alarm management.']
  measured_value double [note: 'The principal numeric measurement captured at the process control point. Represents the raw or lightly-conditioned sensor reading as stored in OSIsoft PI Historian. Units are defined by the engineering_unit field. Examples: flow rate in MGD or GPM, pressure in PSI, turbidity in NTU, pH value, chlorine residual in mg/L, temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  mor_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this process reading is designated for inclusion in the Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submitted to the state primacy agency. True for readings that represent regulatory compliance monitoring points required under the facility\'s operating permit. Enables automated MOR data extraction from the silver layer.']
  parameter_type varchar [note: 'The category of process parameter being measured. Classifies the reading into a standard measurement type for cross-facility analytics and regulatory reporting. Examples: flow (volumetric flow rate), pressure (hydraulic pressure), turbidity (particle measurement in NTU), pH (acidity/alkalinity), chlorine_residual (disinfectant residual in mg/L), temperature (process temperature), chemical_feed_rate (dosing rate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flow|pressure|turbidity|pH|chlorine_residual|temperature|chemical_feed_rate|level|conductivity|dissolved_oxygen|TOC|UV_transmittance|BOD|COD|TSS|TDS — promote to reference product]']
  pi_server_name varchar [note: 'The name of the OSIsoft PI Historian server instance from which this reading was sourced. Water utilities may operate multiple PI servers across different facilities or regions. Used for data lineage, source system reconciliation, and multi-server federation queries.']
  process_stage varchar [note: 'The treatment process stage at which this reading was captured. Identifies the position within the treatment train for process control and regulatory compliance tracking. Key stages include intake (raw water source), coagulation, flocculation, sedimentation, filtration, disinfection (UV/chlorination/RO/UF/MF/GAC), finished_water (post-treatment), and distribution_entry (point of entry to distribution network). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: intake|coagulation|flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection|finished_water|distribution_entry|wastewater_influent|secondary_treatment|tertiary_treatment|wastewater_effluent — promote to reference product]']
  quality_flag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian data quality status code associated with this reading. Indicates the reliability and validity of the measured value. \'Good\' indicates a valid, reliable reading. \'Bad\' indicates a sensor fault or communication failure. \'Uncertain\' indicates the value may be unreliable. \'Substituted\' indicates a manually entered or interpolated value. \'Shutdown\' indicates the process unit was offline. \'ConfigError\' indicates a tag configuration issue.']
  raw_value double [note: 'The unprocessed, unconditioned sensor output value as received directly from the SCADA/PI Historian system before any silver-layer transformations, scaling, or quality adjustments. Preserved for audit and reconciliation against the source system. May differ from measured_value if scaling, offset correction, or substitution was applied.']
  reading_date date [note: 'The calendar date (yyyy-MM-dd) on which this process reading was recorded. Derived from reading_timestamp for partitioning, daily aggregation, and regulatory reporting purposes. Stored as a separate DATE column to support efficient date-based partitioning in the Databricks Lakehouse and daily compliance reporting.']
  reading_status varchar [note: 'The silver-layer curation lifecycle status of this process reading record. \'raw\' indicates the record has been ingested but not yet validated. \'validated\' indicates the record has passed data quality checks. \'rejected\' indicates the record failed validation rules. \'substituted\' indicates the value was replaced by an operator-approved substitute. \'archived\' indicates the record has been moved to long-term storage.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the process measurement was recorded by the SCADA/PI Historian system. This is the principal real-world event time for the observation, stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Distinct from ingestion or audit timestamps.']
  regulatory_limit_type varchar [note: 'The type of regulatory standard that the regulatory_limit_value represents. MCL (Maximum Contaminant Level) is the enforceable standard under SDWA. MCLG (Maximum Contaminant Level Goal) is the non-enforceable health goal. action_level applies to Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR). permit_limit applies to NPDES discharge permits. treatment_technique applies where no MCL is set but a treatment method is required.']
  regulatory_limit_value double [note: 'The applicable regulatory threshold (MCL, MCLG, or permit limit) for this process parameter as defined by the EPA or state primacy agency. For example, the MCL for turbidity is 1 NTU (0.3 NTU for filtered systems), and the MCL for total trihalomethanes (THMs) is 0.080 mg/L. Used for real-time compliance monitoring and MOR/DMR reporting.']
  sample_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'The configured sampling or scan interval in seconds at which this PI tag is polled or archived by the OSIsoft PI Historian system. Common intervals include 1 second (continuous process), 60 seconds (1-minute averages), 900 seconds (15-minute averages), and 3600 seconds (hourly averages). Used for time-series aggregation and data completeness assessment.']
  scada_system_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system instance that collected and transmitted this process reading. Identifies the specific PLC, RTU, or HMI node in the control network. Used for source system traceability and SCADA infrastructure management.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this process reading was ingested. Identifies the originating data source for lineage tracking and data quality assessment. Primary source is OSIsoft PI Historian (PI_Historian). Other sources include direct SCADA feeds (SCADA_Direct), manual operator entries (Manual_Entry), LIMS laboratory imports (LIMS), and AMI platform feeds (AMI).']
  source_tag_name varchar [note: 'The original PI tag name or SCADA point name as it exists in the source system (OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA). Preserved for data lineage and reconciliation purposes. May differ from the normalized tag_id if tag renaming or aliasing has occurred during silver-layer curation.']
  substitution_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the measured_value was substituted or manually overridden from the raw_value. Applicable when reading_status is \'substituted\' or quality_flag is \'Substituted\'. Required for regulatory audit trails under EPA SDWA data integrity requirements. Null when no substitution was applied.']
  treatment_process_type varchar [note: 'The specific treatment technology or process type associated with this reading. Identifies the treatment method for process-specific analytics and compliance reporting. Values include conventional (coagulation/flocculation/sedimentation), UV (Ultraviolet Disinfection), chlorination, RO (Reverse Osmosis), UF (Ultrafiltration), MF (Microfiltration), GAC (Granular Activated Carbon), ozonation, softening, and ion_exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conventional|UV|chlorination|RO|UF|MF|GAC|ozonation|softening|ion_exchange|biological|membrane_bioreactor — promote to reference product]']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this process reading record was validated and promoted to \'validated\' status in the silver layer. Null if the record has not yet been validated or was rejected. Supports data quality audit trails and SLA monitoring for data availability.']

  Note: 'Time-series operational readings from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian for treatment process control points. Each record captures a sensor tag ID, timestamp, measured value, engineering unit (MGD, GPM, PSI, NTU, pH, mg/L, mW/cm², °C), quality flag, and source system. Includes all instrumented process telemetry: flow rates, pressures, turbidity, pH, chlorine residual, chemical feed rates, UV intensity/dose, and temperature. Serves as the silver-layer curated telemetry store for treatment operations, process trending, alarm validation, and regulatory compliance verification.'
}

Table treatment.chemical_dose_event {
  chemical_dose_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each chemical dosing event record in the treatment domain. Primary key for the chemical_dose_event data product.']
  chemical_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical substance master record (e.g., chlorine, alum, polymer, lime, caustic soda, fluoride, potassium permanganate, sodium hypochlorite, GAC). Links to the chemical inventory/supply chain product catalog.']
  chemical_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.chemical_inventory. Business justification: Each chemical dose event draws from a specific on-site inventory lot. This FK enables lot traceability for regulatory compliance and quality control. The chemical_lot_number on chemical_dose_event sho']
  chemical_supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical supplier/vendor master record in SAP MM. Used for chemical cost allocation by supplier, supplier performance analytics, and procurement planning.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: Chemical dosing events during commissioning validate treatment process performance before operational acceptance. Regulatory approval requires documented chemical dose testing during startup. Links do']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed treatment plant operator who initiated or authorized the dosing event. Required for regulatory accountability under state drinking water operator certification programs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where the dosing event occurred. Links to the treatment facility master record.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Chemical dosing events have direct cost impact (event_dose_cost_usd field present). Required for chemical expense accrual, variance analysis against budget, and regulatory cost reporting. Links operat']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.mor_submission. Business justification: Chemical dose events are aggregated and reported in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). The mor_reporting_period string on chemical_dose_event should be replaced by FK to the mor_submission that includes']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed treatment plant operator who initiated or authorized the dosing event. Required for regulatory accountability under state drinking water operator certification programs.']
  process_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_alarm. Business justification: Chemical dose events may be triggered by process alarms (e.g., low chlorine residual alarm triggers automatic dosing). The dose_trigger_type field indicates whether dosing was alarm-triggered. This FK']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., coagulation basin, filter, chlorine contact chamber, RO train) where the chemical was dosed. Sourced from IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS) asset registry.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dosing pump asset used to inject the chemical, as registered in IBM Maximo CMMS. Enables pump performance tracking, maintenance scheduling, and dose accuracy auditing.']
  treatment_process_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., coagulation basin, filter, chlorine contact chamber, RO train) where the chemical was dosed. Sourced from IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS) asset registry.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chemical supplier/vendor master record in SAP MM. Used for chemical cost allocation by supplier, supplier performance analytics, and procurement planning.']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Operators collect verification samples after chemical dosing (chlorine, fluoride) to confirm target residuals achieved. Links dose event to lab confirmation sample for regulatory compliance documentat']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo work order or SAP PM maintenance order associated with this dosing event, if the dosing was performed as part of a scheduled or corrective maintenance activity.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where the dosing event occurred. Links to the treatment facility master record.']
  chemical_concentration_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The concentration of the chemical solution as dosed, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 12.5% sodium hypochlorite, 48% alum solution). Required for accurate mass-to-volume conversion and chemical inventory reconciliation.']
  chemical_mass_applied_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total mass of active chemical ingredient applied during the dosing event, expressed in kilograms. Calculated from volume and concentration for solid or concentrated liquid chemicals. Used for regulatory reporting and chemical cost accounting.']
  chemical_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the chemical dosed. Used for cost allocation by treatment function, regulatory reporting by chemical category, and chemical consumption analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disinfectant|coagulant|flocculant|pH_adjustment|fluoridation|oxidant|corrosion_inhibitor|adsorbent — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this chemical dose event record was first created in the Silver Layer data product, either via SCADA/PI Historian integration or manual entry. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  ct_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dosing event achieved the required CT value for regulatory disinfection compliance (True = compliant, False = non-compliant). Used for automated MOR/DMR compliance reporting and regulatory violation tracking.']
  ct_required_mg_min_per_l decimal(12,4) [note: 'The minimum CT value required by EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) for the applicable temperature and pH conditions to achieve required log inactivation credits. Compared against ct_value_mg_min_per_l for compliance determination.']
  ct_value_mg_min_per_l decimal(12,4) [note: 'The calculated CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value in mg·min/L, representing the product of disinfectant residual concentration and contact time. Required for Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) compliance and Giardia/virus inactivation credit calculations.']
  dbp_formation_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dosing event parameters (dose rate, TOC level, temperature, pH) present an elevated risk of Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) formation, specifically Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Supports proactive DBP compliance management.']
  dose_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the chemical dosing event concluded. Used with dose_start_timestamp to calculate dosing duration and total chemical volume applied. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  dose_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the chemical dosing event, typically sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA event log (e.g., \'CDE-2024-00123\'). Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory audit trails.']
  dose_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the chemical dosing event record. \'completed\' indicates normal termination; \'aborted\' indicates operator-interrupted dosing; \'failed\' indicates pump or system failure; \'pending_review\' indicates the record requires QA validation before regulatory submission.']
  dose_rate_mg_per_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'The chemical dose rate expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L) of water treated. This is the primary dosing metric used for regulatory compliance verification, CT calculations, and comparison against Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) limits.']
  dose_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the chemical dosing event commenced, as recorded by SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. This is the principal real-world event timestamp for regulatory CT (Contact Time) compliance calculations.']
  dose_trigger_type varchar [note: 'Indicates what initiated the dosing event: automatic SCADA/PLC control loop, manual operator action, scheduled dosing program, alarm response (e.g., low residual alarm), or regulatory requirement (e.g., CT compliance mandate).']
  dosing_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the chemical was applied: \'continuous\' (steady-state pump injection), \'batch\' (fixed volume addition), \'slug\' (rapid high-concentration dose), or \'intermittent\' (cyclic on/off dosing). Affects dose efficacy calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  dosing_pump_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the dosing pump asset used to inject the chemical, as registered in IBM Maximo CMMS. Enables pump performance tracking, maintenance scheduling, and dose accuracy auditing.']
  event_dose_cost_usd decimal(14,4) [note: 'The total chemical cost in USD for this dosing event, calculated as chemical_mass_applied_kg multiplied by unit_cost_per_kg. Used for per-event cost allocation, treatment cost-per-MGD analytics, and OPEX reporting in SAP FI/CO.']
  mcl_limit_mg_per_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'The applicable Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or Maximum Contaminant Level Goal (MCLG) for the target parameter as set by EPA or state primacy agency. Stored at event time for point-in-time regulatory compliance verification.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the licensed operator to record observations, anomalies, equipment issues, or contextual information about the dosing event (e.g., \'pump cavitation observed\', \'manual override due to analyzer failure\'). Supports operational audit trail and incident investigation.']
  post_dose_residual_mg_per_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual measured residual concentration of the target parameter (e.g., free chlorine residual, pH) after the dosing event. Critical for CT compliance verification, MCL compliance, and dose efficacy reporting in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR).']
  pre_dose_residual_mg_per_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of the target parameter (e.g., chlorine residual, pH) immediately before the dosing event commenced. Provides baseline for dose efficacy assessment and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  pump_flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'The dosing pump flow rate in Gallons per Minute (GPM) during the event, as recorded by SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian via the pump tag. Used for pump calibration verification and dose accuracy auditing.']
  pump_scada_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA tag identifier for the dosing pump instrument (e.g., \'WTP1.PUMP.CL2.FLOW.001\'). Enables direct linkage to real-time process data in PI Historian for trend analysis and audit trail verification.']
  regulatory_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dosing event is subject to regulatory reporting requirements (True = must be included in MOR, DMR, or CCR submissions). Enables automated filtering of reportable events for EPA and state primacy agency compliance reporting.']
  target_dose_rate_mg_per_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'The planned or setpoint dose rate in mg/L as configured in the SCADA/PLC control system or operator dosing schedule. Compared against actual dose_rate_mg_per_l to assess dosing accuracy and control loop performance.']
  target_parameter varchar [note: 'The water quality parameter that the dosing event is intended to control or achieve (e.g., \'free_chlorine_residual\', \'turbidity\', \'pH\', \'fluoride_level\', \'TOC\', \'PFAS\', \'BOD\', \'TSS\'). Links dosing events to water quality outcomes for efficacy analysis.']
  target_residual_mg_per_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'The desired or regulatory-required residual concentration of the target parameter after dosing. Compared against post_dose_residual_mg_per_l to determine compliance status. Derived from MCL/MCLG standards or internal treatment targets.']
  treatment_process_stage varchar [note: 'The stage in the water treatment process where the chemical was dosed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_treatment|coagulation|flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection|post_treatment|distribution — promote to reference product]']
  turbidity_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Water turbidity in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU) at the dosing point during the event. Turbidity affects disinfection efficacy and is a key regulatory parameter under the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR). Sourced from SCADA or LIMS.']
  unit_cost_per_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'The unit cost of the chemical in USD per kilogram at the time of the dosing event, sourced from SAP MM purchase order pricing. Used for chemical cost allocation per dosing event and OPEX analytics. Classified as confidential due to supplier contract pricing sensitivity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this chemical dose event record was last modified, including QA review updates, operator corrections, or regulatory annotation changes. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  volume_applied_l decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total volume of chemical solution applied during the dosing event, expressed in liters. Used for chemical inventory consumption tracking, cost allocation, and procurement planning in SAP MM.']
  water_flow_rate_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'The water flow rate in Million Gallons per Day (MGD) at the dosing point during the event, as recorded by SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Used to validate dose rate calculations and for CT compliance verification.']
  water_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'The pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of the water at the dosing point during the event. Critical for CT compliance calculations (CT requirements vary with pH for chlorine disinfection), DBP formation potential, and corrosion control under LCRR.']
  water_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Water temperature in degrees Celsius at the dosing point during the event. Critical input for CT compliance calculations (CT requirements vary with temperature) and for chemical reaction rate modeling.']

  Note: 'Records each chemical dosing event at a treatment process unit, capturing chemical type (chlorine, alum, polymer, lime, caustic soda, fluoride, potassium permanganate, sodium hypochlorite), dose rate (mg/L), volume applied, target parameter, actual measured residual, dosing pump tag, operator ID, and timestamp. Supports chemical consumption tracking, cost allocation, and regulatory dose compliance verification.'
}

Table treatment.ct_compliance_record {
  ct_compliance_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CT compliance calculation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete regulatory compliance calculation event with a clear lifecycle under the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR).']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: CT compliance demonstration during commissioning validates disinfection system design meets regulatory requirements. State approval to operate requires CT compliance documentation during startup perio']
  ct_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.ct_calculation. Business justification: CT compliance records reference specific CT calculations for regulatory audit trail. Links compliance determination to underlying calculation for MOR reporting, regulatory inspections, and disinfectio']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment plant operator responsible for recording or verifying this CT compliance calculation. Aligns with state-certified operator licensing requirements under SDWA.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where this CT compliance calculation was performed. Links to the facility master record in the asset management domain.']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submission record in which this CT compliance calculation was included and reported to the state primacy agency. Links this record to the regulatory reporting submission lifecycle.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment plant operator responsible for recording or verifying this CT compliance calculation. Aligns with state-certified operator licensing requirements under SDWA.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: CT compliance is calculated for water entering specific pressure zones. Regulatory requirement: MOR submissions must demonstrate adequate disinfection for each zone served. Different zones have differ']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment unit or disinfection contact basin within the WTP where CT was measured (e.g., chlorine contact chamber, UV reactor, ozone contactor). Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian asset hierarchy or IBM Maximo asset register.']
  treatment_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment unit or disinfection contact basin within the WTP where CT was measured (e.g., chlorine contact chamber, UV reactor, ozone contactor). Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian asset hierarchy or IBM Maximo asset register.']
  treatment_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.treatment_violation. Business justification: CT compliance failures generate treatment violations. The violation_type and violation_reported_date on ct_compliance_record should be normalized to FK to the treatment_violation record. This links CT']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where this CT compliance calculation was performed. Links to the facility master record in the asset management domain.']
  baffling_factor decimal(4,3) [note: 'The dimensionless baffling factor (T10/T) applied to the theoretical hydraulic detention time to calculate the effective T10 contact time. Ranges from 0.1 (poor baffling) to 1.0 (plug flow). Determined by tracer study or EPA baffling factor table per SWTR guidance.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to derive the CT compliance calculation. \'scada_automated\' = real-time automated calculation from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian; \'manual_grab\' = operator-collected grab sample data; \'lims_integrated\' = data sourced from LIMS (LabWare); \'pi_historian_batch\' = batch calculation from PI Historian archived data.']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when the CT compliance calculation was performed or the SCADA/PI Historian data snapshot was captured. Represents the business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes regarding this CT compliance calculation, such as unusual operating conditions, equipment anomalies, data quality flags, or explanations for deviations. Supports MOR narrative documentation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this CT compliance calculation record indicating whether the calculated CT ratio meets regulatory requirements. \'pass\' = CT ratio >= 1.0; \'fail\' = CT ratio < 1.0 triggering corrective action; \'pending_review\' = awaiting operator verification; \'exceedance_reported\' = violation reported to primacy agency; \'data_gap\' = missing data prevents calculation.']
  contact_time_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'The effective hydraulic contact time (T10 — time for 10% of tracer to pass through) in minutes for the disinfection contact basin or reactor at the measured flow rate. T10 is the regulatory-accepted contact time per EPA guidance, accounting for short-circuiting.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action taken or planned in response to a CT compliance failure, such as increasing disinfectant dosage, reducing flow rate, or repairing baffling structures. Populated when corrective_action_required = True.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action plan is required as a result of this CT compliance record (True = corrective action required due to compliance failure; False = no corrective action required). Triggers workflow in IBM Maximo or SAP PM for corrective maintenance or process adjustment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this CT compliance record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  ct_calculated decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value achieved during the measurement period, expressed in mg/L·min (disinfectant concentration multiplied by contact time). This is the primary quantitative result of the compliance calculation. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian real-time process data.']
  ct_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'The ratio of CT calculated to CT required (ct_calculated / ct_required). A value >= 1.0 indicates compliance (pass); a value < 1.0 indicates a compliance failure. Critical metric for MOR regulatory submissions and EPA primacy agency reporting under SWTR.']
  ct_required decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum CT value required by regulation to achieve the target log inactivation credit for Giardia lamblia and/or viruses, expressed in mg/L·min. Derived from EPA CT tables based on disinfectant type, temperature, and pH. Used as the denominator in the CT ratio calculation.']
  ct_table_reference varchar [note: 'The specific EPA CT table identifier used to determine the required CT value for this calculation (e.g., \'Table C-1 Free Chlorine Giardia\', \'Table C-3 Chloramines Virus\'). Provides regulatory traceability for the ct_required value and supports MOR documentation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which the raw measurement data for this CT calculation was sourced. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements. \'pi_historian\' = OSIsoft PI Historian; \'lims\' = LabWare LIMS; \'scada_manual\' = SCADA HMI manual entry; \'hmi_entry\' = Human Machine Interface direct entry; \'imported\' = external data import.']
  disinfectant_concentration decimal(8,4) [note: 'The measured residual concentration of the disinfectant at the point of CT measurement, expressed in mg/L. For free chlorine, this is the free chlorine residual; for chloramines, the total chloramine residual. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian or LIMS grab sample results.']
  disinfectant_type varchar [note: 'The type of primary disinfectant used in the treatment process for this CT calculation. Determines which EPA CT table is applied to derive the required CT value. UV (Ultraviolet Disinfection) is expressed as UV dose (mJ/cm²) rather than concentration-based CT.']
  flow_rate_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'The volumetric flow rate through the treatment unit at the time of CT calculation, expressed in MGD (Million Gallons per Day). Flow rate determines the hydraulic contact time and is used to calculate T10 for the disinfection basin. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meters.']
  log_inactivation_achieved decimal(4,2) [note: 'The actual log inactivation credit achieved based on the calculated CT value, expressed as a log10 value. Derived from EPA CT tables using disinfectant type, temperature, pH, and ct_calculated. Compared against log_inactivation_required to determine compliance status.']
  log_inactivation_required decimal(4,2) [note: 'The minimum log inactivation credit required by regulation for the target organism at this treatment facility, expressed as a log10 value (e.g., 3.0 for Giardia, 4.0 for viruses). Determined by the primacy agency based on source water quality and treatment technique requirements.']
  operator_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the certified operator verified and approved this CT compliance calculation record. Provides audit trail for regulatory defensibility. Null if operator_verified = False. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  operator_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certified water treatment plant operator has reviewed and verified the accuracy of this CT compliance calculation record (True = verified; False = pending verification). Required for regulatory defensibility of MOR submissions.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'The measured pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of the water at the time of CT calculation. pH affects disinfection efficacy, particularly for free chlorine (hypochlorous acid vs. hypochlorite ion equilibrium), and is a key input to EPA CT tables. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian or LIMS.']
  pi_tag_concentration varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the disinfectant concentration measurement used in this CT calculation (e.g., \'WTP01.CL2.RESIDUAL.MGL\'). Enables direct traceability from the compliance record back to the source PI Historian time-series data point for audit and verification.']
  pi_tag_flow varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian tag name for the flow rate measurement used in this CT calculation (e.g., \'WTP01.FLOW.INFLUENT.MGD\'). Enables direct traceability from the compliance record back to the source PI Historian time-series data point for audit and verification.']
  pwsid varchar [note: 'The EPA-assigned Public Water System Identifier (PWSID) for the water system submitting this CT compliance record. Format: 2-letter state code followed by 7-digit system number (e.g., \'CA0100001\'). Required on all regulatory submissions to EPA and state primacy agencies.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this CT compliance calculation record, formatted as CT-{YYYY}-{MM}-{NNNNNN}. Used in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and EPA primacy agency submissions to uniquely reference a specific compliance calculation event.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the regulatory reporting period to which this CT compliance record belongs. Together with reporting_period_start, defines the MOR submission window for CT compliance data to the EPA primacy agency.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the regulatory reporting period (typically monthly) to which this CT compliance record belongs. Used for aggregating CT compliance data into Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMR) submitted to the primacy agency.']
  source_water_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source water type subject to SWTR CT requirements. \'surface_water\' = direct surface water intake; \'groundwater_under_direct_influence\' = GWUDI subject to SWTR; \'purchased_surface\' = purchased water from surface water source. Determines applicable CT regulatory requirements.']
  t10_basis varchar [note: 'The method used to establish the T10 hydraulic contact time for the disinfection basin. \'tracer_study\' = validated tracer study per EPA guidance; \'baffling_factor\' = EPA baffling factor table applied to theoretical detention time; \'cfd_model\' = computational fluid dynamics model; \'conservative_estimate\' = conservative regulatory default.']
  target_organism varchar [note: 'The pathogenic organism for which the CT log inactivation credit is being calculated. \'giardia\' = Giardia lamblia (3-log inactivation required under SWTR); \'virus\' = enteric viruses (4-log inactivation); \'cryptosporidium\' = Cryptosporidium parvum (LT2ESWTR); \'combined\' = combined credit calculation.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The measured water temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of CT calculation. Temperature is a critical parameter in EPA CT tables — lower temperatures require higher CT values to achieve the same log inactivation credit. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  theoretical_detention_time_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'The theoretical hydraulic detention time (volume / flow rate) of the disinfection contact basin in minutes, before applying the baffling factor to derive T10. Used in conjunction with baffling_factor to calculate contact_time_min.']
  turbidity_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'The measured turbidity of the water entering the disinfection stage, expressed in NTU (Nephelometric Turbidity Units). High turbidity can shield pathogens from disinfectants, reducing CT effectiveness. Regulatory trigger: turbidity > 1 NTU may require additional CT credit. Sourced from SCADA/PI Historian or LIMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this CT compliance record was last modified, such as after operator review, data correction, or status change. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  uv_dose_mj_cm2 decimal(8,4) [note: 'The measured UV (Ultraviolet Disinfection) dose delivered by the UV reactor, expressed in mJ/cm² (millijoules per square centimeter). Applicable only when disinfectant_type = \'uv\'. UV dose replaces concentration-based CT for UV disinfection compliance under SWTR and LT2ESWTR. Sourced from UV reactor SCADA/PI Historian.']

  Note: 'Tracks CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) compliance calculations required under the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR). Captures CT value calculated (mg/L·min), CT required (based on Giardia/virus log inactivation targets), CT ratio (achieved/required), disinfectant type, temperature, pH, flow rate at time of calculation, compliance status (pass/fail), and reporting period. Critical for MOR regulatory submissions and EPA primacy agency reporting.'
}

Table treatment.filter_run {
  filter_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual filter run record in the water treatment process. Primary key for the filter_run data product.']
  backwash_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the backwash cycle record that was triggered at the end of this filter run. Links the filter run to its associated backwash event for end-to-end filter cycle analysis and backwash optimization.']
  backwash_sequence_backwash_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the backwash cycle record that was triggered at the end of this filter run. Links the filter run to its associated backwash event for end-to-end filter cycle analysis and backwash optimization.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: Filter performance runs during commissioning demonstrate design specifications are met before operational turnover. Substantial completion requires filter-to-waste runs and performance validation per ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment operator responsible for overseeing or initiating this filter run. Supports operator accountability and regulatory certification tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where this filter run occurred. Supports multi-facility reporting and regulatory compliance aggregation.']
  filter_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific filter unit (granular media, membrane MF/UF, or GAC filter) on which this run was executed. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Filter media (anthracite, sand, GAC) are consumable inventory items with material numbers, procurement lead times, and replacement schedules. Links filter performance tracking to material master for m']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.mor_submission. Business justification: Filter run records are aggregated and reported in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). The mor_reporting_period string on filter_run should be replaced by FK to the mor_submission that includes this run. ']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment operator responsible for overseeing or initiating this filter run. Supports operator accountability and regulatory certification tracking.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Filter runs produce water for specific pressure zones. Critical for water quality incident investigation (which zone received water from a filter with turbidity breakthrough) and hydraulic modeling. O']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific filter unit (granular media, membrane MF/UF, or GAC filter) on which this run was executed. Links to the asset management record in IBM Maximo.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where this filter run occurred. Supports multi-facility reporting and regulatory compliance aggregation.']
  backwash_trigger_reason varchar [note: 'The primary reason that terminated this filter run and initiated a backwash cycle. \'time\' indicates the run reached its maximum configured duration; \'head_loss\' indicates terminal head loss threshold was reached; \'turbidity_breakthrough\' indicates effluent turbidity exceeded the threshold; \'operator_initiated\' indicates manual operator decision; \'scheduled\' indicates a pre-programmed backwash schedule; \'emergency\' indicates an unplanned operational event.']
  coagulant_dose_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'The coagulant chemical dose applied upstream of the filter during this run, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Coagulation quality directly impacts filter performance and turbidity removal efficiency. Sourced from chemical dosing SCADA records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this filter run record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Represents the audit trail creation timestamp, distinct from the operational run_start_timestamp. Used for data lineage and ETL audit purposes.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system from which this filter run record was captured. \'scada_pi_historian\' indicates automated capture from OSIsoft PI Historian; \'manual_entry\' indicates operator-entered data; \'lims\' indicates data sourced from the Laboratory Information Management System; \'hybrid\' indicates a combination of automated and manual data.']
  days_since_last_backwash varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed since the previous backwash cycle for this filter unit, at the time this run commenced. Used to assess backwash frequency compliance and filter maintenance scheduling.']
  filter_to_waste_duration_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Duration in minutes of the filter-to-waste (ripening) phase at the start of this filter run. Null if filter_to_waste_flag is False. Used to optimize ripening time and minimize water loss while ensuring effluent quality compliance.']
  filter_to_waste_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a filter-to-waste (ripening) phase was employed at the start of this filter run, diverting initial effluent to waste rather than the distribution system. Filter-to-waste is used to prevent turbidity spikes from entering the treated water supply during the filter ripening period post-backwash.']
  filter_to_waste_volume_mg decimal(10,6) [note: 'Volume of water in Million Gallons (MG) diverted to waste during the filter-to-waste ripening phase. Contributes to Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations and water loss accounting. Null if filter_to_waste_flag is False.']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average flow rate through the filter unit during this run, expressed in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Used to calculate hydraulic loading rate and assess filter performance relative to design capacity.']
  head_loss_threshold_ft decimal(8,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable head loss in feet of water column configured for this filter unit, above which a backwash is automatically triggered. Stored per run to capture any threshold changes over time for audit and optimization purposes.']
  hydraulic_loading_rate_gpm_sqft decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hydraulic loading rate applied to the filter during this run, expressed as Gallons Per Minute per square foot of filter surface area. A key operational parameter for filter performance assessment and comparison against AWWA design standards.']
  influent_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Turbidity of the water entering the filter at the start of the run, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Provides context for filter loading conditions and is used in performance analysis and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  initial_effluent_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Turbidity of the filtered water (effluent) at the beginning of the filter run, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Establishes the baseline effluent quality at run start for performance trending.']
  initial_head_loss_ft decimal(8,4) [note: 'Differential pressure head loss across the filter media at the start of the filter run, measured in feet of water column. Establishes the baseline for head loss progression monitoring and backwash trigger evaluation.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the certified water treatment operator to record observations, anomalies, or contextual information about this filter run. Supports operational knowledge capture and regulatory audit documentation.']
  particle_count_per_ml decimal(14,2) [note: 'Average particle count in the filter effluent during this run, expressed as particles per milliliter. Particle counting provides a more sensitive measure of filter performance than turbidity alone and is used for Cryptosporidium and Giardia removal credit under the LT2ESWTR.']
  peak_effluent_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'The highest turbidity reading recorded in the filter effluent during the run, measured in NTU. Critical for regulatory compliance — the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule requires individual filter effluent turbidity not to exceed 1.0 NTU (or 0.3 NTU in 95% of measurements). Triggers turbidity breakthrough alerts.']
  ph_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of the water entering the filter during this run. pH affects coagulation efficiency, disinfection effectiveness, and filter media integrity. Sourced from inline SCADA pH analyzers.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filter run met all applicable regulatory turbidity and performance requirements under the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) and LT2ESWTR. False indicates a compliance exceedance event requiring regulatory notification and corrective action documentation.']
  run_duration_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the filter run in hours, from run_start_timestamp to run_end_timestamp. Used for filter performance benchmarking, backwash frequency analysis, and O&M optimization. Populated upon run completion.']
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the filter run was terminated (by backwash trigger, time limit, or operator action), as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system. Null if the run is still in progress.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the filter run, typically assigned by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system or the WTP operations team. Used for cross-referencing with Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and regulatory submissions.']
  run_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of filter runs for this filter unit since the last media replacement or major rehabilitation. Used to track cumulative filter usage, assess media aging, and plan media replacement schedules.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the filter run commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the filter run lifecycle. Used for run duration calculation and regulatory CT (Contact Time) compliance tracking.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the filter run. \'in_progress\' indicates active filtration; \'completed\' indicates normal termination; \'aborted\' indicates premature termination due to operational issue; \'backwash_initiated\' indicates the run ended by triggering a backwash cycle; \'filter_to_waste\' indicates the run is in the filter-to-waste phase post-backwash.']
  scada_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA tag prefix associated with this filter unit\'s instrumentation data stream. Enables direct linkage between this filter run record and the corresponding time-series data in the PI Historian for detailed process analytics and audit trail.']
  terminal_effluent_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Turbidity of the filter effluent at the end of the run (at backwash trigger or run termination), measured in NTU. Used to assess whether turbidity breakthrough was the cause of run termination and for regulatory compliance documentation.']
  terminal_head_loss_ft decimal(8,4) [note: 'Differential pressure head loss across the filter media at the end of the filter run (at backwash trigger or run termination), measured in feet of water column. Used to assess filter loading and determine if head loss was the backwash trigger. Compared against the head loss threshold to evaluate filter condition.']
  toc_effluent_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in the filter effluent during this run, measured in mg/L. Used to calculate TOC removal efficiency across the filter and assess GAC adsorption capacity. Sourced from LIMS or inline TOC analyzers.']
  toc_influent_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in the filter influent during this run, measured in mg/L. TOC is a key parameter for GAC filter performance assessment and Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) precursor removal tracking. Sourced from LIMS or inline TOC analyzers.']
  turbidity_mcl_exceedance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the peak effluent turbidity during this filter run exceeded the regulatory Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) threshold of 1.0 NTU as defined by the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule. True triggers regulatory notification and MOR documentation requirements.']
  turbidity_threshold_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'The effluent turbidity level in NTU configured as the breakthrough trigger for this filter unit. When peak_effluent_turbidity_ntu exceeds this value, a backwash may be triggered. Stored per run to capture threshold configuration at time of run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this filter run record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks data corrections, late-arriving SCADA data updates, or operator note additions. Used for data lineage and change audit trail.']
  volume_filtered_mg decimal(14,6) [note: 'Total volume of water processed through the filter unit during this run, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). A key performance indicator for filter throughput and backwash optimization. Sourced from SCADA flow meters integrated with OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  water_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average temperature of the water being filtered during this run, measured in degrees Celsius. Water temperature affects filter ripening time, head loss development, and biological activity in GAC filters. Sourced from SCADA instrumentation.']

  Note: 'Captures complete filter run lifecycle records for granular media, membrane (MF/UF), or GAC filter units, including the terminal backwash event. Records filter unit ID, run start/end timestamps, run duration (hours), volume filtered (MG), initial and terminal head loss (ft), turbidity breakthrough readings (NTU), backwash trigger reason (time, head loss, turbidity), filter-to-waste duration, backwash water volume used (gallons), air scour duration, backwash flow rate (GPM), post-backwash turbidity (NTU), and backwash waste disposal method. Supports filter performance analysis, backwash optimization, backwash water recovery tracking, NRW calculations, and regulatory turbidity compliance.'
}

Table treatment.backwash_event {
  backwash_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each filter backwash event record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: Backwash system functional testing is standard commissioning requirement for new/upgraded filtration systems. Operations acceptance requires demonstration of backwash cycle performance, air scour oper']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment operator who confirmed and supervised the backwash event. Required for regulatory accountability under state drinking water programs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) facility where the backwash event occurred. Supports multi-facility reporting and MOR regulatory submissions.']
  filter_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific filter unit (filter cell/bed) at the WTP that underwent the backwash event. Links to the filter unit asset record in IBM Maximo CMMS.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Chemical aids used in filter backwash (polymers, surfactants) are procured materials with inventory tracking requirements. Links backwash chemical consumption to material master for usage-based reorde']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.mor_submission. Business justification: Backwash events are aggregated and reported in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). The mor_reporting_period string on backwash_event should be replaced by FK to the mor_submission that includes this even']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment operator who confirmed and supervised the backwash event. Required for regulatory accountability under state drinking water programs.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.pm_schedule. Business justification: Filter backwash events can be preventive (scheduled time-based or meter-based PM) or reactive (triggered by headloss/turbidity). Operators and asset managers need to distinguish PM-scheduled backwashe']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Backwash waste and filter-to-waste volumes impact zone water balance and NRW calculations. Utilities track treatment process losses by destination zone for accurate production accounting. Essential fo']
  process_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_alarm. Business justification: Backwash events may be triggered by process alarms (e.g., high headloss alarm, turbidity breakthrough alarm). The initiation_type and trigger_reason fields indicate whether backwash was alarm-triggere']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific filter unit (filter cell/bed) at the WTP that underwent the backwash event. Links to the filter unit asset record in IBM Maximo CMMS.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo CMMS work order associated with this backwash event, enabling O&M cost tracking and maintenance history linkage.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) facility where the backwash event occurred. Supports multi-facility reporting and MOR regulatory submissions.']
  abnormal_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this backwash event was flagged as abnormal due to unexpected conditions such as media loss, turbidity exceedance, premature termination, or equipment malfunction (True = abnormal, False = normal). Triggers follow-up investigation workflow.']
  air_scour_duration_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Duration of the air scour phase of the backwash cycle in minutes. Null if air scour was not used. Supports optimization of combined air-water backwash procedures per AWWA guidelines.']
  air_scour_rate_scfm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Air flow rate applied during the air scour phase, measured in Standard Cubic Feet Per Minute (SCFM). Null if air scour was not used. Used to verify compliance with design air scour intensity specifications.']
  air_scour_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether air scour was employed as part of the backwash cycle (True) or not (False). Air scour enhances filter media cleaning efficiency and is tracked for process optimization and media longevity analysis.']
  backwash_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the backwash cycle was completed or terminated, recorded from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Used with start timestamp to calculate total backwash duration. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  backwash_flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average flow rate of backwash water through the filter media during the backwash cycle, measured in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Used to assess backwash effectiveness and compliance with design specifications.']
  backwash_flow_rate_max_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Peak flow rate recorded during the backwash cycle in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Supports detection of hydraulic surges that may damage filter media and comparison against design maximum flow rate.']
  backwash_recycle_volume_gal decimal(12,2) [note: 'Volume of backwash waste water recovered and recycled back to the plant headworks for re-treatment, measured in gallons. Null if recycle was not performed. Supports backwash water recovery tracking and NRW reduction reporting.']
  backwash_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the backwash cycle was initiated, recorded from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  backwash_waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of backwash waste water and solids. Options include: \'lagoon\' (settling lagoon), \'sewer_discharge\' (to sanitary sewer), \'recycle_to_head\' (recycled to plant headworks), \'dewatering\' (mechanical dewatering), \'land_application\'. Tracked for NPDES permit compliance and environmental reporting.']
  backwash_water_source varchar [note: 'Source of water used for the backwash cycle. Supports hydraulic capacity planning and ensures backwash water quality meets operational requirements.']
  backwash_water_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of the backwash water at the time of the backwash event, measured in degrees Celsius. Water temperature affects backwash expansion rate and media cleaning efficiency; used for seasonal performance analysis.']
  backwash_water_volume_gal decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume of water used during the backwash cycle, measured in gallons. Critical input for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations, backwash water recovery tracking, and operational efficiency reporting.']
  chemical_aid_dose_mg_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Concentration of chemical ripening aid applied to the filter during the ripening period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Null if no chemical aid was used. Supports chemical dosing optimization and cost tracking.']
  chemical_aid_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a chemical ripening aid (e.g., polymer or coagulant) was dosed to the filter during the ripening period to accelerate turbidity reduction (True) or not (False). Supports chemical dosing optimization and cost tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this backwash event record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse, sourced from the operational system of record (OSIsoft PI Historian or IBM Maximo). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  duration_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the backwash cycle in minutes, from start to end. Sourced from SCADA historian or calculated at ingestion. Supports filter media performance trending and compliance with AWWA backwash duration guidelines.']
  event_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the backwash event, used in operational logs, MOR submissions, and CMMS work order references. Format: BWE-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the backwash event. \'scheduled\' indicates a planned backwash; \'in_progress\' indicates active backwash; \'completed\' indicates successful completion with operator confirmation; \'aborted\' indicates early termination by operator; \'failed\' indicates process failure requiring investigation.']
  filter_media_type varchar [note: 'Type of filter media in the filter unit at the time of the backwash event. Supports media-specific backwash protocol compliance and performance benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sand|anthracite|dual_media|tri_media|gac|membrane — promote to reference product if additional media types are needed]']
  filter_ripening_duration_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time elapsed in minutes between the end of the backwash cycle and the point when the filter was returned to full production service, representing the filter ripening/maturation period. Supports optimization of filter-to-waste procedures and ripening aid chemical dosing.']
  filter_run_time_prior_hrs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours the filter operated in service since the previous backwash event. Key metric for filter media performance trending, backwash frequency optimization, and detection of premature filter breakthrough.']
  filter_to_waste_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filter-to-waste procedure was employed during the ripening period (True) or the filter was returned directly to service (False). Filter-to-waste diverts initial filtered water to waste to prevent turbidity spikes from entering the distribution system.']
  filter_to_waste_volume_gal decimal(12,2) [note: 'Volume of water diverted to waste during the filter ripening period, measured in gallons. Null if filter-to-waste was not used. Contributes to total Non-Revenue Water (NRW) and backwash water recovery calculations.']
  initiation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the backwash event was triggered: \'manual\' by operator command, \'automatic\' by SCADA/PLC logic based on headloss or turbidity threshold, \'scheduled\' per routine maintenance plan, or \'emergency\' due to abnormal filter performance.']
  media_expansion_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage expansion of the filter media bed during the backwash cycle. Optimal expansion (typically 20-50%) ensures effective cleaning without media loss. Supports media performance trending and backwash protocol optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator observations, anomalies, equipment issues, or other contextual information recorded during or after the backwash event. Supports root cause analysis and filter performance investigations.']
  operator_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the certified operator formally confirmed the backwash event and authorized filter return to service. Null if operator confirmation is pending. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  operator_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a certified water treatment operator formally confirmed the backwash event completion and filter return-to-service (True) or not (False). Required for regulatory accountability and MOR documentation.']
  post_backwash_headloss_ft decimal(8,3) [note: 'Differential pressure head loss across the filter media immediately after the backwash cycle completed, measured in feet of water column. Indicates backwash effectiveness; elevated post-backwash headloss may signal media fouling or mudball formation.']
  post_backwash_turbidity_ntu decimal(8,3) [note: 'Filtered water turbidity reading in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU) measured after the backwash cycle and filter ripening period. Critical compliance metric; must meet MCL turbidity limits per EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule before filter is returned to service.']
  pre_backwash_headloss_ft decimal(8,3) [note: 'Differential pressure head loss across the filter media immediately before the backwash cycle began, measured in feet of water column. Primary trigger metric for headloss-initiated backwash events and filter media fouling assessment.']
  pre_backwash_turbidity_ntu decimal(8,3) [note: 'Filtered water turbidity reading in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU) immediately before the backwash cycle. Provides baseline filter performance context and supports turbidity-triggered backwash analysis.']
  scada_event_tag varchar [note: 'OSIsoft PI Historian tag or SCADA event identifier associated with this backwash event, enabling direct traceability to the source process control system record for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  surface_wash_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surface wash (rotary or fixed nozzle) was employed as part of the backwash cycle (True) or not (False). Surface wash breaks up surface mud accumulation and is tracked for process optimization.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'Specific reason that triggered the backwash event. Supports filter performance trending and optimization of backwash frequency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: headloss_threshold|turbidity_threshold|runtime_threshold|scheduled_interval|operator_initiated|emergency — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  turbidity_compliance_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the post-backwash turbidity reading met the applicable Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) turbidity standard before the filter was returned to service (True = compliant, False = non-compliant). Supports regulatory compliance tracking for MOR submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this backwash event record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, reflecting operator corrections, late data updates, or status changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Records each filter backwash event including filter unit, backwash start/end timestamps, backwash water volume used (gallons), air scour duration, backwash flow rate (GPM), post-backwash turbidity (NTU), backwash waste disposal method, and operator confirmation.
