Supports backwash water recovery tracking, NRW calculations, and filter media performance trending.'
}

Table treatment.finished_water_production {
  finished_water_production_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each finished water production record at the Water Treatment Plant (WTP). Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete daily/shift-level production event with a clear lifecycle.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed supervisor or chief operator who approved this production record for MOR submission. Required for regulatory accountability under State Drinking Water Program operator certification requirements. Distinct from the shift operator who entered the data.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Production records during commissioning/startup track new facility performance against design capacity. Project closeout requires demonstration of design flow rate achievement. Links production data t']
  distribution_pump_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pump_station. Business justification: Finished water is pumped into distribution via specific pump stations. Essential for tracking the physical handoff between treatment and distribution, hydraulic modeling, and water quality tracing. Op']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed water treatment operator responsible for this production shift. Used for operator accountability, regulatory certification compliance, and shift-level quality ownership. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) facility where this production record was generated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE / MASTER_RESOURCE reference for TRANSACTION_HEADER role, linking production data to the specific treatment facility. Used for multi-plant production aggregation and facility-level reporting.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Daily production records drive revenue recognition and are compared against budgeted production volumes. Required for monthly financial close, rate case revenue forecasting, budget variance analysis, ']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.mor_submission. Business justification: Finished water production records are aggregated and reported in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). The mor_reporting_period string should be replaced by FK to the mor_submission that includes this prod']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Finished water production is delivered into specific pressure zones. Critical for hydraulic balance, demand forecasting, NRW analysis, and MOR reporting. Distribution operators must track which zone r']
  primary_finished_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed water treatment operator responsible for this production shift. Used for operator accountability, regulatory certification compliance, and shift-level quality ownership. Links to workforce/employee master data.']
  production_meter_installation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Finished water production records must reference the master meter installation measuring total plant output for regulatory compliance. MOR/DMR submissions require certified metered production volumes.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) facility where this production record was generated. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE / MASTER_RESOURCE reference for TRANSACTION_HEADER role, linking production data to the specific treatment facility. Used for multi-plant production aggregation and facility-level reporting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production record was approved by the supervising operator for MOR reporting (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and regulatory submission deadline tracking.']
  avg_production_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average finished water production flow rate during this production period, measured in Gallons per Minute (GPM). Derived from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meter data. Used for hydraulic loading analysis, pump performance monitoring, and comparison against design flow rates.']
  backwash_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of water used specifically for filter backwash operations during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). A sub-component of plant operations water. Tracked separately for filter performance monitoring and optimization of backwash frequency and duration.']
  chemical_dosing_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any unplanned or emergency chemical dosing event occurred during this production period (True = yes, False = no). Flags records requiring additional review in LIMS or SCADA systems. Supports operational exception reporting and chemical usage variance analysis.']
  cl2_residual_avg_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average free chlorine residual concentration in finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Must meet the minimum residual disinfectant level of 0.2 mg/L at the point of entry to the distribution system. Critical for disinfection compliance and MOR reporting.']
  cl2_residual_min_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Minimum free chlorine residual concentration recorded in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Tracked to identify potential disinfection deficiency events. Any reading below 0.2 mg/L triggers regulatory notification requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  ct_achieved_mg_min_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual disinfection Contact Time (CT) value achieved during this production period, expressed in mg·min/L (disinfectant concentration × contact time). Must meet or exceed the CT required for 3-log Giardia and 4-log virus inactivation under the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR). A critical regulatory compliance metric reported in MOR submissions.']
  ct_required_mg_min_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum required disinfection Contact Time (CT) value for this production period based on source water temperature and pH, expressed in mg·min/L. Determined from EPA CT tables for the applicable disinfectant. Stored alongside achieved CT to enable direct compliance gap analysis without recalculation.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality flag indicating the reliability of the production data in this record. \'GOOD\' indicates validated sensor/meter data; \'SUSPECT\' indicates data that may be erroneous pending investigation; \'ESTIMATED\' indicates values estimated due to meter failure or outage; \'MISSING\' indicates data gaps; \'CORRECTED\' indicates values that have been manually corrected from original readings. Aligns with OSIsoft PI Historian data quality coding conventions.']
  design_capacity_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Permitted design treatment capacity of the WTP for this production period, measured in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Captured at the record level to support capacity utilization calculations even if the plant\'s permitted capacity changes over time. Used for regulatory capacity compliance reporting.']
  disinfection_method varchar [note: 'Primary disinfection technology used during this production period. \'CHLORINATION\' uses free chlorine; \'UV\' uses Ultraviolet (UV) disinfection; \'CHLORAMINATION\' uses combined chlorine (chloramines); \'OZONATION\' uses ozone; \'COMBINED\' uses multiple methods. Determines applicable CT requirements and DBP formation potential.']
  filter_to_waste_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volume of water discharged to waste during filter ripening (filter-to-waste) operations following backwash, measured in Million Gallons (MG). A sub-component of plant operations water. Tracked for regulatory compliance with turbidity control requirements and water loss accounting.']
  finished_water_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of finished (treated) water produced by the WTP during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). This is the primary production output metric used in MOR reporting, water balance calculations, and capacity utilization analysis. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian clearwell/effluent flow meters.']
  fluoride_avg_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average fluoride concentration in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). The EPA Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for fluoride is 4.0 mg/L; the secondary standard is 2.0 mg/L. The optimal fluoridation level recommended by HHS is 0.7 mg/L. Reported in MOR and Consumer Confidence Report (CCR).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes related to this production record. Used to document unusual operating conditions, equipment issues, source water quality events, regulatory exceedances, or other contextual information relevant to MOR reporting and operational review.']
  peak_production_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum instantaneous finished water production flow rate recorded during this production period, measured in Gallons per Minute (GPM). Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Used for peak demand analysis, pump sizing validation, and hydraulic capacity planning.']
  ph_avg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period. Optimal range is typically 7.0–8.5 for corrosion control compliance under the Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR). Critical for disinfection effectiveness (CT compliance) and distribution system corrosion control.']
  plant_efficiency_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of finished water volume produced to source water volume intake, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50% efficiency). Calculated as finished_water_volume_mg / source_water_volume_mg. A key operational Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for WTP performance benchmarking and water loss identification. Stored as a raw ratio to support analytics without recalculation.']
  plant_ops_water_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of finished water consumed for internal plant operations during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Includes filter backwash water, filter-to-waste volumes, and other in-plant process uses. Used in water balance accounting and plant efficiency calculations.']
  production_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the finished water production occurred (yyyy-MM-dd). The principal real-world event date for this production record. Used as the primary time dimension for MOR reporting, water balance calculations, and daily production trend analysis. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  production_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this production record, used in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and regulatory submissions. Format: WTP-PRD-YYYY-MM-DD-SHIFT. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production record workflow. \'draft\' indicates data entry in progress; \'submitted\' indicates sent for supervisor review; \'approved\' indicates validated and accepted for MOR reporting; \'rejected\' indicates returned for correction; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a corrected record. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  regulatory_exceedance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any water quality parameter exceeded its Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or treatment technique requirement during this production period (True = exceedance occurred, False = all parameters within limits). Triggers mandatory regulatory notification and public notice requirements under SDWA.']
  scada_data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary data source for the volumetric and quality measurements in this production record. \'PI_HISTORIAN\' indicates automated ingestion from OSIsoft PI Historian; \'MANUAL_ENTRY\' indicates operator-entered data; \'SCADA_DCS\' indicates direct SCADA/DCS system feed; \'AMI\' indicates Advanced Metering Infrastructure; \'HYBRID\' indicates a combination of automated and manual sources. Supports data lineage and quality confidence scoring.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Operational shift during which this production record applies. \'DAY\' typically covers 06:00–14:00, \'EVENING\' covers 14:00–22:00, \'NIGHT\' covers 22:00–06:00, and \'FULL_DAY\' represents a consolidated 24-hour daily record. Used for shift-level production analysis and operator accountability.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the production shift ended (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to precisely bound the production measurement window for SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian data integration and shift-level volume reconciliation.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the production shift began (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to precisely bound the production measurement window for SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian data integration and shift-level volume reconciliation.']
  source_water_volume_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of raw source water drawn into the WTP during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Used as the input denominator for plant efficiency ratio calculations and water balance accounting. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian intake flow meters.']
  temperature_avg_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average temperature of finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Temperature affects disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations, chlorine decay rates, and distribution system water quality. Sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  toc_avg_mg_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Average Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration in finished water during this production period, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). TOC is a precursor to Disinfection Byproducts (DBPs) including Trihalomethanes (THMs) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Monitored for Stage 2 Disinfectants and Disinfection Byproducts Rule (DBPR) compliance.']
  treatment_process_type varchar [note: 'Primary treatment process technology employed at the WTP for this production record. \'CONVENTIONAL\' includes coagulation/flocculation/sedimentation/filtration; \'DIRECT_FILTRATION\' omits sedimentation; \'MEMBRANE\' includes Reverse Osmosis (RO), Ultrafiltration (UF), or Microfiltration (MF); \'SOFTENING\' includes lime softening; \'GROUNDWATER\' includes well-based supply with disinfection only.']
  turbidity_avg_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average turbidity of finished water measured at the WTP effluent during this production period, in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Must comply with the Surface Water Treatment Rule (SWTR) maximum of 0.3 NTU in 95% of monthly samples. Primary finished water quality summary metric for MOR reporting.']
  turbidity_max_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum turbidity value recorded in finished water at the WTP effluent during this production period, in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). The regulatory Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for turbidity is 1 NTU. Tracked separately from average to identify peak exceedance events for regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks data corrections and supervisor approvals.']
  volume_pumped_to_distribution_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of finished water pumped from the WTP clearwell into the distribution network during this production period, measured in Million Gallons (MG). Distinct from finished water produced — the difference represents storage changes. Critical for water balance and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations.']

  Note: 'Daily and shift-level finished water production records for each WTP. Captures production date, shift, total volume produced (MG), volume pumped to distribution (MG), volume used for plant operations (backwash, filter-to-waste), source water volume intake (MG), plant efficiency ratio, and finished water quality summary (turbidity, chlorine residual, pH, fluoride). Primary record for MOR production reporting and water balance calculations.'
}

Table treatment.source_water_intake {
  source_water_intake_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each source water intake event record in the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) operations system. Primary key for this entity.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Intake structures are delivered via capital projects. Required for asset capitalization, linking intake permits to construction permits, tracking intake improvements, and connecting as-built drawings ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the licensed water treatment plant operator who authorized or supervised the intake event. Required for regulatory accountability and operator certification compliance tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) that received the raw water intake. Links to the facility master record.']
  intake_meter_installation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.installation. Business justification: Source water intake events reference the meter installation at the intake point measuring raw water withdrawal volumes. Withdrawal permits require metered intake volumes for compliance tracking. Water']
  intake_pump_station_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the pump station asset through which raw water was drawn from the source. Links to the asset/facility master record managed in IBM Maximo CMMS.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the corresponding raw water quality sample record in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS - LabWare or similar). Links the intake event to laboratory analytical results for quality parameters not measured inline.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the licensed water treatment plant operator who authorized or supervised the intake event. Required for regulatory accountability and operator certification compliance tracking.']
  scada_tag_id varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA tag identifier associated with the primary flow measurement point for this intake event. Enables direct traceability from this record back to the real-time process historian data stream for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  storage_tank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.storage_tank. Business justification: In some configurations, raw water intake pumps directly to distribution storage tanks (common in smaller systems or emergency interconnections). Operators track which tank received raw water for blend']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the regulatory water withdrawal permit under which this intake event was authorized. Links to the permit master record for compliance tracking.']
  water_source_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the specific raw water source body or groundwater well from which water was withdrawn. Links to the water source master record (e.g., river, reservoir, aquifer, well).']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) that received the raw water intake. Links to the facility master record.']
  algae_count_cells_per_ml decimal(14,2) [note: 'Algae cell count in raw water at intake, measured in cells per milliliter (cells/mL). Elevated algae counts indicate potential for taste and odor issues, cyanotoxin presence, and increased treatment chemical demand. Triggers source water protection alerts and treatment process modifications.']
  conductivity_us_per_cm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of raw water at intake, measured in microsiemens per centimeter (µS/cm). Serves as a proxy indicator for Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration and overall ionic strength of the source water. Used to assess suitability for treatment processes such as Reverse Osmosis (RO).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this source water intake record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality assurance flag indicating the reliability and validation status of the intake event data. \'validated\' indicates data has passed QA/QC review; \'provisional\' indicates data is pending final validation; \'suspect\' indicates potential measurement anomaly requiring investigation; \'invalid\' indicates data has been rejected; \'estimated\' indicates values were estimated.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or method by which the intake event data was captured. \'scada_pi_historian\' indicates automated capture from OSIsoft PI Historian; \'manual_entry\' indicates operator-entered data; \'lims\' indicates data sourced from the Laboratory Information Management System; \'estimated\' indicates values were estimated due to meter failure.']
  dissolved_oxygen_mg_per_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Dissolved oxygen concentration in raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates biological health of the source water body and potential for anaerobic conditions that can release iron, manganese, and hydrogen sulfide, affecting treatment requirements.']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average flow rate of raw water intake during the event, expressed in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Captured from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian flow meter readings at the intake pump station. Used for hydraulic modeling and pump performance analysis.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the raw water intake point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for spatial analysis, GIS mapping of intake locations, and source water protection zone delineation in Esri ArcGIS.']
  gis_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the raw water intake point in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for spatial analysis, GIS mapping of intake locations, and source water protection zone delineation in Esri ArcGIS.']
  intake_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the raw water intake event concluded. Used with intake_timestamp to calculate duration and total volume withdrawn during the event.']
  intake_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the intake event, used in operational logs, regulatory submissions, and Monthly Operating Reports (MOR). Format: SWI-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  intake_method varchar [note: 'Method by which raw water was conveyed from the source to the treatment facility. \'gravity\' indicates flow by gravity through an intake channel or conduit; \'pumped\' indicates mechanical pumping; \'combined\' indicates both methods; \'emergency_bypass\' indicates use of an emergency intake route.']
  intake_screen_condition varchar [note: 'Operational condition of the intake screening equipment (bar screens, traveling screens) at the time of the intake event. Blocked or bypassed screens affect flow rates and may allow debris into the treatment process, impacting downstream equipment and water quality.']
  intake_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intake event record. \'active\' indicates intake is in progress; \'completed\' indicates the event concluded normally; \'suspended\' indicates intake was halted due to quality or operational issue; \'cancelled\' indicates the event was voided; \'pending_review\' indicates the record requires quality assurance review.']
  intake_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time at which the raw water intake event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian system at the intake pump station. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mor_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The regulatory Monthly Operating Report (MOR) reporting period (YYYY-MM) to which this intake event belongs. Used to aggregate intake volumes and quality data for submission to State Drinking Water Programs and Primacy Agencies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the plant operator regarding the intake event, including any anomalies, equipment issues, unusual source water conditions, or actions taken. Supports operational log requirements and regulatory audit trails.']
  number_of_pumps_active varchar [note: 'Count of intake pumps operating simultaneously during the intake event. Used for energy consumption analysis, pump performance benchmarking, and maintenance scheduling in IBM Maximo CMMS.']
  permit_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of this intake event relative to the applicable water withdrawal permit limits. \'compliant\' indicates volume is within permit limits; \'non_compliant\' indicates a permit exceedance requiring regulatory notification; \'approaching_limit\' indicates volume is within 90% of the permit limit; \'under_review\' indicates compliance determination is pending.']
  permit_daily_limit_mg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum daily volume of raw water authorized for withdrawal under the applicable water withdrawal permit, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). Used to assess compliance status against actual volume withdrawn.']
  ph_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of raw water at the point of intake, measured on the standard 0-14 scale. Influences coagulation efficiency, disinfection effectiveness, and corrosion control treatment. Monitored per Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR) requirements.']
  pump_station_operating_pressure_psi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Operating pressure at the intake pump station during the intake event, measured in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Monitored via SCADA to ensure pumps operate within design parameters and to detect potential main breaks or pressure anomalies.']
  raw_water_color_cpu decimal(8,2) [note: 'Apparent color of raw water at intake, measured in Color Units (CPU) using the platinum-cobalt standard. High color values indicate elevated natural organic matter (NOM) and humic substances, which are precursors to Disinfection Byproducts (DBP) including Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5).']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the raw water source type. Determines applicable treatment processes, regulatory monitoring requirements, and source water assessment protocols under SDWA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: surface_water|groundwater|purchased_water|recycled_water|brackish_water|other — promote to reference product]']
  source_water_alert_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active source water quality alert or advisory was in effect at the time of this intake event (e.g., algal bloom advisory, upstream contamination notice, drought restriction). When True, triggers enhanced monitoring protocols and treatment process review.']
  source_water_protection_zone varchar [note: 'Designation of the source water protection zone or wellhead protection area associated with this intake point, as defined in the utility\'s Source Water Assessment and Protection (SWAP) program. Used for land use management and contamination risk assessment.']
  tds_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration of raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates overall mineral content and salinity of the source water. Drives decisions on treatment technology selection including Reverse Osmosis (RO) and ion exchange.']
  temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of raw water at the point of intake, measured in degrees Celsius. Affects disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations, chemical dosing rates, biological activity, and seasonal treatment process adjustments.']
  toc_mg_per_l decimal(8,3) [note: 'Total Organic Carbon (TOC) concentration of raw water at intake, measured in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Critical parameter for predicting Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) formation potential including Trihalomethanes (THM) and Haloacetic Acids (HAA5). Drives treatment process adjustments.']
  turbidity_ntu decimal(8,3) [note: 'Turbidity of raw water at the point of intake, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). A key raw water quality parameter that drives coagulation chemical dosing decisions and treatment process optimization. Captured at intake via inline turbidimeter or LIMS sample.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this source water intake record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Tracks data corrections, QA/QC updates, and regulatory review amendments. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  upstream_event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any known upstream events or conditions that may affect raw water quality at the time of intake (e.g., upstream spill, agricultural runoff, upstream WWTP discharge, construction activity). Supports source water protection program documentation.']
  volume_withdrawn_mg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total volume of raw water withdrawn during the intake event, expressed in Million Gallons (MG). This is the principal quantitative measurement for the intake event, used for permit compliance, production planning, and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) calculations.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather condition at the time of the intake event. Weather conditions significantly influence raw water quality parameters (turbidity spikes during storm events, algae blooms during drought/heat). Used for source water quality trend analysis and treatment process correlation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|heavy_rain|storm|snow|drought|flood_event — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records raw water intake events from surface water or groundwater sources. Captures intake date/time, source water body or well ID, volume withdrawn (MG), raw water quality parameters at intake (turbidity NTU, TOC mg/L, temperature, pH, conductivity, algae count), intake pump station ID, withdrawal permit limit, and compliance status. Supports source water protection program and intake optimization.'
}

Table treatment.membrane_performance {
  membrane_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each membrane performance record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the membrane module or rack asset record in IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS). Enables lifecycle tracking and maintenance history linkage.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: Membrane system performance testing during commissioning validates integrity, flux rates, and treatment effectiveness. Regulatory approval requires membrane validation before placing into service. Lin']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where the membrane system is installed. Links to the facility master.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Membrane units are capital assets requiring depreciation calculation, maintenance cost tracking, and replacement planning. Required for asset lifecycle costing, rate base valuation, capital planning, ']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Membrane modules are high-value consumable inventory items with manufacturer part numbers, tracked in material master for procurement, warranty management, and replacement planning. Critical for membr']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.mor_submission. Business justification: Membrane performance records are reported in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) when mor_reportable flag is true. This FK links membrane performance metrics to the MOR submission for regulatory reporting']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Membrane treatment units serve specific pressure zones. Performance monitoring (flux decline, TMP increase, recovery rate) is tracked in context of zone demand and water quality requirements. Essentia']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the membrane module or rack asset record in IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS). Enables lifecycle tracking and maintenance history linkage.']
  scada_tag_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OSIsoft PI Historian tag that is the source of real-time SCADA process data for this membrane performance record.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo work order associated with a chemical cleaning event or integrity test that generated this performance record.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where the membrane system is installed. Links to the facility master.']
  backwash_frequency_per_day decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of backwash cycles performed per day on the membrane unit. Applicable to UF and MF systems. Increasing frequency indicates fouling progression and may trigger a chemical cleaning event.']
  chemical_cleaning_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of chemical cleaning events (Clean-In-Place / CIP) performed on this membrane unit since installation. Excessive cleaning frequency indicates accelerated fouling and may signal replacement need.']
  cleaning_chemical_type varchar [note: 'Type of chemical agent used in the most recent Clean-In-Place (CIP) cleaning event (e.g., citric acid, sodium hydroxide, sodium hypochlorite, EDTA, enzyme cleaner). Relevant for chemical compatibility and O&M cost tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: citric_acid|sodium_hydroxide|sodium_hypochlorite|EDTA|enzyme|sulfuric_acid|hydrochloric_acid — promote to reference product]']
  concentrate_pressure_psi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Pressure of the concentrate (reject) stream exiting the membrane system in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Used in net driving pressure calculations and energy recovery assessments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this membrane performance record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data lineage and regulatory traceability.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or method by which this performance record was captured: pi_historian (OSIsoft PI Historian automated), scada_manual (SCADA operator entry), lims (LIMS laboratory result), cmms_manual (IBM Maximo manual entry), or calculated (derived value).']
  feed_flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of feed water entering the membrane system in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA data.']
  feed_pressure_psi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Inlet feed water pressure to the membrane system in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Used to calculate net driving pressure and assess pump performance.']
  feed_tds_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration of the feed water in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Used to calculate salt rejection rate for RO systems and assess scaling potential.']
  feed_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of the feed water in degrees Celsius at the membrane inlet. Used for temperature correction of normalized flux calculations per ASTM D6908. Lower temperatures increase viscosity and reduce flux.']
  feed_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Turbidity of the feed water entering the membrane system measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Elevated feed turbidity increases fouling rate and cleaning frequency.']
  flux_decline_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage decline in normalized permeate flux from the baseline (clean membrane) value, calculated as (baseline flux - current flux) / baseline flux × 100. Triggers chemical cleaning when threshold is exceeded per O&M protocol.']
  fouling_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Silt Density Index (SDI) or Modified Fouling Index (MFI) of the feed water, indicating the fouling potential. Higher values indicate greater particulate loading and predict more frequent cleaning cycles. Applicable primarily to RO systems.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the membrane element or module was installed in service at the treatment facility. Used to calculate membrane age and remaining useful life for replacement planning.']
  integrity_test_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Direct Integrity Test (DIT) was performed on the membrane unit. Required at minimum daily under EPA LT2ESWTR for membrane systems claiming Cryptosporidium log removal credit.']
  integrity_test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the Direct Integrity Test (DIT) or pressure decay test for the membrane unit: pass, fail, or inconclusive. A fail result triggers regulatory notification and corrective action under EPA LT2ESWTR.']
  last_chemical_cleaning_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent chemical cleaning event (Clean-In-Place / CIP) performed on the membrane unit. Used to track cleaning frequency and correlate with performance recovery.']
  log_removal_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'Calculated Log Removal Value (LRV) for Cryptosporidium or other target pathogens based on the integrity test results. Must meet or exceed the treatment credit assigned by the state primacy agency under EPA LT2ESWTR.']
  membrane_age_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the membrane has been in service since installation date. Used for lifecycle management, replacement planning, and performance degradation trending.']
  membrane_configuration varchar [note: 'Physical configuration of the membrane element: spiral wound (common for RO/NF), hollow fiber (common for UF/MF), tubular, or flat sheet. Affects cleaning protocols and flux benchmarks.']
  membrane_technology_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the membrane filtration technology: Reverse Osmosis (RO), Ultrafiltration (UF), Microfiltration (MF), Nanofiltration (NF), or Electrodialysis Reversal (EDR). Drives applicable performance benchmarks and regulatory thresholds.']
  membrane_unit_tag varchar [note: 'Plant-level equipment tag identifier for the specific membrane module, rack, or train (e.g., MEM-RO-01, UF-RACK-03). Aligns with P&ID and SCADA/HMI tag naming conventions.']
  mor_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this performance record contains data that must be included in the Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submitted to the state primacy agency. Supports regulatory compliance workflow.']
  normalized_permeate_flux decimal(10,4) [note: 'Temperature-corrected normalized permeate flux in litres per square metre per hour (L/m²/h). The principal performance indicator for membrane productivity; decline over time indicates fouling or scaling. Normalized to 25°C per ASTM D6908.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world timestamp when the membrane performance measurement or test was recorded, sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA system. Distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the membrane unit: online (in production), offline (out of service), cleaning (undergoing chemical cleaning), integrity_test (under pressure decay test), standby, or decommissioned. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online|offline|cleaning|integrity_test|standby|decommissioned — promote to reference product]']
  permeate_flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of product water (permeate) exiting the membrane system in Gallons Per Minute (GPM). Used to calculate system recovery and normalized flux.']
  permeate_pressure_psi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Pressure on the permeate (product water) side of the membrane in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI). Used in TMP calculation and system hydraulic analysis.']
  permeate_tds_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) concentration of the permeate (product water) in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Used to calculate salt rejection rate and verify finished water quality compliance.']
  permeate_turbidity_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Turbidity of the permeate (filtered product water) exiting the membrane system in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Must meet MCL of 0.3 NTU (95th percentile) under EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule.']
  pressure_decay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Rate of pressure decay (psi/min or kPa/min) measured during a Direct Integrity Test (DIT) / pressure decay test. Exceeding the control limit indicates a breach in membrane integrity requiring investigation per EPA LT2ESWTR.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this performance record, formatted as MEM-{UNIT_CODE}-{YYYYMMDD}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) and regulatory submissions.']
  recovery_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of feed water converted to permeate (product water), calculated as (permeate flow / feed flow) × 100. Higher recovery reduces waste but increases scaling risk. Key operational efficiency metric.']
  replacement_recommended boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the membrane unit has been assessed as requiring replacement based on performance degradation, age, or failed integrity tests. Drives capital planning and procurement activities.']
  salt_rejection_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of dissolved salts (Total Dissolved Solids / TDS) rejected by the RO or NF membrane, calculated as (1 - permeate TDS / feed TDS) × 100. Applicable to RO and NF systems only. Decline indicates membrane integrity loss.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the individual membrane element or module. Enables traceability for warranty claims and failure analysis.']
  specific_energy_consumption_kwh_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Energy consumed per cubic metre of permeate produced (kWh/m³). Key operational efficiency and OPEX metric for membrane systems, particularly RO. Increasing values indicate pump inefficiency or fouling.']
  tmp_increase_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage increase in Transmembrane Pressure (TMP) from the baseline (clean membrane) value. A TMP increase exceeding the O&M threshold (typically 15-20%) triggers a chemical cleaning event.']
  transmembrane_pressure_psi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Transmembrane Pressure (TMP) measured in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI), representing the pressure differential across the membrane. Rising TMP at constant flux indicates fouling. Key SCADA-sourced process control parameter.']
  turbidity_removal_efficiency_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage reduction in turbidity (NTU) across the membrane system, calculated as (1 - permeate NTU / feed NTU) × 100. Applicable to UF and MF systems. Regulatory compliance metric under EPA LT2ESWTR.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this membrane performance record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data quality auditing and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Tracks performance metrics for membrane treatment systems (RO, UF, MF) at the module and rack level. Captures normalized permeate flux (L/m²/h), transmembrane pressure (TMP), salt rejection rate (% for RO), turbidity removal efficiency, membrane integrity test results (pressure decay rate), fouling index, chemical cleaning frequency, and membrane age. Supports membrane lifecycle management and replacement planning.'
}

Table treatment.uv_disinfection_event {
  uv_disinfection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each UV disinfection operational event record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: UV system validation during commissioning demonstrates dose delivery and pathogen inactivation per design. EPA/state approval to operate requires UV validation testing. Links dose monitoring events to']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment operator who acknowledged or reviewed this UV disinfection event. Populated when operator_acknowledged_flag is True. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and operator certification tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant where this UV disinfection event occurred. Supports facility-level regulatory reporting and MOR submissions.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: UV lamps are consumable inventory items with manufacturer part numbers, rated hours, and replacement schedules. Links lamp performance tracking (lamp_hours_elapsed vs lamp_rated_hours) to material mas']
  maximo_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo Asset Management work order created in response to a UV system fault, alarm, or maintenance event. Links the disinfection event to the maintenance activity for asset lifecycle tracking and O&M cost analysis.']
  mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.mor_submission. Business justification: UV disinfection events are aggregated and reported in Monthly Operating Reports (MOR) when mor_included_flag is true. The mor_reporting_period string should be replaced by FK to the mor_submission tha']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified water treatment operator who acknowledged or reviewed this UV disinfection event. Populated when operator_acknowledged_flag is True. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and operator certification tracking.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: UV disinfection is the final treatment barrier before distribution. Dose compliance must be tracked by destination pressure zone for regulatory reporting and incident response. If UV dose is inadequat']
  process_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_alarm. Business justification: UV disinfection events capture alarm conditions (alarm_code, alarm_description exist on uv_disinfection_event). These should be normalized to FK to the treatment_alarm table which is the authoritative']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific UV reactor unit that generated this disinfection event. Links to the asset registry for the UV reactor equipment.']
  reactor_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific UV reactor unit that generated this disinfection event. Links to the asset registry for the UV reactor equipment.']
  scada_tag_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA tag or data point associated with this UV reactor event record, enabling traceability back to the source process historian.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant where this UV disinfection event occurred. Supports facility-level regulatory reporting and MOR submissions.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the certified operator acknowledged this UV disinfection event record. Null if not yet acknowledged. Provides audit trail for regulatory compliance and response time tracking.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action taken by the operator in response to a non-compliant or alarm UV disinfection event (e.g., lamp replacement, sleeve cleaning, flow reduction, system bypass). Null for compliant operational events. Required for regulatory non-compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this UV disinfection event record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Represents the audit trail creation timestamp, distinct from the event_timestamp which captures the real-world measurement time.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for this UV disinfection event record as assessed during ETL ingestion from OSIsoft PI Historian: good (validated measurement), suspect (possible sensor anomaly), bad (confirmed sensor failure or out-of-range), missing (gap-filled), or interpolated (value estimated between readings). Used for data quality governance and exclusion from regulatory calculations.']
  dose_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the delivered UV dose (uv_dose_delivered_mj_cm2) met or exceeded the required UV dose (uv_dose_required_mj_cm2) for regulatory log inactivation credit. True = compliant; False = non-compliant. Core field for regulatory reporting and MOR submissions.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this UV disinfection event record: compliant (dose requirements met), non_compliant (dose requirements not met), pending_review (awaiting operator or QA review), or voided (record invalidated). Drives regulatory compliance workflow and MOR submission readiness.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the UV disinfection event observation or measurement was recorded by the SCADA/PI Historian system. This is the principal real-world event time used for regulatory log inactivation credit verification and MOR reporting.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the UV disinfection event: operational (normal periodic reading), alarm (threshold breach), fault (equipment failure), startup (reactor coming online), shutdown (reactor going offline), or maintenance (scheduled service window). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operational|alarm|fault|startup|shutdown|maintenance — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of water passing through the UV reactor at the time of the event, measured in gallons per minute (GPM). Sourced from the flow meter via SCADA/PI Historian. A key variable in UV dose calculation; higher flow rates reduce hydraulic retention time and effective dose.']
  flow_rate_mgd decimal(12,6) [note: 'Volumetric flow rate of water passing through the UV reactor expressed in million gallons per day (MGD). Derived from the GPM reading for facility-level production reporting, MOR submissions, and capacity utilization analytics.']
  lamp_hours_elapsed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative operating hours logged on the UV lamp(s) at the time of this event. Used to track lamp aging, predict end-of-life, and schedule preventive lamp replacement per manufacturer recommendations and CMMS work orders.']
  lamp_power_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Current power output of the UV lamp(s) expressed as a percentage of rated maximum power (0–100%). Controlled by the Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) or ballast controller. Used to assess lamp performance degradation and energy efficiency.']
  lamp_rated_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer-rated maximum operating hours for the UV lamp before replacement is required. Used in conjunction with lamp_hours_elapsed to calculate remaining lamp life and trigger preventive maintenance work orders in IBM Maximo.']
  lamp_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the UV lamp(s) in the reactor at the time of the event: on (operating normally), off (intentionally de-energized), fault (lamp failure or electrical fault detected), warming_up (lamp in pre-operational warm-up phase), or end_of_life (lamp hours exceeded rated life). Sourced from SCADA/PLC lamp monitoring.']
  log_inactivation_target decimal(6,2) [note: 'The required log inactivation credit (e.g., 2.0-log, 3.0-log, 4.0-log) for the target pathogen as mandated by the applicable regulatory treatment technique (e.g., LT2ESWTR Cryptosporidium bin assignment). Expressed as a decimal log value. Determines the required UV dose threshold.']
  mor_included_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this UV disinfection event record has been included in the Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submission for the applicable reporting period. True = included in MOR; False = excluded (e.g., voided, data quality issue). Supports MOR completeness verification.']
  operator_acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a certified operator has reviewed and acknowledged this UV disinfection event record, particularly for non-compliant or alarm events. True = acknowledged; False = pending acknowledgment. Required for regulatory audit trails and MOR documentation.']
  pi_historian_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian tag name or point name associated with this UV reactor event record (e.g., \'WTP1.UV.BANK1.DOSE\'). Enables direct traceability from the Silver Layer record back to the source PI Historian time-series data point for data lineage and quality validation.']
  reactor_bank_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the UV reactor bank or train within the treatment facility (e.g., Bank 1, Bank 2). Multiple reactor banks may operate in series or parallel. Used for bank-level performance comparison and redundancy management.']
  sensor_reading_mv decimal(10,4) [note: 'Raw millivolt (mV) output from the UV intensity sensor (radiometer) within the reactor, as recorded by the SCADA/PI Historian system before conversion to mW/cm². Retained for sensor calibration verification and data quality auditing.']
  sleeve_fouling_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Dimensionless correction factor (0.0–1.0) representing the degree of fouling on the UV lamp quartz sleeve. A value of 1.0 indicates a clean sleeve; lower values indicate fouling that reduces UV transmittance through the sleeve and effective dose delivery. Used in dose calculation models and sleeve cleaning scheduling.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this UV disinfection event was ingested: PI_Historian (OSIsoft PI Historian automated feed), SCADA (direct SCADA system feed), Manual_Entry (operator-entered data), or LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  target_pathogen varchar [note: 'The primary pathogen for which the UV dose and log inactivation credit is being calculated and verified: Cryptosporidium, Giardia, virus, or bacteria. Determines the applicable dose-response curve and regulatory credit table used in compliance assessment.']
  turbidity_ntu decimal(8,3) [note: 'Turbidity of the water entering the UV reactor, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). High turbidity can shield pathogens from UV exposure and reduce disinfection efficacy. Sourced from inline turbidimeter via SCADA/PI Historian.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this UV disinfection event record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse (e.g., operator acknowledgment, corrective action update, data quality flag revision). Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  uv_dose_delivered_mj_cm2 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated UV dose actually delivered to the water stream, expressed in millijoules per square centimeter (mJ/cm²). Computed by the UV reactor control system using validated dose-response models incorporating UV intensity, UVT, and flow rate. This is the primary metric for log inactivation credit verification.']
  uv_dose_required_mj_cm2 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum UV dose required to achieve the regulatory log inactivation credit for the target pathogen (e.g., Cryptosporidium, Giardia, viruses), expressed in mJ/cm². Defined by the applicable regulatory treatment technique requirement (e.g., LT2ESWTR). Used to determine dose compliance status.']
  uv_intensity_mw_cm2 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured UV irradiance at the sensor within the reactor, expressed in milliwatts per square centimeter (mW/cm²). Sourced from the UV sensor array via SCADA/PI Historian. Critical input for UV dose calculation and lamp performance monitoring.']
  uv_transmittance_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Ultraviolet transmittance of the water at 254 nm, expressed as a percentage (UVT%). Measured by an online UVT analyzer or photometer. Directly affects the effective UV dose delivered to pathogens; lower UVT reduces dose efficiency.']
  water_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Temperature of the water passing through the UV reactor at the time of the event, measured in degrees Celsius. Water temperature affects UV transmittance and lamp output efficiency. Sourced from inline temperature sensor via SCADA/PI Historian.']

  Note: 'Records UV disinfection system operational events including UV reactor ID, event timestamp, UV intensity (mW/cm²), UV transmittance (UVT%), calculated UV dose (mJ/cm²), required UV dose for log inactivation credit, flow rate through reactor (GPM/MGD), lamp status (on/off/fault), sleeve fouling factor, and dose compliance status. Supports UV dose credit verification for regulatory log inactivation requirements.'
}

Table treatment.sludge_production {
  sludge_production_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sludge production record in the treatment domain. Primary key for the sludge_production data product.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: Sludge handling system testing during commissioning validates dewatering equipment and disposal processes. Operational turnover requires demonstration of residuals handling per design specifications. ']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.dma. Business justification: Sludge disposal and biosolids hauling routes are planned by DMA for logistics optimization and cost allocation. Some utilities track treatment residuals generation in context of service area for full-']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified treatment plant operator who recorded or supervised the sludge production event. Supports regulatory compliance (operator certification requirements) and workforce accountability.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) where the sludge was generated. Links to the facility master record.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Sludge disposal has significant cost (disposal_cost_usd field present). Required for waste disposal expense tracking, environmental compliance cost reporting, budget monitoring, and GL posting of disp']
  hauler_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Biosolids hauling and disposal contractors are vendors providing services under contracts with performance requirements, insurance, and regulatory compliance. Essential for vendor performance tracking']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the associated analytical sample record in the LIMS (LabWare or equivalent). Links sludge production records to laboratory test results for total solids, volatile solids, metals, pathogens, and other quality parameters.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified treatment plant operator who recorded or supervised the sludge production event. Supports regulatory compliance (operator certification requirements) and workforce accountability.']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., clarifier, digester, filter) within the facility that generated the sludge. Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian or IBM Maximo asset hierarchy.']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the NPDES permit under which this sludge production event is reported. Required for Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR) submissions to the EPA or state primacy agency.']
  treatment_process_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., clarifier, digester, filter) within the facility that generated the sludge. Sourced from OSIsoft PI Historian or IBM Maximo asset hierarchy.']
  treatment_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.treatment_violation. Business justification: Sludge production records that fail biosolids compliance (metals_compliance, biosolids_class) generate treatment violations. This FK links sludge production records to the formal violation record for ']
  biosolids_class varchar [note: 'EPA biosolids quality classification under 40 CFR Part 503. \'Class_A\' indicates pathogen reduction to below detectable levels (suitable for unrestricted land application); \'Class_B\' indicates reduced but detectable pathogens (restricted land application with site restrictions); \'not_classified\' for non-biosolids residuals such as alum or lime sludge.']
  bod_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Biochemical Oxygen Demand of the sludge liquor expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates organic loading returned to the treatment process via sludge liquors (reject water/filtrate). Sourced from LIMS analytical results.']
  cake_solids_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Solids content of the dewatered sludge cake expressed as a percentage by weight after dewatering. Indicates dewatering efficiency; higher values reduce disposal volume and cost. Applicable when a dewatering method is applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sludge production record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which this sludge production record was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assurance, and Silver Layer provenance tracking. Values: SCADA (OSIsoft PI Historian), LIMS (LabWare), manual_entry, CMMS (IBM Maximo), ERP (SAP).']
  dewatering_method varchar [note: 'The mechanical or passive method used to reduce moisture content of the sludge prior to disposal. Options include centrifuge, belt press, filter press, drying beds, gravity thickener, or none (liquid sludge). Drives cake solids content and disposal cost.']
  disposal_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for sludge disposal or beneficial use for this production event, expressed in US dollars. Includes hauling, tipping fees, and land application costs. Used for Operations and Maintenance (O&M) cost tracking, OPEX reporting, and Capital Improvement Program (CIP) planning.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the sludge was transported to and accepted at the disposal or beneficial use site. Used for manifest reconciliation and regulatory reporting period alignment.']
  disposal_manifest_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the waste disposal manifest or shipping document associated with the sludge disposal event. Required for chain-of-custody tracking and regulatory compliance under state solid waste and biosolids regulations.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The final disposal or beneficial use pathway for the sludge or biosolids. Determines applicable regulatory requirements under EPA 40 CFR Part 503 and state biosolids programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: landfill|land_application|incineration|composting|beneficial_reuse|co_disposal|surface_disposal — promote to reference product]']
  disposal_site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the landfill, land application site, incineration facility, or composting facility receiving the sludge. Used for disposal tracking, permit compliance, and annual biosolids reporting.']
  dry_weight_tons decimal(12,4) [note: 'Dry solids weight of the sludge expressed in dry tons (US short tons). Calculated from volume and total solids concentration; used for biosolids land application permitting, disposal cost tracking, and EPA 40 CFR Part 503 annual reporting.']
  hauler_permit_number varchar [note: 'State-issued waste transporter permit number for the sludge hauler. Required for regulatory compliance verification and manifest documentation under state solid waste and biosolids regulations.']
  is_biosolids_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sludge production event must be included in the EPA annual biosolids report (40 CFR Part 503) or state biosolids program report. True = reportable; False = exempt (e.g., alum sludge disposed to landfill under solid waste rules).']
  maximo_work_order_number varchar [note: 'Work order number from IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS) associated with the dewatering or sludge handling maintenance activity. Supports Operations and Maintenance (O&M) cost tracking and asset performance management.']
  metals_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the biosolids meet EPA 40 CFR Part 503 Table 1 ceiling concentration limits for regulated metals (arsenic, cadmium, copper, lead, mercury, molybdenum, nickel, selenium, zinc). True = all metals within ceiling limits; False = one or more exceedances detected. Sourced from LIMS metals analysis.']
  pathogen_reduction_method varchar [note: 'The process used to reduce pathogen levels in biosolids to meet EPA 40 CFR Part 503 Class A or Class B requirements. Determines land application site restrictions and public access limitations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: anaerobic_digestion|aerobic_digestion|composting|heat_drying|lime_stabilization|alkaline_stabilization|none — promote to reference product]']
  ph_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'pH measurement of the sludge at time of production or dewatering. Relevant for lime-stabilized biosolids (pH ≥ 12 required for Class A), corrosion control, and land application agronomic suitability. Sourced from LIMS.']
  production_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which sludge was generated or withdrawn from the treatment process. Principal business event date used for Monthly Operating Report (MOR) and DMR period aggregation.']
  production_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this sludge production event, formatted as SLG-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in residuals management logs, disposal manifests, and regulatory submissions.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sludge production record. \'draft\' indicates data entry in progress; \'confirmed\' indicates volumes and quality data have been validated; \'disposed\' indicates final disposal has been completed and manifested; \'cancelled\' indicates the record was voided.']
  production_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when sludge generation or withdrawal was recorded, sourced from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian. Supports process analytics and shift-level reporting.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes regarding the sludge production event, including process anomalies, equipment issues, sampling deviations, or regulatory observations. Supports MOR narrative sections.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The calendar month and year (YYYY-MM) to which this sludge production record is attributed for Monthly Operating Report (MOR) and Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR) regulatory submissions. Ensures correct period alignment for compliance reporting.']
  scada_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference tag or event identifier from the OSIsoft PI Historian SCADA system corresponding to the sludge withdrawal or dewatering operation. Enables traceability between process control data and residuals management records.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Shift during which the sludge production event was recorded (day, evening, or night). Supports shift-level operational reporting, staffing analysis, and process performance benchmarking.']
  sludge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sludge by its chemical or biological origin. \'alum_sludge\' results from coagulation with aluminum sulfate; \'lime_sludge\' from lime softening; \'biological_sludge\' from activated sludge or biofilm processes at WWTPs; \'filter_backwash_solids\' from filter media cleaning; \'mixed_sludge\' from combined streams; \'digested_sludge\' from anaerobic or aerobic digestion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: alum_sludge|lime_sludge|biological_sludge|filter_backwash_solids|mixed_sludge|digested_sludge — promote to reference product]']
  total_solids_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Concentration of total solids in the sludge expressed as a percentage by weight (% TS). Key parameter for dewatering performance assessment and dry weight calculation. Sourced from LIMS analytical results.']
  treatment_process varchar [note: 'The specific water or wastewater treatment process that generated the sludge. Supports process-level residuals tracking and optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coagulation|lime_softening|filtration|biological_treatment|anaerobic_digestion|aerobic_digestion|membrane_filtration|chemical_precipitation — promote to reference product]']
  tss_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total Suspended Solids concentration in the sludge or associated liquor expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Key parameter for NPDES permit compliance and process load calculations. Sourced from LIMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sludge production record was last modified. Supports change tracking and data quality auditing in the Silver Layer.']
  vector_attraction_reduction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sludge or biosolids meets vector attraction reduction (VAR) requirements under EPA 40 CFR Part 503.33. True = VAR requirements met; False = not met or not applicable. Required for land application permit compliance.']
  volatile_solids_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of volatile (organic) solids in the sludge relative to total solids. Indicates organic content and biological stability; used to assess vector attraction reduction compliance under EPA 40 CFR Part 503.']
  volume_gallons decimal(18,4) [note: 'Volume of liquid sludge generated during the production event, measured in gallons. Primary quantitative measure for residuals management planning and NPDES permit compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks sludge and residuals generation from water and wastewater treatment processes. Captures sludge type (alum sludge, lime sludge, biological sludge, filter backwash solids), volume generated (gallons/dry tons), solids concentration (% TS), dewatering method (centrifuge, belt press, drying beds), cake solids content, disposal method (landfill, land application, incineration), and disposal manifest reference. Supports residuals management compliance and NPDES permit reporting.'
}

Table treatment.chemical_inventory {
  chemical_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each chemical inventory record in the water treatment facility. Primary key for the chemical_inventory data product.']
  chemical_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.chemical. Business justification: Chemical inventory should reference the chemical master table for authoritative chemical properties (NSF certification, regulatory status, dose limits, storage requirements). The chemical_inventory ta']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water treatment or wastewater treatment plant (WTP/WWTP) where this chemical inventory record is maintained. Links to the facility master record.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Chemical inventory is a balance sheet asset (unit_cost and quantity_on_hand present). Required for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, financial statement preparation, and GL posting to inventory a']
  storage_asset_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Chemical inventory management requires precise physical location tracking within facilities for safety compliance (EPCRA Tier II reporting), emergency response planning, and operational logistics. Sto']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.vendor']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage tank, container, or designated storage area within the facility where the chemical is stored. Used for precise physical location tracking and OSHA Tier II reporting.']
  average_daily_usage decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average quantity of the chemical consumed per day at the facility, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Calculated from historical dosing records and used to estimate days of supply remaining and optimize reorder frequency.']
  avg_quantity_in_year decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average daily quantity of the chemical present at the facility during the Tier II reporting year, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Required field for OSHA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II annual report submission.']
  container_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or tag number assigned to the specific storage tank or container holding this chemical (e.g., T-101, DRUM-045). Used for physical asset tracking, maintenance records in IBM Maximo, and OSHA Tier II site maps.']
  days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of days the current quantity on hand will last based on average daily usage. Provides an operational indicator for procurement urgency and treatment process continuity risk management.']
  dosing_point varchar [note: 'Specific treatment process stage or location where this chemical is applied (e.g., pre-chlorination, coagulation basin, filtration, post-disinfection, pH correction, fluoride addition). Supports process mapping and SCADA integration.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Manufacturer-specified expiration or shelf-life date for the chemical lot. Chemicals used beyond expiration may degrade in efficacy, impacting treatment performance and regulatory compliance. Triggers disposal or replacement workflows.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous materials classification code for the chemical (e.g., Class 8 Corrosive, Class 2.3 Toxic Gas, Class 5.1 Oxidizer). Required for transportation compliance, emergency response planning, and OSHA Tier II reporting.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the chemical inventory record indicating whether the chemical is actively in use, on order, depleted, quarantined due to quality concerns, expired, or discontinued from use at the facility.']
  is_epcra_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical meets the threshold quantity for mandatory reporting under EPCRA Section 312 (Tier II) to state and local emergency planning committees. True if the chemical is an Extremely Hazardous Substance (EHS) or meets reporting thresholds.']
  is_rmp_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical is a regulated substance under EPA Risk Management Program (RMP) rules (40 CFR Part 68). True if the chemical is listed and stored above the threshold quantity, requiring an RMP plan submission.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent chemical delivery received at the facility. Used to track supply chain cadence, validate inventory levels, and support procurement planning.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or verification performed at the storage location. Required for inventory accuracy audits and OSHA Tier II annual reporting reconciliation.']
  last_physical_count_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Used to reconcile system quantity against physical stock and identify discrepancies.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier-assigned lot or batch number for the chemical delivery. Enables traceability for quality incidents, product recalls, and treatment process investigations.']
  max_quantity_in_year decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum quantity of the chemical present at the facility at any point during the Tier II reporting year, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Required field for OSHA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II annual report submission.']
  max_storage_capacity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable quantity of the chemical that can be stored at the facility location, expressed in the designated unit of measure. Governed by OSHA PSM thresholds, EPA RMP requirements, and local fire codes. Used for OSHA Tier II reporting.']
  maximo_item_number varchar [note: 'Item number assigned to this chemical in IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS) storeroom inventory. Enables cross-referencing between the lakehouse chemical inventory and the operational CMMS for work order material consumption tracking.']
  nfpa_fire_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 flammability hazard rating for the chemical on a scale of 0 (will not burn) to 4 (extremely flammable). Used for fire safety planning, storage segregation requirements, and emergency response.']
  nfpa_health_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 health hazard rating for the chemical on a scale of 0 (minimal hazard) to 4 (deadly). Used for emergency response planning, worker safety training, and facility hazard communication.']
  nfpa_reactivity_rating varchar [note: 'NFPA 704 instability/reactivity hazard rating for the chemical on a scale of 0 (stable) to 4 (may detonate). Critical for chemical storage segregation, incompatibility management, and emergency response planning.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP purchase order number associated with the most recent or active procurement of this chemical. Supports procurement traceability and accounts payable reconciliation.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current physical quantity of the chemical available in storage at the facility, expressed in the designated unit of measure (lbs or gallons). Updated after each delivery receipt and physical count. Critical for treatment process continuity and procurement planning.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this chemical inventory record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this chemical inventory record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold at which a replenishment purchase order should be triggered to prevent stockout and ensure uninterrupted treatment operations. Expressed in the same unit of measure as quantity_on_hand.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Buffer quantity of chemical maintained above the reorder point to protect against supply chain disruptions, delivery delays, or unexpected demand spikes. Expressed in the same unit of measure as quantity_on_hand.']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'SAP ERP material master number assigned to this chemical in the MM module. Enables traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and SAP procurement, goods receipt, and inventory management transactions.']
