  sds_reference_number varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this chemical, as required by OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom). Links to the SDS document management system for emergency response and worker safety.']
  sds_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most current revision of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) on file for this chemical. OSHA requires SDS documents to be current; outdated SDS may indicate a compliance gap.']
  secondary_containment_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location for this chemical has secondary containment (e.g., berm, dike, containment basin) to prevent spills from reaching the environment. Required for SPCC plan compliance and OSHA PSM.']
  storage_container_type varchar [note: 'Type of storage vessel or container used to hold the chemical at the facility (e.g., bulk tank, day tank, drum, cylinder, tote, bag). Relevant for spill response planning, OSHA Tier II, and maintenance scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk_tank|day_tank|drum|cylinder|tote|bag|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in degrees Celsius, as specified in the SDS. Exceeding this threshold may cause chemical degradation, hazardous reactions, or loss of treatment efficacy.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature for the chemical in degrees Celsius, as specified in the SDS. Ensures chemical integrity and prevents hazardous reactions due to improper storage conditions.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned part or catalog number for this chemical product as used in purchase orders and delivery documentation. Facilitates accurate procurement and receiving.']
  tier2_reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this chemical inventory record is applicable for OSHA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II annual hazardous chemical inventory reporting. Supports annual regulatory submission to state and local emergency planning committees.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of measure for the chemical as established in the most recent purchase order or contract. Used for chemical cost tracking, OPEX budgeting, and treatment cost-per-gallon calculations. Expressed in USD.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used to quantify the chemical inventory (e.g., lbs for dry chemicals, gallons for liquid chemicals). Must be consistent with procurement orders and dosing calculations.']

  Note: 'Tracks on-site chemical inventory levels at each treatment facility. Captures chemical name, CAS number, storage tank or container ID, current quantity on hand (lbs/gallons), unit of measure, reorder point, maximum storage capacity, last delivery date, last physical count date, and safety data sheet (SDS) reference. Supports chemical procurement planning, OSHA Tier II reporting, and treatment process continuity.'
}

Table treatment.process_control_setpoint {
  process_control_setpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each process control setpoint record in the treatment domain. Primary key for the process_control_setpoint data product.']
  commissioning_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.commissioning_activity. Business justification: Control setpoints are established and validated during commissioning. Operational turnover requires setpoint validation testing and operator training on control philosophy. Links setpoint configuratio']
  contaminant_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.contaminant_limit. Business justification: Operators set process control setpoints (chlorine residual targets, turbidity alarm thresholds) based on regulatory contaminant limits. Links operational setpoint to regulatory driver for compliance-d']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified treatment plant operator who approved or last modified this setpoint. Required for regulatory traceability under state drinking water operator certification programs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (WTP or WWTP) where this setpoint is configured and enforced. Links the setpoint to its operational site.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified treatment plant operator who approved or last modified this setpoint. Required for regulatory traceability under state drinking water operator certification programs.']
  pi_point_id varchar [note: 'The unique PI Point identifier in the OSIsoft PI Historian system corresponding to this setpoint\'s SCADA tag. Used for direct data retrieval and synchronization between the Silver Layer setpoint register and the PI Historian real-time data store.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.pm_schedule. Business justification: Setpoint adjustments frequently follow preventive maintenance activities (instrument calibration, control loop tuning, sensor replacement). Water treatment operators need to track which PM events trig']
  previous_setpoint_process_control_setpoint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding version of this setpoint record (self-referential). Enables reconstruction of the full setpoint change history chain for regulatory audit and process optimization analysis.']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., filter unit, clarifier, chlorination contact chamber, RO train, UV reactor) to which this setpoint applies.']
  scada_tag_id varchar [note: 'The unique PI Point identifier in the OSIsoft PI Historian system corresponding to this setpoint\'s SCADA tag. Used for direct data retrieval and synchronization between the Silver Layer setpoint register and the PI Historian real-time data store.']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.treatment_permit. Business justification: Process control setpoints are governed by regulatory permit limits (e.g., turbidity limit, chlorine residual limit). The permit_number string on process_control_setpoint should be replaced by FK to th']
  treatment_process_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., filter unit, clarifier, chlorination contact chamber, RO train, UV reactor) to which this setpoint applies.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo work order that authorized or triggered this setpoint change (e.g., a preventive maintenance work order for filter media replacement that requires a setpoint adjustment). Null if the setpoint change was not associated with a maintenance work order.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (WTP or WWTP) where this setpoint is configured and enforced. Links the setpoint to its operational site.']
  alarm_delay_seconds varchar [note: 'The time delay in seconds before an alarm is activated after the process variable crosses an alarm limit. Prevents nuisance alarms from transient spikes. A value of 0 means immediate alarm activation.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'The priority classification of alarms generated when this setpoint\'s limits are breached. \'critical\' alarms require immediate operator response and may trigger automatic shutdown; \'high\' requires prompt response; \'medium\' and \'low\' are advisory. Aligns with ISA-18.2 alarm management philosophy.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the setpoint was formally approved by the certified operator (referenced by operator_id). Required for regulatory audit trails demonstrating that setpoint changes were authorized by a qualified operator.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business or operational reason for creating or modifying this setpoint record (e.g., \'Seasonal chloramine conversion\', \'Post-LCRR compliance adjustment\', \'Filter media replacement\', \'Regulatory agency directive\'). Required for change management audit trails.']
  control_mode varchar [note: 'The control mode under which this setpoint operates in the SCADA/PLC system. \'automatic\' means the control loop maintains the setpoint autonomously; \'manual\' means operator-directed; \'cascade\' means the output of one controller drives the setpoint of another; \'ratio\' and \'feedforward\' are advanced control strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this setpoint record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the setpoint lifecycle.']
  deadband_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The deadband or hysteresis value around the setpoint within which no corrective control action is taken. Prevents excessive actuator cycling (e.g., chemical dosing pump hunting). Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this setpoint value became or becomes operationally effective in the control system. Used for temporal tracking of setpoint changes and regulatory audit trails. Aligns with MOR reporting periods.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The engineering unit associated with the setpoint value and alarm limits (e.g., mg/L for chlorine residual, NTU for turbidity, pH for potential of hydrogen, GPM for flow rate, PSI for pressure). Critical for correct interpretation of all numeric fields on this record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg/L|NTU|pH|GPM|MGD|PSI|°C|°F|%|hours|minutes|mg/L/min|mV|ft|scfm — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this setpoint version expires or is scheduled to be superseded. Null for open-ended setpoints with no planned expiry. Supports scheduled setpoint reviews and seasonal adjustments (e.g., summer/winter chloramine dosing targets).']
  high_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The high alarm threshold for the process variable. When the measured value exceeds this limit, a high alarm is triggered in the SCADA/HMI system, alerting operators to take corrective action. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  high_trip_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The high-high (HH) trip or shutdown limit for the process variable. Exceeding this value triggers an automatic safety interlock or process shutdown (e.g., chlorine over-feed trip, filter bypass). More severe than the high alarm limit. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  is_ct_compliance_parameter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this setpoint is a CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) compliance parameter as required under the EPA Surface Water Treatment Rule. CT compliance parameters (e.g., chlorine residual, contact time) are subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny and must be tracked in MOR submissions.']
  is_mcl_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this setpoint is directly associated with an EPA or state MCL parameter (e.g., chlorine residual, turbidity, pH for corrosion control under LCRR). MCL-related setpoints require additional documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this setpoint record was most recently updated. Tracks the most recent change event for audit and change management purposes.']
  low_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The low alarm threshold for the process variable. When the measured value falls below this limit, a low alarm is triggered in the SCADA/HMI system. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  low_trip_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The low-low (LL) trip or shutdown limit for the process variable. Falling below this value triggers an automatic safety interlock or process shutdown (e.g., chlorine under-feed trip, pump cavitation protection). More severe than the low alarm limit. Expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational context, special conditions, or instructions associated with this setpoint (e.g., \'Applies only during peak demand hours\', \'Coordinate with distribution operations before adjustment\', \'Seasonal adjustment per SWTR CT table\'). Supplements the change_reason field.']
  operator_certification_grade varchar [note: 'The certification grade level of the operator who approved this setpoint (e.g., Grade I through Grade IV per state drinking water operator certification programs). Ensures the approving operator holds the appropriate grade for the facility classification.']
  operator_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued operator certification license number of the operator who approved this setpoint. Stored directly on the setpoint record for regulatory traceability without requiring a join, as required by MOR and DMR submissions.']
  parameter_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the process variable being controlled (e.g., water_quality for chlorine/pH/turbidity, flow_rate for GPM targets, pressure for PSI limits, chemical_dose for mg/L targets, equipment_runtime for filter run time). Drives analytics segmentation and alarm management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: water_quality|flow_rate|pressure|chemical_dose|equipment_runtime|level|temperature — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The treatment process area or stage to which this setpoint belongs. Classifies the setpoint within the treatment train for process control, analytics, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coagulation_flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection|chemical_dosing|membrane_treatment|residuals_management|distribution_pumping|wastewater_primary|wastewater_secondary|wastewater_tertiary — promote to reference product]']
  ramp_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum rate at which the setpoint value may be changed per unit time (expressed in engineering_unit per minute). Prevents sudden process upsets during setpoint transitions (e.g., gradual chlorine dose ramp-up after a plant restart). Null if no ramp rate constraint applies.']
  ramp_rate_unit varchar [note: 'The time unit denominator for the ramp_rate field (e.g., per_minute, per_hour). Clarifies the rate of change constraint for the setpoint transition.']
  regulatory_mcl_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The applicable EPA or state Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or permit limit for this parameter, expressed in the same engineering unit as setpoint_value. Used to verify that the operational setpoint provides adequate safety margin above the regulatory threshold. Null if no MCL applies to this parameter.']
  scada_tag varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian or SCADA/PLC tag name that uniquely identifies the process variable associated with this setpoint (e.g., WTP01.CL2.RESIDUAL.SP, WTP01.FILTER1.TURBIDITY.HH). Used for real-time integration between the setpoint register and the SCADA/PI Historian system.']
  setpoint_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the setpoint parameter type within the facility\'s process control register (e.g., CL2_RESIDUAL_SP, PH_TARGET_SP, TURB_ALARM_HI). Used for cross-system integration and MOR regulatory submissions.']
  setpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the process control setpoint parameter (e.g., \'Chlorine Residual Target\', \'pH Target\', \'Turbidity Alarm Threshold\', \'Filter Run Time Limit\', \'Chemical Dose Rate\'). Serves as the primary identity label for the setpoint in operator interfaces and regulatory reports.']
  setpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the setpoint record. \'active\' indicates the setpoint is currently enforced in the control system. \'pending_approval\' indicates it has been proposed but not yet approved by a certified operator. \'superseded\' indicates it has been replaced by a newer version. \'under_review\' indicates it is being evaluated for change.']
  setpoint_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The target or nominal value for the process control parameter. This is the principal quantitative fact — the value the control system actively maintains (e.g., 1.5 for chlorine residual in mg/L, 7.2 for pH, 0.1 for turbidity in NTU). Corresponds to the SP (setpoint) field in OSIsoft PI Historian.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this setpoint was originated or last synchronized (e.g., OSIsoft_PI for PI Historian-sourced setpoints, SCADA_PLC for direct PLC configuration, Maximo for maintenance-driven setpoints, Manual_Entry for operator-entered values).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this setpoint record, incremented each time the setpoint value or limits are modified. Supports full version history and audit trail for regulatory compliance. Version 1 is the initial approved setpoint.']

  Note: 'Master configuration of process control setpoints for treatment process units. Captures setpoint parameter name (chlorine residual target, pH target, turbidity alarm threshold, filter run time limit, chemical dose rate), setpoint value, engineering unit, high/low alarm limits, high/high and low/low trip limits, effective date, approved-by operator, and associated SCADA tag. Provides the authoritative setpoint register for process control and regulatory compliance.'
}

Table treatment.treatment_permit {
  treatment_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the treatment operating permit record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the treatment_permit data product.']
  discharge_point_id varchar [note: 'Regulatory identifier for the permitted discharge point or outfall as designated in the NPDES permit (e.g., \'Outfall 001\'). Used in Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs) and effluent sampling records. May be null for non-discharge permits such as drinking water operating permits.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (Water Treatment Plant or Wastewater Treatment Plant) to which this permit is issued. Links the permit to its regulated facility.']
  regulatory_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory agency that issued this permit (e.g., EPA Region, State Primacy Agency, State Environmental Department). Used for compliance tracking and correspondence routing.']
  biosolids_permit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit includes authorization for biosolids or residuals management (land application, incineration, or landfill disposal). Relevant for WWTPs subject to EPA 40 CFR Part 503 biosolids regulations.']
  bod_limit_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Permitted maximum concentration of Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) in treated effluent, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). A primary indicator of organic pollution load in wastewater discharge. Used for DMR compliance evaluation.']
  chlorine_residual_limit_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum or maximum permitted chlorine residual concentration in finished water or treated effluent, expressed in mg/L. For drinking water, a minimum residual is required at the point of entry to the distribution system. For NPDES discharge, a maximum may be set to protect aquatic life.']
  compliance_schedule_end_date date [note: 'The final date by which the facility must achieve full compliance with all permit limits per the regulatory-approved compliance schedule. Null if no compliance schedule is in effect. Used to track CIP project milestones and regulatory commitments.']
  compliance_schedule_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit includes a formal compliance schedule (a regulatory-approved timeline for achieving permit limits that the facility cannot currently meet). True triggers tracking of interim milestones and final compliance dates in the compliance management system.']
  ct_requirement_mg_min_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum required CT (Contact Time) value for disinfection as specified in the permit, expressed in mg·min/L (product of disinfectant concentration and contact time). CT compliance is a core Surface Water Treatment Rule requirement for Giardia and virus inactivation credit.']
  dmr_submission_method varchar [note: 'The required or preferred method for submitting Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMRs) or Monthly Operating Reports (MORs) to the regulatory agency. NetDMR is the EPA\'s electronic DMR system; eDMR refers to state-specific electronic systems; paper and state_portal are alternatives.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Document management system reference, file path, or URL pointing to the official permit document, fact sheet, and associated attachments stored in the enterprise document repository. Enables direct access to the authoritative permit text for compliance review.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit conditions become legally binding and enforceable. May differ from the issue date if a grace period or administrative continuance applies.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit term expires. Triggers renewal workflow and compliance calendar alerts. Under administrative continuance provisions, operations may continue under the expired permit while renewal is pending.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the treatment facility covered by this permit. Water Treatment Plant (WTP) produces finished drinking water; Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) or Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) treats and discharges wastewater; pump_station may hold ancillary permits; combined covers co-located facilities.']
  haa5_mcl_ug_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for Haloacetic Acids (HAA5) as specified in the permit or applicable regulatory standard, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). The EPA MCL for HAA5 is 60 µg/L. A key Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) compliance parameter for drinking water permits.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory agency formally issued or reissued the permit. Marks the start of the current permit term and is used to calculate permit age and renewal lead times.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory compliance inspection conducted by the issuing agency or primacy agency at the permitted facility. Used to track inspection frequency, prepare for upcoming inspections, and assess compliance standing.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of effluent or finished water quality monitoring as specified in the permit. Drives the sampling schedule in the LIMS and the reporting calendar for DMR/MOR submissions. Represents the most frequent monitoring requirement across all parameters.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Anticipated or scheduled date of the next regulatory compliance inspection. Supports proactive compliance preparation and internal audit scheduling. May be estimated based on inspection frequency requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, regulatory correspondence summaries, internal compliance observations, or context not captured in structured fields. Used by compliance staff to document permit history, agency communications, and action items.']
  npdes_major_minor_class varchar [note: 'EPA classification of the NPDES permit as major or minor based on facility size, discharge volume, and potential environmental impact. Major facilities are subject to more stringent oversight, mandatory DMR reporting, and EPA inspection priorities. Applicable only to NPDES permit types.']
  permit_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the permit is an individual permit (facility-specific terms and conditions), a general permit (covers a category of similar dischargers), or an emergency permit (temporary authorization). Determines the regulatory review process and compliance obligations.']
  permit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the permit as assigned by the issuing agency or used internally (e.g., \'City Water Treatment Plant NPDES Discharge Permit\', \'Regional WTP Drinking Water Operating Permit\'). Supports human-readable identification in compliance registers and reports.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned permit number as issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., NPDES permit number, state drinking water operating permit number). This is the official identifier used in all regulatory correspondence, DMRs, MORs, and CCRs.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit. Active indicates the permit is in force; pending_renewal indicates the renewal application has been submitted and the permit is administratively continued; expired indicates the permit term has lapsed without renewal; suspended or revoked indicates regulatory action has been taken.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit by regulatory program. NPDES governs wastewater discharge under the Clean Water Act; drinking_water_operating governs WTP operations under SDWA; air_emission covers process air releases; residuals_management covers biosolids/sludge disposal; industrial_pretreatment covers IUP programs; stormwater covers MS4 or industrial stormwater. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NPDES|drinking_water_operating|air_emission|residuals_management|industrial_pretreatment|stormwater|other — promote to reference product]']
  permitted_avg_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Monthly average flow rate authorized under this permit in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Distinct from the peak daily limit; used for long-term capacity planning and average-basis compliance evaluation in DMR submissions.']
  permitted_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum daily flow rate authorized under this permit, expressed in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). This is the principal quantitative limit governing facility capacity and is used for capacity planning, compliance monitoring, and MOR/DMR reporting.']
  pfas_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit requires monitoring for Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) compounds. Reflects EPA\'s PFAS Action Plan and emerging state-level PFAS MCLs. Drives LIMS sampling plan configuration and CCR disclosure requirements.']
  ph_limit_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted pH value for treated effluent or finished water as specified in the permit. Used alongside ph_limit_min to define the acceptable pH range for compliance monitoring and corrosion control program evaluation.']
  ph_limit_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum permitted pH value for treated effluent or finished water as specified in the permit. pH is a key indicator of water chemistry and corrosion control. Typically 6.0–9.0 for NPDES effluent; 6.5–8.5 for drinking water under SDWA.']
  receiving_water_body varchar [note: 'Name of the surface water body (river, lake, estuary, or ocean) designated as the receiving water for permitted discharges (e.g., \'Ohio River\', \'Lake Michigan\'). Required for NPDES permits; used in water quality impact assessments and antidegradation analysis.']
  renewal_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit renewal application was submitted to the issuing regulatory agency. NPDES regulations typically require submission at least 180 days before expiration. Used to verify timely renewal and administrative continuance eligibility.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the permit renewal process. Tracks progression from initial application through agency review to final determination. Supports compliance calendar management and regulatory relationship tracking.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for submitting compliance reports (DMRs, MORs, or equivalent) to the regulatory agency as specified in the permit. Drives the compliance reporting calendar and submission deadlines tracked in the MOR submission workflow.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any special conditions, schedules of compliance, best management practices (BMPs), or additional requirements imposed by the regulatory agency beyond standard effluent limitations. Examples include PFAS monitoring requirements, CSO long-term control plan milestones, or LCRR service line inventory obligations.']
  term_years varchar [note: 'Duration of the permit in years as specified by the issuing agency. NPDES permits are typically issued for 5-year terms; state drinking water operating permits may vary. Used for renewal planning and compliance scheduling.']
  tss_limit_mg_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Permitted maximum concentration of Total Suspended Solids (TSS) in treated effluent, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Core secondary treatment standard for WWTP NPDES compliance. Used in DMR reporting and effluent quality monitoring.']
  tthm_mcl_ug_l decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for Total Trihalomethanes (TTHMs) as specified in the permit or applicable regulatory standard, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). The EPA MCL for TTHMs is 80 µg/L. A key Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) compliance parameter for drinking water permits.']
  turbidity_limit_ntu decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum permitted turbidity of finished water or treated effluent in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). For WTPs, the Surface Water Treatment Rule requires combined filter effluent turbidity not to exceed 0.3 NTU in 95% of measurements. Critical for MCL compliance.']
  violation_count_active varchar [note: 'Number of currently unresolved permit violations (effluent limit exceedances, reporting violations, or schedule of compliance violations) associated with this permit. Sourced from the regulatory agency\'s compliance tracking system. Used for compliance risk scoring and executive reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record of operating permits governing treatment facility operations. Captures permit type (NPDES, state drinking water operating permit, air permit, residuals permit), permit number, issuing regulatory agency, issue date, expiration date, permitted flow limits (MGD), effluent/finished water quality limits, special conditions, renewal status, and associated facility. Authoritative permit register for the treatment domain supporting compliance management.'
}

Table treatment.chemical {
  chemical_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for chemical']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Treatment chemicals (chlorine, alum, polymer, caustic) are materials with ERP material numbers, procurement specifications, and inventory management requirements. Fundamental link between treatment ch']

  Note: 'Reference master for all chemicals used in treatment processes. Captures chemical name, common name, CAS number, chemical category (coagulant, disinfectant, pH adjuster, corrosion inhibitor, fluoridation agent, dechlorination agent, nutrient), approved supplier list, typical dose range (mg/L), maximum allowable dose (NSF/ANSI 60 certified), storage requirements, and regulatory approval status. Serves as the authoritative chemical catalog for treatment operations.'
}

Table treatment.treatment_violation {
  treatment_violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each treatment regulatory violation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  change_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.change_order. Business justification: Treatment violations during construction/commissioning may trigger corrective action change orders. Non-compliance during startup may require design modifications or additional treatment processes. Li']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified treatment plant operator responsible for the process area at the time the violation was identified.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility where the violation occurred.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Violations trigger penalty costs (penalty_amount field present), corrective action expenses, and legal fees. Required for penalty accrual, corrective action cost tracking, regulatory cost reporting, a']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified treatment plant operator responsible for the process area at the time the violation was identified.']
  primary_treatment_lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Laboratory sample identifier from the LIMS (LabWare or similar) that produced the analytical result triggering this violation. Enables traceability from violation back to the original lab sample and chain of custody.']
  process_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.process_alarm. Business justification: Treatment violations are often triggered by process alarms (e.g., CT compliance failure, turbidity exceedance). The scada_alarm_tag string on treatment_violation should be replaced by FK to the treatm']
  regulatory_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory agency (e.g., EPA, State Primacy Agency) to which this violation must be reported.']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the NPDES permit under which the violation was recorded, applicable for wastewater discharge violations at WWTP facilities.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IBM Maximo CMMS work order created to execute the corrective action plan for this violation. Links compliance management to maintenance and operations workflows.']
  ccr_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation must be disclosed in the annual Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) sent to customers, as required by EPA SDWA regulations for community water systems.']
  contaminant_code varchar [note: 'EPA or state-assigned contaminant code for the parameter that was violated (e.g., TTHM for Total Trihalomethanes, PFAS, turbidity, lead, copper, BOD, TSS). Aligns with EPA SDWIS contaminant codes.']
  contaminant_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the contaminant or treatment parameter involved in the violation (e.g., Total Trihalomethanes, Turbidity, Lead, Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS), Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD)).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the corrective action plan must be completed and compliance restored, as agreed with the regulatory agency or mandated by the compliance schedule.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the corrective action plan developed to address the root cause of the violation and restore compliance. Includes immediate remediation steps, process adjustments, and preventive measures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this violation record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the violation was detected, either through SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian alarm, laboratory result, or operator observation. Supports CT compliance and public notification deadline calculations.']
  dmr_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The DMR reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this violation must be included for NPDES permit compliance reporting. Applicable for WWTP effluent violations. Links to the corresponding DMR submission.']
  enforcement_action_taken boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory agency has initiated a formal enforcement action (e.g., Notice of Violation, Administrative Order, Consent Order, penalty) in response to this violation.']
  enforcement_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of formal enforcement action taken by the regulatory agency (e.g., Notice of Violation (NOV), Administrative Order (AO), Consent Order, Civil Penalty, Criminal Referral). Null if no enforcement action was taken. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NOV|administrative_order|consent_order|civil_penalty|criminal_referral|compliance_schedule|none — promote to reference product]']
  exceedance_magnitude decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numeric difference between the measured value and the regulatory limit (measured_value minus regulatory_limit). Positive values indicate the degree of exceedance above the limit, supporting severity assessment and trend analysis.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Type of treatment facility where the violation occurred: WTP (Water Treatment Plant), WWTP (Wastewater Treatment Plant), STP (Sewage Treatment Plant), booster station, or storage facility.']
  is_repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a repeat occurrence of the same contaminant or parameter violation at the same facility within the preceding 12-month period. Repeat violations may trigger escalated regulatory response and increased penalties.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured concentration, level, or parameter value recorded at the time of the violation (e.g., turbidity in NTU, chlorine residual in mg/L, BOD in mg/L, CT value in mg·min/L). This is the quantitative result that triggered the violation.']
  measured_value_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured_value field (e.g., mg/L for chemical concentrations, NTU for Nephelometric Turbidity Units, mg·min/L for CT Contact Time, CFU/100mL for microbial counts). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg/L|NTU|mg·min/L|CFU/100mL|pH|mg/kg|ug/L|pCi/L|MPN/100mL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mor_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The MOR reporting period (YYYY-MM) in which this violation must be included for regulatory submission. Links the violation to the corresponding Monthly Operating Report for the facility.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the certified treatment plant operator to record observations, circumstances, and contextual information about the violation at the time of detection or during investigation.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed by the regulatory agency for this violation, in USD. Supports financial liability tracking, OPEX reporting, and GAAP/GASB financial disclosures for municipal utilities.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'The operating permit number (e.g., NPDES permit number, state drinking water permit) under which the facility operates and against which the violation is recorded. Used in regulatory correspondence and DMR/MOR submissions.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The specific treatment process area or unit operation where the violation originated (e.g., coagulation, filtration, UV disinfection, chlorination, RO, UF, MF, GAC, sedimentation, aeration, biological treatment).']
  public_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which public notification was issued to customers and the public in accordance with the EPA Public Notification Rule. Null if notification has not yet been issued.']
  public_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation triggers a mandatory public notification requirement under EPA SDWA Public Notification Rule (40 CFR Part 141 Subpart Q). True when the violation type and severity mandate customer and public notification.']
  public_notification_tier varchar [note: 'EPA Public Notification Rule tier classification: Tier 1 requires notification within 24 hours (acute health risk); Tier 2 requires notification within 30 days; Tier 3 requires annual notification. Drives notification workflow and deadline management.']
  regulatory_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The applicable Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL), treatment technique standard, or permit limit that was exceeded or violated. For CT violations, this is the required CT value in mg·min/L.']
  regulatory_limit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory standard that was violated: MCL (Maximum Contaminant Level), MCLG (Maximum Contaminant Level Goal), treatment technique standard, NPDES permit limit, action level (e.g., Lead and Copper Rule), or required CT (Contact Time for Disinfection).']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory agency (EPA or State Primacy Agency) was formally notified of this violation, as required by SDWA or NPDES permit conditions.']
  regulatory_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formal notification to the regulatory agency (EPA or State Primacy Agency) regarding this violation. Tracks whether notification is pending, has been submitted, has been acknowledged, or is overdue per regulatory deadlines.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the violation was resolved and compliance was restored, confirmed by a return-to-compliance sample result or regulatory acknowledgment. Null if the violation remains open.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause of the violation to support trend analysis and preventive action programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|process_upset|operator_error|source_water_event|chemical_dosing|monitoring_failure|reporting_failure|infrastructure|other — promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity classification of the violation based on the degree of exceedance, public health risk, and regulatory consequence. Critical indicates immediate public health risk; high indicates significant exceedance; medium indicates moderate concern; low indicates minor or administrative violation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this violation record was last modified in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and incremental Silver Layer processing.']
  violation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the treatment violation or regulatory exceedance was first identified or occurred, as recorded in the compliance management system.']
  violation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to this violation record, used in regulatory correspondence, public notifications, and compliance tracking systems.']
  violation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the violation record, tracking its progression from identification through corrective action to regulatory closure.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory violation category. MCL exceedance indicates a Maximum Contaminant Level was exceeded; treatment technique indicates a required process was not followed; monitoring and reporting indicates a missed sample or late report; CT violation indicates Contact Time for disinfection was insufficient; turbidity violation indicates NTU limits were breached. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mcl_exceedance|treatment_technique|monitoring_reporting|ct_violation|turbidity_violation|public_notification|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records treatment-related regulatory violations and exceedances at WTP/WWTP facilities. Captures violation type (MCL exceedance, treatment technique violation, monitoring and reporting violation, CT violation, turbidity violation), violation date, contaminant or parameter, measured value, regulatory limit, severity level, public notification requirement triggered, corrective action plan, resolution date, and regulatory agency notification status. Supports compliance management and public notification workflows.'
}

Table treatment.mor_submission {
  mor_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submission record in the treatment domain Silver layer.']
  amended_mor_submission_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on mor_submission (amended_mor_submission_id)']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Monthly operating reports during commissioning document new facility performance for regulatory approval. State approval to operate requires MOR submissions demonstrating compliance during startup per']
  compliance_mor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.mor. Business justification: Treatment MOR submissions feed compliance MOR records for regulatory submission. Business process: monthly operating report workflows where treatment operators prepare operational data (production, CT']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Monthly operating reports are prepared by facility/cost center and drive labor cost allocation. Required for regulatory reporting cost allocation, operator labor tracking, overhead distribution, and c']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified operator who prepared and submitted this Monthly Operating Report on behalf of the facility.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Water Treatment Plant (WTP) or Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) facility for which this MOR is submitted.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certified water treatment plant operator responsible for preparing and submitting the monthly operating report.']
  original_submission_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original MOR submission record when this record is a resubmission or amendment. Null for original submissions. Enables lineage tracking across submission versions.']
  regulatory_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory agency (e.g., state primacy agency, EPA regional office) that is the designated recipient of this MOR submission.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervising operator or plant manager who reviewed and approved the monthly operating report before submission.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency formally accepted and acknowledged the MOR submission as complete and compliant. Null if not yet accepted.']
  average_daily_flow_mgd decimal(12,3) [note: 'Average daily flow rate of water produced or wastewater treated during the reporting month, measured in million gallons per day.']
  avg_daily_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average daily flow volume processed at the facility during the reporting month, expressed in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Key operational metric reported in MOR and used for capacity planning and regulatory compliance.']
  avg_turbidity_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average turbidity of finished water or effluent during the reporting month, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Reported in MOR as an indicator of filtration and treatment effectiveness.']
  bod_avg_mg_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) of treated effluent during the reporting month, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Applicable to WWTP/STP MOR submissions; reported for NPDES permit compliance against effluent limits.']
  chlorine_residual_avg_mg_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average free or total chlorine residual concentration in finished water or distribution system during the reporting month, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Reported in MOR to demonstrate disinfection maintenance compliance.']
  chlorine_residual_compliance_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether chlorine residual levels in the distribution system during the reporting month met the minimum required disinfectant residual of 0.2 milligrams per liter.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or tracking number issued by the regulatory agency upon receipt of the monthly operating report submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MOR submission record was first created in the system (Silver layer ingestion time). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  ct_compliance_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all disinfection contact time calculations during the reporting month achieved the required CT values for pathogen inactivation.']
  ct_compliance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of operating days during the reporting month where the facility achieved the required CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) value as mandated by the Surface Water Treatment Rule. Critical SDWA compliance metric reported in MOR.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which the monthly operating report data was compiled, such as OSIsoft PI Historian, LIMS, or SCADA.']
  days_late varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the MOR submission exceeded the regulatory due date. Zero or null if submitted on time. Used for enforcement and compliance scoring.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the official MOR document (PDF or electronic file) submitted to the regulatory agency. Enables retrieval of the source report for audit and compliance review.']
  due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which this Monthly Operating Report must be submitted to the designated agency. Used for compliance tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the treatment facility covered by this MOR. WTP = Water Treatment Plant; WWTP = Wastewater Treatment Plant; STP = Sewage Treatment Plant. Determines applicable regulatory standards and report sections.']
  haa5_level_ug_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of Haloacetic Acids (HAA5), a class of Disinfection Byproducts (DBPs), in finished water during the reporting month, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). Reported in MOR for DBP Rule compliance; MCL is 60 µg/L.']
  has_mcl_exceedance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) was exceeded at the facility during the reporting month (True = exceedance occurred). Triggers public notification and corrective action requirements under SDWA.']
  has_sso_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Sanitary Sewer Overflow (SSO) event occurred at or associated with the facility during the reporting month (True = SSO occurred). Applicable to WWTP submissions; triggers mandatory regulatory notification under NPDES.']
  is_late_submission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MOR was submitted after the regulatory due date (True = late submission). Used for compliance tracking and regulatory penalty assessment.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MOR submission record was most recently modified in the system. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  max_daily_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum single-day flow volume processed at the facility during the reporting month, expressed in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Used to assess peak demand and permit compliance against maximum daily flow limits.']
  max_turbidity_ntu decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maximum single turbidity reading recorded during the reporting month, measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Used to identify peak exceedance events and assess MCL compliance.']
  mcl_exceedance_count varchar [note: 'Number of maximum contaminant level exceedances detected during the reporting month across all monitored parameters.']
  min_daily_flow_mgd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum single-day flow volume processed at the facility during the reporting month, expressed in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Used for operational range analysis and low-flow condition monitoring.']
  narrative_summary varchar [note: 'Operator-provided narrative summary describing significant operational events, process adjustments, equipment issues, or unusual conditions during the reporting month.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or compliance staff to record explanatory notes, unusual conditions, corrective actions taken, or other contextual information relevant to this MOR submission period.']
  operator_certification_grade varchar [note: 'Certification grade level of the submitting operator (e.g., Grade 1, Grade 2, Grade 3, Grade 4) as issued by the state primacy agency. Must meet or exceed the classification grade of the facility being reported on.']
  operator_certification_number varchar [note: 'State-issued certification number of the licensed operator who prepared and certified the monthly operating report.']
  operator_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued certification license number of the responsible operator who signed and submitted this MOR. Required by state drinking water programs to verify that reports are prepared by a certified operator.']
  peak_daily_flow_mgd decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum daily flow rate recorded during the reporting month, measured in million gallons per day.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'The NPDES permit number or state operating permit number associated with the facility for which this MOR is submitted. Links the report to the governing regulatory authorization.']
  ph_avg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average pH (Potential of Hydrogen) of treated water or effluent during the reporting month. Reported in MOR as a water quality parameter; typical drinking water range is 6.5–8.5 per SDWA secondary standards.']
  public_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which public notification was issued for any violations or exceedances during the reporting month.']
  public_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any violations or exceedances during the reporting month triggered mandatory public notification requirements.']
  pwsid varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned public water system identification number for the facility submitting the monthly operating report.']
  regulatory_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency officially accepted the monthly operating report as complete and compliant.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency formally rejected the MOR submission. Null if not rejected. Used to track resubmission timelines and compliance history.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation provided by the regulatory agency for rejecting the MOR submission. Null if not rejected. Used to guide corrective action and resubmission.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this MOR submission, used for regulatory correspondence and tracking (e.g., MOR-WTP01-2024-03).']
  reporting_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1–12) for which this Monthly Operating Report covers operational data. Combined with reporting_year to define the reporting period.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the calendar month covered by this monthly operating report submission.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the calendar month covered by this monthly operating report submission.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year (e.g., 2024) for which this Monthly Operating Report covers operational data. Combined with reporting_month to define the reporting period.']
  resubmission_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which a corrected monthly operating report must be resubmitted to the regulatory agency.']
  resubmission_number varchar [note: 'Sequential resubmission count for this reporting period and facility (0 = original submission, 1 = first resubmission, 2 = second resubmission, etc.). Used to track amendment history following regulatory rejection or voluntary correction.']
  resubmission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory agency has requested resubmission of the monthly operating report with corrections or additional information.']
  scada_data_source varchar [note: 'Identifier or tag reference for the OSIsoft PI Historian / SCADA data source from which process metrics (flow, turbidity, CT values) were extracted to populate this MOR. Supports data lineage and audit traceability back to the operational system of record.']
  submission_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the monthly operating report must be submitted to avoid non-compliance.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the MOR was submitted to the regulatory agency. Electronic submissions may be via state regulatory portal or EPA NetDMR; paper submissions are physical mail.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this monthly operating report submission for tracking and correspondence with regulatory agencies.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the MOR submission in the regulatory workflow. Draft indicates report is being prepared; submitted indicates it has been sent to the regulatory agency; accepted indicates agency approval; rejected indicates agency rejection requiring resubmission; under_review indicates agency is actively reviewing.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the MOR was formally submitted to the regulatory agency. This is the principal business event timestamp for the submission transaction.']
  supervisor_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supervising operator or plant manager approved the monthly operating report for submission.']
  total_production_volume_mg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of finished water produced or wastewater treated during the reporting month, measured in million gallons.']
  total_volume_treated_mg decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total volume of water or wastewater treated at the facility during the reporting month, expressed in Million Gallons. Derived from daily flow records and reported in the MOR for production accounting.']
  tss_avg_mg_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average Total Suspended Solids (TSS) concentration in treated effluent during the reporting month, expressed in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Applicable to WWTP/STP MOR submissions; reported for NPDES permit compliance.']
  tthm_level_ug_l decimal(8,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of Total Trihalomethanes (THMs), a class of Disinfection Byproducts (DBPs), in finished water during the reporting month, expressed in micrograms per liter (µg/L). Reported in MOR for DBP Rule compliance; MCL is 80 µg/L.']
  turbidity_compliance_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all turbidity measurements during the reporting month met the regulatory maximum contaminant level of 0.3 Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU) for 95% of samples.']
  turbidity_compliance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of turbidity measurements during the reporting month that met the Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) or treatment technique standard, expressed in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Key finished water quality metric in MOR.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this monthly operating report submission record was last modified in the system.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of regulatory violations recorded during the reporting month, including monitoring, reporting, and treatment technique violations.']
  violations_count varchar [note: 'Total number of regulatory violations (MCL exceedances, treatment technique violations, monitoring violations) recorded at the facility during the reporting month and included in this MOR submission.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for mor_submission. Referenced by: treatment.ct_compliance_record.mor_submission_id, treatment.dbp_monitoring_result.mor_submission_id'
}

Table treatment.scada_tag {
  scada_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian tag record in the master tag registry. Primary key for the scada_tag data product.']
  derived_from_scada_tag_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on scada_tag (derived_from_scada_tag_id)']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the water treatment plant (WTP) or wastewater treatment plant (WWTP) where this SCADA tag is deployed. Links the tag to its physical facility location.']
  instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset that provides the measurement for this SCADA tag. Links the tag to the asset management system (CMMS) for calibration tracking and maintenance scheduling.']
  pi_point_id varchar [note: 'The unique point identifier in the OSIsoft PI Historian system that corresponds to this SCADA tag. Used for direct integration with PI System for time-series data retrieval and analysis.']
  primary_scada_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset (e.g., flow meter, pressure transmitter, turbidimeter, chlorine analyzer, pH probe, level sensor) registered in the asset management system (IBM Maximo) that this tag represents.']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the specific treatment process unit (filter, clarifier, UV reactor, chemical dosing system, etc.) that this SCADA tag monitors or controls. Enables drill-down analysis by process stage.']
  treatment_process_process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific treatment process unit (e.g., coagulation basin, filter unit, UV reactor, chlorination contact chamber, RO train) to which this tag is associated.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment facility (WTP or WWTP) where this SCADA tag is physically installed and operationally active.']
  alarm_delay_seconds varchar [note: 'The time delay in seconds before an alarm is triggered after the tag value crosses the alarm threshold. Used to prevent nuisance alarms from transient process fluctuations. Typical values range from 0 (immediate) to 300 seconds (5 minutes).']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'The priority classification assigned to alarms generated by this SCADA tag. Critical alarms require immediate operator response, high alarms need prompt attention, medium alarms should be addressed within shift, low alarms are monitored, and informational alarms are logged for trending.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the physical instrument associated with this tag must next be calibrated per the facility\'s calibration schedule and regulatory requirements. Overdue calibration may invalidate regulatory compliance data and trigger MOR reporting obligations.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'The required frequency in days between instrument calibrations as specified by the manufacturer, facility SOP, or regulatory requirement. Used to auto-calculate calibration_due_date and trigger preventive maintenance work orders in IBM Maximo.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'The date this SCADA tag was first commissioned and activated in the PI Historian system, marking the start of its operational data collection lifecycle. Used for data availability analysis and asset age tracking.']
  compression_deviation decimal(10,4) [note: 'The exception/compression deviation threshold configured in PI Historian for this tag. Values within this deviation from the last stored value are suppressed to reduce archive storage. Critical for balancing data fidelity against storage efficiency. Expressed in the tag\'s engineering units.']
  compression_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether data compression is enabled for this SCADA tag in the historian system. Compression reduces storage requirements by only recording values when they change significantly, rather than at every scan interval.']
  compression_max_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The maximum time in seconds that PI Historian will suppress a value due to compression before forcing a new archive event, even if the value has not changed beyond the compression deviation. Ensures regulatory compliance data is recorded at minimum required intervals.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SCADA tag record was first created in the master tag registry. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Current data quality status of the tag as assessed by PI Historian or the data quality management process. \'good\' = reliable data; \'questionable\' = data may be unreliable (e.g., instrument drift suspected); \'bad\' = known bad data (e.g., instrument failure, communication error); \'substituted\' = manual or calculated substitute value in use; \'out_of_service\' = instrument intentionally offline.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the SCADA system or historian that is the authoritative source for this tag\'s data, such as OSIsoft PI Historian, Wonderware Historian, or specific PLC/RTU system. Enables data lineage tracking.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'The native data type of the tag value as stored in the PI Historian archive. Determines precision, storage requirements, and compatibility with downstream analytics. \'float32\'/\'float64\' for analog measurements, \'int16\'/\'int32\' for integer counts, \'boolean\'/\'digital\' for on/off states, \'string\' for text-based status tags. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: float32|float64|int16|int32|int64|boolean|string|digital — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  decommission_date date [note: 'The date this SCADA tag was decommissioned and removed from active data collection. Null for active tags. Used for historical data completeness analysis and regulatory audit trails.']
  display_digits varchar [note: 'The number of decimal places to display for this tag\'s value in HMI screens, PI Vision dashboards, and operator reports. Configured per instrument precision requirements (e.g., 2 for turbidity in NTU, 3 for chlorine residual in mg/L, 0 for integer counts).']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measurement for the tag\'s process variable as configured in PI Historian (e.g., NTU, MGD, GPM, PSI, mg/L, pH, °C, mJ/cm², %, ft, mW/cm²). Aligns with AWWA and EPA standard measurement units for water treatment process parameters.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,8) [note: 'The latitude coordinate (in decimal degrees) of the physical location of the instrument or measurement point associated with this SCADA tag. Enables spatial analysis and integration with GIS systems for asset mapping and network modeling.']
  gis_longitude decimal(11,8) [note: 'The longitude coordinate (in decimal degrees) of the physical location of the instrument or measurement point associated with this SCADA tag. Enables spatial analysis and integration with GIS systems for asset mapping and network modeling.']
  high_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The threshold value above which the SCADA system generates a high alarm for this tag. Alerts operators to potential process deviations requiring attention. May be regulatory-driven for compliance parameters such as turbidity or chlorine residual.']
  high_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The critical upper alarm threshold for this tag. When the process variable exceeds this value, a high-priority (Level 1) alarm is triggered in the SCADA/HMI system, typically requiring immediate operator intervention. For regulatory parameters (e.g., turbidity, chlorine residual), this may align with MCL exceedance thresholds.']
  high_range_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum value in the normal operating range for this SCADA tag. Values above this threshold may indicate sensor malfunction, process upset, or alarm condition. Used for data validation and quality checks.']
  high_trip_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The critical threshold value above which the SCADA system triggers an automatic shutdown or safety interlock for this tag. Represents a safety or regulatory compliance boundary that requires immediate automated response.']
  installation_date date [note: 'The date when the instrument or SCADA tag was first installed and commissioned in the treatment plant. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, depreciation calculations, and planning for equipment replacement.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical instrument or sensor type associated with this tag (e.g., flow_meter, pressure_transmitter, turbidimeter, chlorine_analyzer, ph_probe, level_sensor, temperature_sensor, uv_intensity_sensor, conductivity_analyzer, dissolved_oxygen_analyzer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flow_meter|pressure_transmitter|turbidimeter|chlorine_analyzer|ph_probe|level_sensor|temperature_sensor|uv_intensity_sensor|conductivity_analyzer|dissolved_oxygen_analyzer — promote to reference product]']
  is_critical_control_point boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag monitors a Critical Control Point (CCP) in the treatment process as defined by the facility\'s Water Safety Plan or Process Control Plan. CCP tags require enhanced monitoring, alarm management, and data quality assurance per AWWA and EPA guidance.']
  is_ct_compliance_parameter boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SCADA tag is used in CT (Contact Time) compliance calculations for disinfection. CT is the product of disinfectant concentration and contact time, required to demonstrate adequate pathogen inactivation per EPA regulations.']
  is_ct_compliance_tag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag is used in the calculation of CT (Contact Time for Disinfection) compliance values per the Surface Water Treatment Rule. CT compliance tags (disinfectant residual and flow rate) require continuous monitoring and are critical for regulatory reporting.']
  is_dmr_reportable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SCADA tag\'s data must be included in the Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR) submitted under the NPDES (National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System) permit for wastewater discharge compliance.']
  is_mor_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data from this tag is included in the Monthly Operating Report (MOR) submitted to the state primacy agency. MOR-reportable tags require higher data quality standards, complete audit trails, and validated calibration records.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'The date the physical instrument associated with this tag was most recently calibrated and verified. Used to assess data quality, support MOR submissions, and schedule preventive maintenance in IBM Maximo.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the physical location of the instrument or measurement point associated with this SCADA tag, including building, room, equipment identifier, and piping location. Aids field technicians in locating equipment for maintenance and calibration.']
  low_alarm_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The threshold value below which the SCADA system generates a low alarm for this tag. Alerts operators to potential process deviations requiring attention. May be regulatory-driven for compliance parameters.']
  low_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower warning alarm threshold for this tag. When the process variable falls below this value, a low-priority warning alarm is triggered (e.g., low chlorine residual warning before MCL violation, low flow rate indicating pump issue).']
  low_range_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum value in the normal operating range for this SCADA tag. Values below this threshold may indicate sensor malfunction, process upset, or alarm condition. Used for data validation and quality checks.']
  low_trip_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The critical threshold value below which the SCADA system triggers an automatic shutdown or safety interlock for this tag. Represents a safety or regulatory compliance boundary that requires immediate automated response.']
  maximo_asset_num varchar [note: 'The asset number assigned to the physical instrument in IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS). Enables cross-referencing between the SCADA tag registry and the asset management system for maintenance work orders, failure history, and lifecycle cost tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operators and engineers to document additional context, configuration details, operational considerations, or historical information about this SCADA tag that is not captured in structured fields.']
  parameter_measured varchar [note: 'The physical or chemical parameter that this SCADA tag measures or controls, such as flow rate, pressure, turbidity, chlorine residual, pH, temperature, level, or valve position. Uses industry-standard terminology.']
  pi_af_element_path varchar [note: 'The full hierarchical path of this tag within the OSIsoft PI Asset Framework (AF) element hierarchy (e.g., \'\\WaterUtilities\WTP1\Filtration\Filter1\EffluentTurbidity\'). Enables contextual analytics, template-based reporting, and structured navigation of the treatment process hierarchy in PI Vision and PI DataLink.']
  pi_server_name varchar [note: 'The name of the OSIsoft PI Data Archive server instance where this tag\'s time-series data is stored. Water utilities may operate multiple PI servers (e.g., one per WTP/WWTP or a centralized enterprise PI server). Used for data retrieval routing and server capacity management.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The fully qualified, unique tag name as registered in the OSIsoft PI Historian server. This is the primary operational identifier used by SCADA/PI systems to retrieve time-series data (e.g., \'WTP1.FILT1.TURB.PV\'). Must conform to PI tag naming conventions.']
  plc_controller_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the PLC or RTU (Remote Terminal Unit) that physically reads or controls this SCADA tag. Links the tag to the specific control hardware in the field.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The treatment process area or stage where this tag is located (e.g., raw_water_intake, coagulation, flocculation, sedimentation, filtration, disinfection_uv, disinfection_chlorination, ro_treatment, gac_adsorption, finished_water_storage, chemical_feed, backwash). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: raw_water_intake|coagulation|flocculation|sedimentation|filtration|disinfection_uv|disinfection_chlorination|ro_treatment|gac_adsorption|finished_water_storage|chemical_feed|backwash — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_limit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory limit applied to this SCADA tag. MCL (Maximum Contaminant Level) is an enforceable standard, MCLG (Maximum Contaminant Level Goal) is a non-enforceable health goal, action level triggers additional monitoring, treatment technique requires specific treatment processes, and operational limit is a facility-specific permit condition.']
  regulatory_limit_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The regulatory compliance threshold for this SCADA tag, such as Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL) for turbidity, chlorine residual, or disinfection byproducts. Used for automated compliance monitoring and Monthly Operating Report (MOR) generation.']
  regulatory_parameter varchar [note: 'Identifies whether this tag monitors a parameter subject to EPA or state drinking water/wastewater regulatory compliance requirements. Maps the tag to specific regulatory frameworks: turbidity (Surface Water Treatment Rule), chlorine_residual (SDWA disinfection), ct_value (CT compliance), uv_dose (UV disinfection rule), dbp_thm (THM/TTHM rule), dbp_haa5 (HAA5 rule), pfas (PFAS MCL), none (non-regulatory operational tag). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: turbidity|chlorine_residual|ph|fluoride|ct_value|uv_dose|toc|bod|tss|tds|pfas|dbp_thm|dbp_haa5|none — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scada_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the SCADA or DCS platform that is the originating source of this tag\'s data before it is archived in PI Historian. Supports multi-vendor SCADA environments and data lineage tracking.']
  scan_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the SCADA system reads or updates this tag\'s value, expressed in seconds. Critical for understanding data granularity and system performance. Typical values range from 1 second for critical control loops to 300 seconds (5 minutes) for slow-changing parameters.']
  scan_rate_seconds varchar [note: 'The frequency in seconds at which the PI Historian interface scans the PLC/RTU/SCADA source for new data values for this tag. Common values: 1 (high-frequency process control), 5, 10, 30, 60 (regulatory compliance monitoring), 300 (trend monitoring). Directly impacts data storage volume and process control responsiveness.']
  source_address varchar [note: 'The register address or OPC-UA node identifier on the PLC/RTU from which the PI Historian interface reads this tag\'s value (e.g., Modbus register address, OPC item path). Essential for SCADA interface configuration and data quality troubleshooting.']
  span_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum (span) end of the instrument\'s calibrated measurement range, expressed in engineering units. Used for signal scaling, over-range detection, and calibration verification (e.g., 10.0 for a 0–10 NTU turbidimeter, 200.0 for a 0–200 PSI pressure transmitter).']
  substitution_mode varchar [note: 'Defines how PI Historian handles missing or bad-quality data values for this tag during data gaps (e.g., instrument offline, communication failure). \'none\' = no substitution, gap remains; \'last_value\' = hold last good value; \'interpolated\' = linear interpolation; \'manual_entry\' = operator-entered substitute value for regulatory reporting.']
  tag_alias varchar [note: 'An alternative short name or alias for the tag used in HMI displays, operator logs, or legacy SCADA systems where the full PI tag name is too long. Facilitates cross-system tag mapping and operator familiarity.']
  tag_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the tag\'s measurement purpose and physical location (e.g., \'Filter 1 Effluent Turbidity Process Variable - WTP1\'). Used in operator HMI displays, PI Vision dashboards, and MOR reporting.']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric name or identifier assigned to the SCADA tag within the SCADA system. This is the human-readable tag name used by operators and engineers to identify the measurement point or control point.']
  tag_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the SCADA tag. \'active\' = tag is live and collecting data; \'inactive\' = tag exists but data collection is paused; \'decommissioned\' = tag retired from service; \'maintenance\' = tag temporarily offline for instrument servicing; \'spare\' = tag reserved for future instrument.']
  tag_type varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian tag data class type. \'analog_input\' = continuous process variable (e.g., turbidity, flow, pressure); \'analog_output\' = control output signal; \'digital_input\' = discrete on/off state; \'digital_output\' = discrete control command; \'calculated\' = PI Performance Equation derived tag; \'accumulator\' = totalizer tag; \'string\' = text-based state tag. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: analog_input|analog_output|digital_input|digital_output|calculated|accumulator|string — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SCADA tag record was most recently modified in the master tag registry (e.g., alarm limit change, scan rate adjustment, status update). Supports change management and audit trail requirements.']
  zero_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum (zero) end of the instrument\'s calibrated measurement range, expressed in engineering units. Used for signal scaling, calibration verification, and alarm setpoint validation (e.g., 0.0 for a 0–10 NTU turbidimeter).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for scada_tag. Referenced by: distribution.pressure_monitoring_point.scada_tag_id, treatment.source_water_intake.scada_tag_id, treatment.uv_disinfection_event.scada_tag_id'
}

Table treatment.facility_service_allocation {
  facility_service_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-territory allocation record. Primary key.']
  interconnection_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical interconnection point (valve, pump station, pipeline junction) where this facility connects to the distribution system serving this territory. Used for hydraulic modeling and emergency operations.']
