  service_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to service.service_territory identifying the territory receiving service from this facility']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service territory receiving water supply']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to treatment.facility identifying the facility providing service to this territory']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of facility capacity allocated to serve this territory under normal operations. Used for cost allocation, capital planning, and regulatory capacity reporting. Sum across all territories for a facility should approximate 100%.']
  backup_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility serves as a backup/redundant water source for this territory. Critical for emergency response and system resilience planning.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system']
  design_capacity_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Design capacity in million gallons per day allocated from this facility to this territory. Calculated from allocation_percentage and facility design capacity. Used for capital planning and regulatory capacity analysis.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this facility-territory allocation ended or will end. Null indicates current active allocation. Supports historical analysis of service configuration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this facility-territory allocation became effective. Supports historical tracking of service configuration changes for regulatory reporting and capital planning analysis.']
  facility_service_allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this facility-territory allocation. ACTIVE = currently serving, INACTIVE = temporarily offline, PLANNED = future allocation, DECOMMISSIONED = historical record.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was last modified']
  primary_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this facility is the primary (normal operations) water source for this territory. Used for emergency response protocols and operational planning.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational allocation between water treatment facilities and service territories. It captures the primary/backup facility designations, capacity allocation percentages for cost distribution, and interconnection infrastructure that enables one facility to serve multiple territories and one territory to receive water from multiple facilities. Each record links one facility to one service territory with attributes that exist only in the context of this operational relationship.. Existence Justification: Water utilities operate multiple treatment facilities serving overlapping service territories with primary and backup designations for system redundancy and emergency response. One facility provides water to multiple territories, and one territory receives water from multiple facilities through interconnection infrastructure. The business actively manages allocation percentages for cost distribution, primary/backup status for emergency protocols, and interconnection points for hydraulic modeling.'
}

Table treatment.chemical_supply_agreement {
  chemical_supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the chemical supply agreement record. Primary key.']
  treatment_chemical_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the treatment chemical being sourced under this supply agreement']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor supplying the chemical under this agreement']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply agreement indicating whether this vendor is currently approved and available for procurement of this chemical.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the master supply contract or blanket purchase order governing this chemical-vendor supply relationship. Links to legal procurement agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply agreement record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement became effective and the vendor was approved for supplying this chemical.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this supply agreement expires or requires renewal. Null if evergreen agreement.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order issued for this chemical from this vendor. Used to identify inactive supply relationships.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard delivery lead time in days from purchase order submission to delivery for this chemical from this vendor. Critical for inventory planning and emergency procurement.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the vendor for this chemical, typically in gallons, pounds, or tons. Impacts procurement batch sizing and inventory holding costs.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred or primary supplier for this chemical based on price, reliability, or strategic relationship.']
  unit_price decimal [note: 'Negotiated price per unit (typically per gallon, pound, or ton) for this chemical from this vendor. Enables price comparison across vendors for competitive sourcing decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply agreement record was last modified.']
  vendor_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific part number or SKU for the chemical product as cataloged by the supplier. Critical for purchase order accuracy and receiving verification.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual supply relationship between treatment chemicals and approved vendors. It captures vendor-specific sourcing terms, pricing, and delivery commitments for each chemical-vendor pairing. Each record links one chemical to one vendor with attributes that exist only in the context of this supply relationship, enabling competitive sourcing, price comparison, and supply chain resilience management.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, treatment chemicals are routinely sourced from multiple approved vendors to ensure supply chain resilience and competitive pricing (e.g., sodium hypochlorite from 3+ suppliers). Each vendor supplies multiple chemicals across their product catalog. The business actively manages vendor-specific supply agreements with distinct pricing, lead times, minimum order quantities, and contract terms for each chemical-vendor pairing.'
}

Table treatment.facility_project {
  facility_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this facility-project involvement record. Primary key for the association.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the capital improvement project']
  facility_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility operations staff member serving as primary contact for this project. Coordinates operational constraints, access, and commissioning activities.']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the water treatment facility affected by this project']
  actual_completion_date_at_facility date [note: 'Actual date project work was completed at this facility and turned over to operations. Null if work is ongoing.']
  actual_cost_at_facility decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual project expenditures incurred for work at this facility. Cumulative from project start through current date.']
  actual_start_date_at_facility date [note: 'Actual date project work commenced at this facility. Null if work has not yet started. Used for schedule variance tracking.']
  capacity_impact_mgd decimal(12,3) [note: 'Change in treatment capacity (million gallons per day) resulting from this project at this facility. Positive for capacity expansion, negative for temporary capacity reduction during construction.']
  construction_impact_level varchar [note: 'Severity of operational impact to the facility during construction. Critical impacts require detailed contingency planning and may affect treatment capacity or regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility-project involvement record was created in the system.']
  estimated_cost_at_facility decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated project cost allocated to work at this specific facility. For multi-facility projects, enables cost tracking by site.']
  facility_area_affected varchar [note: 'Specific functional area or process unit within the facility that is affected by this project. Enables facility operators to understand which treatment processes will be impacted and plan operational adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing facility-specific project considerations, operational constraints, lessons learned, or coordination issues.']
  operational_status_during_construction varchar [note: 'Operating mode of the facility or affected area during project construction. Tracks whether the facility can maintain normal treatment operations or requires operational adjustments.']
  permit_modification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this project requires modification of the facility\'s operating permit (NPDES, discharge permit, or air quality permit).']
  planned_completion_date_at_facility date [note: 'Planned date for project work to be completed at this facility, including commissioning and operational turnover.']
  planned_start_date_at_facility date [note: 'Planned date for project work to commence at this specific facility. In phased multi-facility projects, each site has its own start date.']
  project_phase_at_facility varchar [note: 'Current phase of the project work at this specific facility. In multi-facility projects, different sites may be at different phases (e.g., Site A in construction while Site B still in design).']
  project_role varchar [note: 'Role of this facility within the overall project scope. For regional projects, distinguishes between primary implementation sites, pilot sites for testing, and future phase sites.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this facility-project involvement record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational involvement between a water treatment facility and a capital improvement project. It captures which facilities are affected by which CIP projects, the specific areas of the facility under construction or upgrade, the construction phasing, and operational impacts during project execution. Each record links one facility to one CIP project with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, enabling project managers to track multi-facility projects and facility operators to manage multiple concurrent projects at a single plant.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, treatment facilities routinely have multiple concurrent capital projects (e.g., filter upgrades, SCADA improvements, chemical feed system replacements), and regional CIP programs often span multiple facilities (e.g., utility-wide SCADA replacement, disinfection byproduct compliance program across all plants). Project managers need to track which facilities are affected by each project, the construction phasing at each site, and operational impacts. Facility operators need to manage multiple concurrent projects affecting different areas of their plant.'
}

Table treatment.operator_qualification {
  operator_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this operator qualification record. Primary key for the association.']
  certifying_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the supervisor or lead operator who certified this qualification. Used for audit trail and accountability in the qualification process.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the qualified operator employee record']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the treatment process unit for which the operator is qualified']
  authorization_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the operator authorization expires for this process unit. Requires recertification assessment before this date to maintain qualification status. Critical for regulatory compliance.']
  competency_level varchar [note: 'Assessed competency level of the operator for this process unit. Trainee for operators in training under supervision, Qualified for independent operation, Advanced for complex troubleshooting, Lead for shift leadership, Instructor for training others.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent training session completed for this process unit. Used for tracking continuing education requirements and recertification scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding special conditions, restrictions, or observations related to this operator qualification. Examples include equipment-specific limitations or areas requiring additional training.']
  primary_operator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operator is designated as the primary operator for this process unit. Used for shift scheduling and responsibility assignment. True for primary operator, false for backup qualified operators.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date the operator successfully completed qualification assessment for this specific process unit. Used for tracking initial qualification and recertification cycles.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the operator qualification. Active for current valid qualification, Expired when authorization_expiration_date has passed, Suspended for temporary removal of authorization, In_Training for operators undergoing initial qualification.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the qualification and authorization relationship between treatment process units and utility operators. It captures operator-specific competency assessments, equipment-specific training records, and authorization status required for regulatory compliance and safe operation of treatment processes. Each record links one process unit to one qualified operator with certification dates, competency levels, and authorization expiration tracking.. Existence Justification: In water utility treatment operations, operators must be individually qualified and authorized to operate specific process units (membrane systems, UV reactors, chemical feed systems, etc.) based on equipment-specific training and competency assessments. Each operator is qualified on multiple process units to provide operational flexibility, and each process unit requires multiple qualified operators to ensure 24/7 shift coverage and regulatory compliance. The qualification relationship itself carries critical operational data including certification dates, competency levels, authorization expiration dates, and primary operator designations that belong to neither the process unit nor the employee alone.'
}

Table treatment.process_maintenance_plan {
  process_maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this process unit-job plan maintenance configuration record. Primary key.']
  job_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the standardized job plan template']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the treatment process unit being maintained']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance plan configuration was first created in the system.']
  frequency_override varchar [note: 'Process-specific maintenance frequency that overrides the default frequency_code from the job plan. Allows customization based on process unit operating conditions, flow rates, or regulatory requirements specific to this unit.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who most recently updated this maintenance plan configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this maintenance plan configuration was most recently updated.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Date when this job plan was most recently executed on this specific process unit. Used to calculate next due date and track compliance with maintenance schedules.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when this job plan is next scheduled to be performed on this process unit, based on frequency_override and last_performed_date. Critical for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  process_maintenance_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this process unit-job plan maintenance configuration. Only ACTIVE plans generate scheduled work orders.']
  process_specific_steps varchar [note: 'Additional or modified task instructions specific to this process unit that supplement the standard job plan task_sequence. Captures unit-specific SCADA tag references, valve numbers, sensor IDs, or equipment configurations.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan is required for regulatory compliance (EPA, state drinking water standards, NPDES permit conditions). Compliance-required plans have elevated priority and stricter scheduling enforcement.']
  seasonal_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan requires seasonal frequency adjustments (e.g., more frequent filter backwashing during high turbidity seasons, increased UV lamp inspection during peak demand periods).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the maintenance planner who configured this process unit-job plan relationship.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the maintenance planning relationship between treatment process units and standardized job plans. It captures process-specific maintenance configurations including frequency overrides, seasonal adjustments, and execution history for each process unit-job plan combination. Each record links one treatment process unit to one job plan with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific maintenance planning relationship.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, treatment process units (filters, clarifiers, UV reactors, membrane systems) require multiple different job plans for various maintenance activities (daily inspections, weekly calibrations, quarterly overhauls, annual certifications), and each standardized job plan template applies to multiple process units of the same type across facilities. Maintenance planners actively configure and manage these relationships with process-specific execution parameters, frequency overrides based on operating conditions, and compliance tracking for each unit-plan combination.'
}

Table treatment.process_compliance_monitoring {
  process_compliance_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this process-condition compliance monitoring record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the operator or compliance staff member responsible for monitoring and reporting this specific process-condition combination. Enables accountability and workload management.']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific permit condition that applies to this process unit']
  process_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the treatment process unit being monitored for compliance']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this process unit against this specific permit condition. Values: compliant (meeting all requirements), non_compliant (violation detected), pending_assessment (monitoring data collected but not yet evaluated), not_applicable (condition temporarily not applicable), under_compliance_schedule (phased implementation in progress). This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this process-condition monitoring relationship became active. May differ from permit effective date if process units are added or modified after permit issuance.']
  exceedance_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of permit limit exceedances for this process-condition combination since the permit effective date or last reset. Used for enforcement tracking and trend analysis. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this process-condition monitoring relationship expires or is superseded. Null if currently active. Used when process units are decommissioned or permit conditions are modified.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when compliance status was last evaluated for this process-condition combination. Updated each time monitoring results are assessed against the permit limit. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase.']
  last_exceedance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent permit limit exceedance for this process-condition combination. Null if no exceedances have occurred. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The actual monitoring frequency applied to this specific process-condition combination, which may differ from the permit\'s base requirement based on operational risk assessment or enhanced monitoring requirements. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection phase.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, operational adjustments, or compliance issues specific to this process-condition combination.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational compliance monitoring relationship between treatment process units and regulatory permit conditions. It captures the ongoing compliance assessment, monitoring frequency, and exceedance tracking for each process-condition combination. Each record links one treatment process unit to one permit condition with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance monitoring relationship.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, treatment process units must comply with multiple regulatory permit conditions simultaneously (e.g., a filtration unit must meet turbidity limits, CT requirements, and flow constraints), and each permit condition applies to multiple process units across the facility (e.g., effluent pH limits apply to all discharge points). Utilities actively manage compliance monitoring for each process-condition combination, tracking compliance status, assessment dates, exceedance counts, and monitoring frequencies as operational data that belongs to neither the process unit nor the permit condition alone.'
}

Table treatment.permit_compliance_obligation {
  permit_compliance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this permit-requirement compliance obligation record']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or compliance officer responsible for monitoring and ensuring compliance with this specific requirement for this permit.']
  permit_treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the treatment permit that must comply with the regulatory requirement']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement that applies to the treatment permit']
  treatment_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to treatment_permit']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting compliance issues, corrective actions, variances, or special conditions specific to this permit-requirement relationship.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this permit with respect to this specific regulatory requirement. Tracks whether the permit holder is meeting the obligation imposed by this requirement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific regulatory requirement became applicable to this specific permit. May differ from the permit effective date or the requirement effective date if the requirement was added mid-permit-term.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which compliance with this specific requirement was last assessed or audited for this permit. Used to track assessment schedules and identify overdue reviews.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The monitoring frequency required for this specific permit-requirement combination. May be more stringent than the base requirement frequency due to permit-specific conditions or facility risk profile.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next compliance assessment is due for this permit-requirement combination. Calculated based on monitoring frequency and last assessment date.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The reporting frequency required for this specific permit-requirement combination. Defines how often compliance data must be submitted to the regulatory agency for this obligation.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which any granted waiver expires and full compliance is required. Null if no waiver granted or waiver is permanent.']
  waiver_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory waiver or exemption has been granted for this specific requirement on this specific permit.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance obligation between a treatment permit and a regulatory requirement. It captures the specific monitoring, reporting, and compliance tracking for each permit-requirement combination. Each record links one treatment permit to one regulatory requirement with attributes that track compliance status, assessment dates, and monitoring schedules that exist only in the context of this specific permit-requirement relationship.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, each treatment permit must comply with multiple regulatory requirements (SDWA MCLs, Surface Water Treatment Rule, Lead and Copper Rule, Stage 2 DBPR, etc.), and each regulatory requirement applies to multiple permits across different facilities. The utility actively manages these permit-compliance obligations by tracking compliance status, monitoring schedules, assessment dates, and responsible staff for each permit-requirement combination. This is an operational compliance management process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table treatment.water_source {
  water_source_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for water_source']
  parent_water_source_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on water_source (parent_water_source_id)']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the water source was acquired or brought into service.']
  capacity_mgd decimal(12,3) [note: 'Design maximum water production capacity in million gallons per day.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the water source location.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the water source was retired or taken out of service, if applicable.']
  depth_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Typical depth of the water source (e.g., well depth).']
  elevation_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Surface elevation of the water source above mean sea level.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between routine inspections.']
  is_protected_area boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source lies within a legally protected environmental area.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction governing the water source.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Most recent date a regulatory or internal inspection was performed.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the water source location.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the water source location.']
  municipality varchar [note: 'Municipality where the water source is located.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the water source (e.g., River X, Reservoir Y).']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection.']
  source_code varchar [note: 'External code or identifier used for regulatory filings and operational systems.']
  source_conductivity_us decimal(8,2) [note: 'Electrical conductivity of source water in microsiemens per centimeter.']
  source_owner varchar [note: 'Organization or agency that owns the water source.']
  source_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'Typical pH measurement of the source water.']
  source_regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with applicable water‑quality regulations.']
  source_remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the water source.']
  source_salinity_psu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical salinity of the source water in Practical Salinity Units.']
  source_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical temperature of the water at the source.']
  source_turbidity_ntu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical turbidity of source water measured in Nephelometric Turbidity Units.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the water source by its origin.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province code for the water source location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the water source.']
  water_quality_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory water‑quality classification for the source.']
  watershed_name varchar [note: 'Name of the watershed or basin that feeds the water source.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for water_source. Referenced by water_source_id.'
}

Table treatment.discharge_point {
  discharge_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for discharge_point']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the water treatment plant that supplies water to this discharge point.']
  receiving_discharge_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on discharge_point (receiving_discharge_point_id)']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the organization responsible for the discharge point.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the organization responsible for the discharge point.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the discharge point location.']
  county varchar [note: 'County where the discharge point is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the discharge point record was first created in the system.']
  current_flow_rate_m3_per_hr decimal(12,2) [note: 'Most recent measured flow rate at the discharge point.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the discharge point.']
  discharge_capacity_m3_per_hr decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum design discharge capacity in cubic meters per hour.']
  discharge_point_category varchar [note: 'Business categorization of the discharge point within the treatment network.']
  discharge_point_code varchar [note: 'Business code or tag assigned to the discharge point for operational reference.']
  discharge_point_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the discharge point used in reports and dashboards.']
  discharge_point_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of discharge point per internal taxonomy.']
  discharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of water being discharged (e.g., outfall, effluent, stormwater).']
  downstream_receiving_body varchar [note: 'Name of the water body or system receiving the discharge.']
  environmental_impact_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the discharge point\'s environmental impact.']
  gps_accuracy_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval between inspections, expressed in days.']
  is_public_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discharge point is accessible to the public (true) or restricted (false).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or internal inspection.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance activity was performed.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the discharge point in decimal degrees.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the physical location of the discharge point.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the discharge point in decimal degrees.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Textual description of the routine maintenance schedule for the discharge point.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture discharge data (sensor, manual, remote).']
  monitoring_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA or sensor system that monitors this discharge point.']
  operational_status_date date [note: 'Date when the current status became effective.']
  owner_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that owns or is responsible for the discharge point.']
  permit_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the discharge permit expires.']
  permit_issue_date date [note: 'Date the discharge permit was issued.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit identifier authorizing the discharge.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the discharge point with applicable regulations.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province abbreviation for the discharge point location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the discharge point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the discharge point record.']
  water_quality_ph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured pH of the discharged water.']
  water_quality_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the discharged water in degrees Celsius.']
  water_quality_turbidity_ntu decimal(8,2) [note: 'Turbidity of the discharged water in Nephelometric Turbidity Units.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for discharge_point. Referenced by discharge_point_id.'
}

Table treatment.filter_unit {
  filter_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for filter_unit']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water treatment plant that houses the filter unit.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where the filter unit is installed.']
  paired_filter_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on filter_unit (paired_filter_unit_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor supplying the filter unit.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date the filter unit\'s measurement instruments were last calibrated.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration validity.']
  capacity_m3_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum water flow the filter unit can handle, expressed in cubic meters per hour.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the filter unit became operational.']
  compliance_ct_value_mg_per_l decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured chlorine residual (CT) value for compliance, expressed in mg/L.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Acquisition cost of the filter unit in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filter unit record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the filter unit was retired or removed from service.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the filter media was disposed of.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of spent filter media.']
  filter_age_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the filter unit was first commissioned.']
  filter_condition varchar [note: 'Overall health condition of the filter unit.']
  filter_efficiency_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured removal efficiency of the filter unit expressed as a percentage.']
  filter_media_type varchar [note: 'Material used as the filtering media.']
  filter_type varchar [note: 'Category of filter technology used.']
  flow_rate_m3_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current operational flow rate through the filter unit.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the filter unit was installed at the plant.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filter unit is critical to plant operation.']
  is_remote_monitored boolean [note: 'True if the filter unit is monitored via a remote SCADA/IoT system.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the most recent maintenance was performed.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between routine maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the maintenance schedule.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the filter unit.']
  media_grade varchar [note: 'Specification grade of the filter media (e.g., size, purity).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model designation provided by the manufacturer.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the filter unit.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the upcoming maintenance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the filter unit.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the filter unit.']
  pressure_drop_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured pressure drop across the filter unit in kilopascals.']
  remote_monitoring_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system providing remote monitoring data.']
  replacement_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number for the filter media or components used in replacements.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the filter unit.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the filter unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the filter unit record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the manufacturer\'s warranty ends.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for filter_unit. Referenced by filter_unit_id.'
}

Table customer.customer_account {
  customer_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer_account data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Utilities assign customer accounts to cost centers for management accounting, enabling revenue and cost tracking by geographic district, service territory, or operational division. Standard practice f']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Water utilities assign each customer account to a specific fund (water, wastewater, stormwater) for GASB fund accounting and regulatory reporting. Essential for financial statement preparation and rat']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.organization. Business justification: Commercial and industrial customer accounts should reference the organization master record. This allows proper tracking of corporate account structures. The account table currently only has person_id']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.facility. Business justification: Multi-facility utilities must track which WTP serves each account for facility-specific CCR distribution, water quality inquiry routing, source water reporting on bills, and facility maintenance notif']

  Note: 'Master record for every water and wastewater service account — residential, commercial, industrial, and municipal. Serves as the SSOT for customer identity across Oracle CC&B, SAP, AMI, and all downstream systems. Captures account number, account type (residential/commercial/industrial/municipal), account status (active/inactive/pending/suspended/terminated), service class, credit rating, account open date, account close date, language preference, paperless billing flag, autopay enrollment, lifecycle stage, and water budget allocation (where applicable). This is the primary anchor entity for the customer domain — all billing, metering, service delivery, and regulatory reporting references flow through this entity.'
}

Table customer.person {
  person_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the person record. Primary key for the person entity. Serves as the single source of truth for individual identity within the customer domain.']
  autopay_enrollment_date date [note: 'The date when the person enrolled in or opted out of autopay in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for payment preference tracking and billing operations.']
  autopay_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person has enrolled in automatic payment (autopay) for their utility bills. True if enrolled in autopay, False otherwise. Used for payment processing and customer convenience tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this person record was first created in the system in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trails, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  credit_check_consent_date date [note: 'The date when the person provided consent for credit check in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for compliance documentation and audit trails.']
  credit_check_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person has provided consent for the utility to perform a credit check. True if consent given, False otherwise. Required for deposit determination and account establishment.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The business segment classification of the person based on their primary account relationship. Used for rate classification, service level determination, and customer analytics. Aligns with rate schedule eligibility.']
  data_sharing_consent_date date [note: 'The date when the person provided or withdrew data sharing consent in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for compliance documentation and privacy management.']
  data_sharing_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person has consented to sharing their data with third parties (e.g., energy efficiency program partners, government agencies, research organizations). True if consent given, False otherwise.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The person\'s date of birth in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for identity verification, age-based service eligibility (e.g., senior citizen rates), and compliance with age-restricted service programs.']
  disability_accommodation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person requires disability accommodations for service delivery, communications, or billing. True if accommodations required, False otherwise. Used to ensure accessible service delivery.']
  disability_accommodation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing specific disability accommodations required by the person (e.g., large print bills, TTY/TDD phone service, accessible meter location). Used to ensure appropriate service delivery and ADA compliance.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'The primary email address for the person. Used for electronic billing, service notifications, CCR (Consumer Confidence Report) delivery, and digital customer communications.']
  government_id_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the government-issued identification document in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used to ensure identity verification documents remain current and valid.']
  government_id_issuing_state varchar [note: 'The U.S. state or territory that issued the government identification document. Used for identity verification and fraud prevention. Two-letter state abbreviation (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  government_id_number_masked varchar [note: 'The masked or partially redacted government-issued identification number (e.g., last 4 digits of SSN, masked driver\'s license number). Full number stored in secure vault; this field contains display-safe version for operational use.']
  government_id_type varchar [note: 'The type of government-issued identification document provided by the person for identity verification during account application or service connection. Used to comply with customer identification requirements.']
  identity_verification_date date [note: 'The date when the person\'s identity was successfully verified in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for audit trails and compliance reporting on customer identification procedures.']
  identity_verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the person\'s identity (in-person document review, online verification service, mail-in documentation, third-party identity verification service). Used for audit and compliance tracking.']
  identity_verification_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the person\'s identity verification process. Indicates whether government-issued identification has been validated and approved for service connection and account establishment.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'The person\'s preferred language for communications and service delivery. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code. Used to ensure accessible customer service and compliance with language access requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ENG|SPA|CHI|VIE|KOR|RUS|FRE|ARA|POR|OTH — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this person record was last updated in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'The legal first name of the person as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for identity verification and legal correspondence.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'The legal last name (surname) of the person as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for identity verification and legal correspondence.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'The legal middle name or initial of the person as it appears on government-issued identification documents. May be null if not provided.']
  life_support_equipment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person or household member relies on life-support medical equipment that requires uninterrupted water service. True if life-support equipment present, False otherwise. Used for service disconnection protection and emergency prioritization.']
  life_support_verification_date date [note: 'The date when the life-support equipment status was verified by medical certification in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for program compliance and annual recertification tracking.']
  low_income_assistance_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person is eligible for low-income assistance programs, rate discounts, or payment assistance based on income verification. True if eligible, False otherwise. Used for social equity program administration.']
  low_income_verification_date date [note: 'The date when the person\'s low-income status was verified for assistance program eligibility in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for program compliance and recertification tracking.']
  marketing_consent_date date [note: 'The date when the person provided or withdrew marketing consent in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for compliance documentation and preference management.']
  marketing_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional materials from the utility. True if consent given, False otherwise. Used to comply with marketing communication regulations.']
  paperless_billing_enrollment_date date [note: 'The date when the person enrolled in or opted out of paperless billing in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for billing preference tracking and environmental impact reporting.']
  paperless_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person has enrolled in paperless billing and prefers to receive bills electronically. True if enrolled in paperless billing, False if paper bills preferred.']
  person_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the person record. Active indicates the person is currently associated with one or more accounts. Inactive indicates no current account relationships. Deceased, merged, and duplicate statuses support data quality and master data management.']
  person_type varchar [note: 'The role or relationship type of the person within the customer domain. Distinguishes between account holders, co-applicants, authorized contacts, guarantors, and other person roles. One person may have multiple types across different accounts.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'The person\'s preferred method for receiving utility communications and notifications. Used to honor customer communication preferences and improve engagement rates.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the person prefers to be called in day-to-day interactions, which may differ from their legal name. Used for customer service communications and personalization.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'The primary contact phone number for the person. Used for service notifications, billing inquiries, outage alerts, and emergency communications. Format may include country code, area code, and extension.']
  primary_phone_type varchar [note: 'The type of primary phone number (mobile, home, work, other). Used to determine appropriate communication channels and times for customer outreach.']
  secondary_phone varchar [note: 'An alternate contact phone number for the person. Used as backup contact method when primary phone is unavailable or for emergency escalation.']
  secondary_phone_type varchar [note: 'The type of secondary phone number (mobile, home, work, other). Used to determine appropriate communication channels and times for alternate contact.']
  senior_citizen_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the person qualifies as a senior citizen for age-based rate discounts or service programs. True if senior citizen, False otherwise. Age threshold defined by utility policy and regulatory requirements.']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Generational or professional suffix appended to the person\'s legal name (e.g., Jr, Sr, II, III). Used to distinguish individuals with identical names.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual persons associated with water utility accounts — account holders, co-applicants, authorized contacts, and guarantors. Captures legal name, date of birth, government ID type and masked number, primary phone, secondary phone, email address, preferred contact method, language preference, identity verification status, and privacy consent flags. Distinct from the account entity: one person may hold multiple accounts (e.g., landlord with multiple rental properties). SSOT for individual identity within the customer domain.'
}

Table customer.organization {
  organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the organization entity. Primary key for commercial, industrial, and municipal organizations holding water and wastewater service accounts.']
  parent_organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organization in corporate hierarchies. Enables consolidated billing, enterprise account management, and multi-location reporting for corporate customers.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.vendor. Business justification: Water utilities frequently have dual-role relationships where commercial/industrial customer organizations are also approved vendors (chemical suppliers, contractors, equipment providers). Tracking th']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'Date when the organization\'s account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'Date when the organization\'s first service account was established with the utility. Used for customer tenure analysis and loyalty program eligibility.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organization account. Active accounts receive service; Suspended accounts have service restrictions; Closed accounts are terminated.']
  annual_revenue_range varchar [note: 'Estimated annual revenue range of the organization. Used for credit assessment, account prioritization, and business development targeting.']
  auto_pay_enrolled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization is enrolled in automatic payment processing. True if enrolled, False otherwise.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the organization\'s billing address, typically street number and name. Used for invoice delivery and legal correspondence.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of billing address for suite, floor, or department information.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the organization\'s billing address.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the organization\'s billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the organization\'s billing address.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code for the organization\'s billing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the organization record was first created in the customer information system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for the organization before service restrictions are applied. Expressed in USD.']
  credit_tier varchar [note: 'Internal credit rating tier assigned to the organization based on payment history, financial strength, and risk assessment. Determines deposit requirements and payment terms.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for the organization. Used for rate structure assignment, service level agreements, and market analysis.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or fictitious business name under which the organization operates, if different from legal name. Used for customer-facing communications and service delivery.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of security deposit held for the organization. Expressed in USD. Null if no deposit is required.']
  deposit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is required for this organization based on credit assessment. True if deposit is required, False otherwise.']
  employee_count_range varchar [note: 'Estimated number of employees at the organization. Used for water demand forecasting and commercial rate structure assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1_to_10|11_to_50|51_to_200|201_to_500|501_to_1000|over_1000|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  federal_tax_number varchar [note: 'IRS-issued Employer Identification Number (EIN) for tax reporting and identification purposes. Nine-digit number in format XX-XXXXXXX.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the organization was legally incorporated, registered, or chartered. Used for account tenure analysis and credit assessment.']
  incorporation_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code where the organization is legally incorporated or registered. Used for jurisdictional compliance and legal correspondence.']
  industrial_user_classification varchar [note: 'EPA classification level for industrial users subject to pretreatment requirements. Categorical Industrial User (CIU) discharges regulated pollutants; Significant Industrial User (SIU) meets discharge thresholds; Non-Significant does not meet thresholds.']
  industrial_user_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization is classified as an industrial user subject to pretreatment program requirements under the Clean Water Act. True if industrial user, False otherwise.']
  iup_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current IUP permit expires and renewal is required. Used for compliance tracking and permit renewal notifications.']
  iup_permit_number varchar [note: 'Permit number issued for industrial users authorized to discharge wastewater into the municipal collection system. Required for tracking pretreatment compliance and discharge monitoring.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the organization record was last updated. Used for data synchronization and audit trail.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The official registered legal name of the organization as filed with state incorporation documents or municipal charter. Used for contracts, billing, and regulatory reporting.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code identifying the organization\'s primary industry sector. Used for industrial user classification, rate structure assignment, and regulatory reporting.']
  organization_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure classification of the organization. Determines billing rules, credit policies, and regulatory treatment.']
  paperless_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization has opted for electronic billing only. True if paperless, False if paper invoices are required.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date. Standard terms are typically 30 days; extended terms may be granted based on credit tier.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for electronic billing, service notifications, and account correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary authorized representative or contact person for the organization. Used for account management, billing inquiries, and service notifications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the organization contact. Used for urgent service notifications, outage alerts, and account inquiries.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary contact person within the organization (e.g., Facilities Manager, CFO, City Manager).']
  sic_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit SIC code for legacy industry classification. Maintained for historical reporting and systems that have not migrated to NAICS.']
  special_billing_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for custom billing requirements, invoice formatting preferences, or special handling instructions for the organization account.']
  tax_exempt_certificate_number varchar [note: 'State-issued tax exemption certificate number for organizations claiming tax-exempt status. Required for audit compliance.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organization is exempt from sales tax or utility taxes. True if tax-exempt (typically government entities), False otherwise.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL for the organization. Used for customer research and business development.']

  Note: 'Master record for commercial, industrial, and municipal organizations that hold water and wastewater service accounts. Captures legal entity name, DBA name, federal tax ID (EIN), NAICS/SIC industry code, organization type (corporation/LLC/municipality/HOA/government), primary contact name, incorporation state, credit tier, industrial user classification (for IUP purposes), and parent organization reference for corporate hierarchies. Supports B2B account management and industrial pretreatment program tracking.'
}

Table customer.service_address {
  service_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service address record. Primary key for the service address entity.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Service addresses added or modified through CIP work (subdivision developments, service area extensions, infrastructure upgrades). Essential for geographic expansion tracking, service territory planni']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.dma. Business justification: Service addresses fall within District Metered Areas for non-revenue water tracking, leak detection program management, and consumption pattern analysis. Essential for AWWA water audit compliance, tar']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.parcel. Business justification: Service address is physically located on a land parcel; linking provides geographic context and eliminates isolated parcel table.']
  address_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this service address became effective and available for service delivery. Start of the address lifecycle.']
  address_end_date date [note: 'Date when this service address was retired or became unavailable for service. Null if address is still active.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including house number, street name, and street type. First line of the physical service delivery location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment number, suite, unit, building, floor, or other location qualifier within the premise.']
  address_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the service address including special access instructions, delivery restrictions, or historical context.']
  address_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application that created or last updated this service address record (e.g., CC&B, GIS, CRM).']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the service address record. Values: active (currently serviceable), inactive (temporarily not in use), pending (awaiting activation), retired (permanently closed).']
  address_validation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the address has been validated against USPS or other authoritative address databases. Values: validated, unvalidated, corrected, invalid.']
  apn varchar [note: 'County assessor parcel number uniquely identifying the land parcel for property tax and ownership purposes. Links service address to GIS parcel data.']
  building_type varchar [note: 'Type or classification of building structure at the service address (e.g., single-family, multi-family, office, retail, warehouse, school).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name where the service address is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the service address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  county varchar [note: 'County or parish name where the service address is located. Used for regulatory reporting and jurisdictional compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service address record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  customer_class varchar [note: 'Customer classification for rate and billing purposes. Values: residential, commercial, industrial, municipal, agricultural, institutional.']
  flood_zone_designation varchar [note: 'FEMA flood zone classification (e.g., A, AE, X, VE) indicating flood risk level. Used for infrastructure planning and emergency response.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier linking this service address to the corresponding feature in the Esri ArcGIS spatial database for network modeling and asset management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service address record was last updated or modified. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, network modeling, and spatial analysis.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, network modeling, and spatial analysis.']
  meter_location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of where the water meter is physically located at the premise (e.g., front yard, basement, alley, inside garage).']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'Current occupancy status of the premise. Values: occupied, vacant, seasonal, under_construction.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit ZIP code or ZIP+4 format postal code for the service address. Used for mail delivery and geographic segmentation.']
  pressure_zone varchar [note: 'Water distribution pressure zone identifier indicating the hydraulic zone serving this address. Used for hydraulic modeling and pressure management.']
  service_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the utility service territory or franchise area where the address is located. Determines regulatory jurisdiction and service provider.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of utility service(s) available at this address. Values: water_only, wastewater_only, water_and_wastewater, stormwater, reclaimed_water.']
  sewer_basin varchar [note: 'Wastewater collection basin or drainage area identifier indicating which sewer system and treatment plant serve this address.']
  standardized_address varchar [note: 'Fully standardized and concatenated address string following USPS formatting rules. Used for address matching and deduplication.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province abbreviation following USPS standards (e.g., CA, TX, NY).']
  within_service_boundary_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the address is within the utility\'s authorized service boundary. True if within boundary, False if outside.']

  Note: 'Physical location where water and/or wastewater service is delivered. Captures full street address, city, state, ZIP+4, county, parcel number (APN), GIS coordinates (latitude/longitude), service territory code, pressure zone, DMA (District Metered Area) code, sewer basin, flood zone designation, address validation status, and whether the address is within the utility service boundary. Linked to the distribution network and metering domains via service point. One address may have multiple active accounts over time (tenant turnover).'
}

Table customer.premise {
  premise_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the premise record. Primary key representing the utility\'s asset record for a serviceable location.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Premises created or significantly modified by CIP projects (new subdivisions, service area expansions, infrastructure replacements requiring new service lines). Critical for asset-to-project traceabil']
  connected_pipe_main_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pipe_main. Business justification: Premises physically connect to specific distribution mains for water service delivery. Essential for hydraulic modeling, service line inventory (LCRR compliance), outage impact analysis, and main brea']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the postal and Geographic Information System (GIS) address record for this premise. Links premise to distribution network location.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic service territory in which this premise is located. Determines regulatory jurisdiction and operational district.']
  backflow_prevention_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise requires backflow prevention devices due to cross-connection hazards. Mandatory for commercial, industrial, and irrigation services per Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA).']
  building_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total conditioned floor area of structures on the premise in square feet. Used for commercial water demand forecasting and capacity fee assessments.']
  building_type varchar [note: 'Physical structure classification of the building on the premise. Used for demand forecasting and infrastructure capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: detached_house|townhouse|apartment|office|retail|warehouse|manufacturing|school|hospital|government|mixed_use — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  connection_fee_paid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total one-time connection or capacity fees paid for this premise to establish utility service. Includes system development charges and impact fees.']
  connection_fee_paid_date date [note: 'Date when connection or capacity fees were paid for this premise. Used for revenue recognition and capital improvement program (CIP) funding tracking.']
  construction_year varchar [note: 'Year the primary structure on the premise was originally constructed. Used for infrastructure age analysis and lead service line risk assessment per Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this premise record was first created in the utility system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and regulatory compliance.']
  district_metered_area_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the District Metered Area (DMA) for water loss management and Non-Revenue Water (NRW) tracking. Used for hydraulic zone analysis.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this premise record was retired or became inactive. Null for currently active premises. Supports temporal data management and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this premise record became active and available for service connections. Supports temporal data management and historical analysis.']
  elevation_feet decimal(8,2) [note: 'Ground elevation of the premise in feet above mean sea level. Critical for hydraulic pressure calculations and gravity sewer flow analysis.']
  estimated_daily_demand_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected average daily water consumption in gallons for this premise based on premise type, units, and historical usage patterns. Used for capacity planning and meter sizing.']
  fats_oils_grease_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise is subject to Fats, Oils, and Grease (FOG) control program requirements. Applicable to food service establishments to prevent Sanitary Sewer Overflows (SSO).']
  fire_protection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise requires dedicated fire protection service (fire hydrant or sprinkler connection). Determines fire service charge applicability.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the premise location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for spatial analysis, hydraulic modeling, and field service dispatch.']
  gis_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the premise location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for spatial analysis, hydraulic modeling, and field service dispatch.']
  industrial_user_permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise requires an Industrial User Permit (IUP) for wastewater discharge due to industrial processes. Triggers pretreatment program compliance monitoring.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this premise record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  lot_size_square_feet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total land area of the premise parcel in square feet. Used for irrigation demand estimation and stormwater fee calculations.']
  low_income_assistance_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the premise qualifies for low-income customer assistance programs based on property characteristics or census tract designation. Used for rate discount eligibility.']
  meter_size_inches decimal(4,2) [note: 'Standard meter size in inches required or installed at this premise based on demand characteristics. Common sizes: 0.625, 0.75, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 6.0, 8.0 inches.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of individual dwelling or tenant units within the premise. Applicable for multi-family residential and commercial properties. Used for equivalent dwelling unit (EDU) calculations.']
  parcel_number varchar [note: 'County assessor\'s parcel number (APN) or tax lot identifier for the property. Used for cross-reference with property tax records and GIS systems.']
  peak_demand_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated peak instantaneous water demand in Gallons Per Minute (GPM) for this premise. Used for hydraulic modeling and service line sizing.']
  premise_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the premise, used in customer communications and field operations. Unique across the utility service territory.']
  premise_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the premise in the utility\'s service inventory. Determines whether the premise is available for service connection.']
  premise_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the premise based on its primary use and service characteristics. Determines applicable rate schedules and service requirements.']
  pressure_zone varchar [note: 'Hydraulic pressure zone designation for this premise within the distribution network. Determines operating Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) range and Pressure Reducing Valve (PRV) assignments.']
  reclaimed_water_service_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether recycled or reclaimed water distribution infrastructure is available for non-potable uses such as irrigation. Part of water conservation programs.']
  service_line_diameter_inches decimal(4,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the water service line in inches. Determines flow capacity and pressure loss from main to premise.']
  service_line_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the water service line connecting the distribution main to the premise. Critical for Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR) compliance and lead service line inventory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: copper|galvanized_steel|lead|pvc|pex|hdpe|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sewer_lateral_diameter_inches decimal(4,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the sanitary sewer lateral in inches. Determines wastewater conveyance capacity from premise to collection system.']
  sewer_lateral_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the sanitary sewer lateral connecting the premise to the collection main. Used for Inflow and Infiltration (I&I) risk assessment.']
  special_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, access instructions, or unique characteristics of the premise relevant to field service personnel and customer service representatives.']
  stormwater_service_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stormwater drainage infrastructure serves this premise. Determines applicability of stormwater utility fees.']
  wastewater_service_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sanitary sewer collection infrastructure is available to serve this premise. True if sewer mains are accessible within standard connection distance.']
  water_service_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether potable water distribution infrastructure is available to serve this premise. True if water mains are accessible within standard connection distance.']
  zoning_classification varchar [note: 'Municipal zoning code designation for the premise parcel. Determines permitted land uses and development density. Format varies by jurisdiction.']

  Note: 'The physical property or facility at which utility service is provided, representing the utility\'s view of a serviceable location independent of the customer occupying it. Captures premise type (single-family residential, multi-family, commercial, industrial, irrigation, fire protection), lot size, building type, number of units (for multi-family), number of fixture units (for capacity planning), zoning classification, construction year, lead service line status (known lead, galvanized requiring replacement, non-lead, unknown — per LCRR inventory), and whether the premise is subject to low-income assistance programs. Bridges the customer domain to the distribution network and metering domains. Distinct from service_address: a premise is the utility\'s asset record for the location; service_address is the postal/GIS record.'
}

Table customer.service_agreement {
  service_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer_service_agreement data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Each service agreement tied to specific fund type (water/wastewater/stormwater/reclaimed) for proper revenue recognition and GASB compliance. Critical for multi-service utilities with separate fund ac']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Service agreements are established for specific premises (physical properties). This FK links the contractual relationship to the physical location where service is provided. Currently service_agreeme']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_address. Business justification: Service agreements contain denormalized address fields that should reference the service_address master. This eliminates redundancy and ensures address consistency. The service_address table is the au']

  Note: 'The contractual relationship between a customer account and the utility for a specific service type (potable water, wastewater, recycled water, fire protection, irrigation) at a premise. Captures service agreement number, service type, rate schedule code, start date, end date, deposit amount, deposit waiver reason, service class, budget billing enrollment, and agreement status. This is the SSOT for what service a customer is contracted to receive and at what rate. Distinct from billing invoices (which are transactional) and from the rate schedule (which is a reference entity in the service domain).'
}

Table customer.account_person_rel {
  account_person_rel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account-person relationship record. Primary key.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water utility account in this relationship. Links to the service account that the person is associated with.']
  primary_account_person_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the person entity in this relationship. Links to the individual who has a role on the account.']
  accessibility_requirements varchar [note: 'Special accessibility or accommodation needs for this person when communicating about the account. May include requirements for large print, braille, TTY, or other assistive technologies.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'The date when this relationship was formally authorized or approved. Used for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  authorization_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the legal document or form that authorizes this relationship. May reference a power of attorney, lease agreement, or authorization form.']
  billing_authority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this person has authority to make billing decisions, dispute charges, or modify payment arrangements for the account. True if authorized, false otherwise.']
  ccr_delivery_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this person must receive the annual Consumer Confidence Report for water quality as required by the Safe Drinking Water Act. True if delivery is required, false otherwise.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The system user ID or username of the person who created this relationship record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this relationship record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this person-account relationship ended or will end. Null for open-ended relationships. Used for temporal tracking and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this person-account relationship became active and legally binding. Used for temporal tracking and compliance reporting.']
  emergency_contact_priority varchar [note: 'The order in which this person should be contacted in case of emergency at the service address. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Null if not an emergency contact.']
  financial_responsibility_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of account charges for which this person is financially responsible. Used in split-billing scenarios or shared responsibility arrangements. Value between 0.00 and 100.00.']
