  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Leak detection costs are allocated across pressure zones/DMAs for cost recovery and rate design in cost-of-service studies, rate case cost allocation, and NRW program tracking.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal field crew or team that performed the leak detection survey.']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the District Metered Area (DMA) in which the surveyed pipe segment is located, used for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) analysis.']
  grant_expenditure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant_expenditure. Business justification: Leak detection programs often funded by state/federal grants (EPA WIFIA, state revolving funds) requiring grant compliance reporting, eligible cost tracking, and drawdown documentation.']
  meter_leak_detection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.leak_detection_event. Business justification: Acoustic leak surveys investigate AMI-generated continuous flow alerts. Field crews need to reference which AMI leak event triggered the survey and update resolution status. Enables closed-loop leak m']
  nrw_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Revenue Water (NRW) reduction program or initiative under which this survey was conducted.']
  pipe_main_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific distribution main or service line segment that was surveyed for leaks.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pressure zone in which the surveyed pipe segment operates.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external contractor or vendor that performed the leak detection survey, if outsourced.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order or service request that initiated this leak detection survey activity.']
  ambient_noise_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of ambient noise levels during the survey, which can affect acoustic leak detection effectiveness.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved the survey results.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey results were officially approved by a supervisor or manager.']
  completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the leak detection survey was marked as completed and results were finalized.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this leak detection survey record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the reliability and accuracy of the survey data collected, used for quality assurance and analytics filtering.']
  equipment_used varchar [note: 'Description or list of specific leak detection equipment and instruments used during the survey (e.g., model numbers, device names).']
  estimated_leak_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated total leak flow rate for all leaks detected during this survey, measured in Gallons Per Minute (GPM).']
  leak_locations_gis varchar [note: 'Geographic coordinates or GIS feature identifiers for each leak location detected during the survey, typically stored as comma-separated latitude/longitude pairs or GIS asset IDs.']
  leaks_found_count varchar [note: 'Total number of leaks identified during this survey.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this leak detection survey record was last updated or modified.']
  repair_work_order_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a repair work order was automatically or manually generated as a result of leaks found during this survey.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The originally planned or scheduled date for this leak detection survey, which may differ from the actual survey date.']
  survey_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the survey cost amount. Defaults to USD for U.S. water utilities.']
  survey_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the leak detection survey was conducted in the field.']
  survey_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field crew completed the leak detection survey activity.']
  survey_length_feet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total linear length of pipe surveyed during this leak detection activity, measured in feet.']
  survey_method varchar [note: 'The technology or technique used to conduct the leak detection survey (e.g., acoustic correlator, listening stick, ground-penetrating radar, leak noise logger).']
  survey_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician observations, special conditions, challenges encountered, or additional context about the survey.']
  survey_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number assigned to this leak detection survey for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  survey_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome or result classification of the leak detection survey activity.']
  survey_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this leak detection survey based on factors such as DMA performance, customer complaints, or infrastructure criticality.']
  survey_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field crew began the leak detection survey activity.']
  survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leak detection survey activity.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead technician or operator who conducted the leak detection survey.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions during the survey, which may impact detection accuracy (e.g., rain, wind, temperature).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of field leak detection surveys conducted on distribution mains and service lines using acoustic correlators, listening sticks, or ground-penetrating radar. Captures survey date, survey method, crew/contractor, pipe segment surveyed, length surveyed (feet), leaks found count, leak locations (GIS), estimated leak rate (GPM), and survey outcome status. Feeds into repair work order generation and NRW reduction programs.'
}

Table distribution.main_break {
  main_break_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution main break event. Primary key for the main break record.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Main breaks frequently trigger capital replacement projects. Tracking which CIP project was initiated by a break event is essential for asset management, root cause analysis, and justifying project pr']
  compliance_public_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.public_notification. Business justification: Main breaks that compromise water quality trigger public notification requirements under Safe Drinking Water Act. Boil water advisories and service disruption notices must link to specific break event']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Emergency repair costs are allocated to cost centers/pressure zones for budgeting and rate recovery in O&M expense tracking, cost-of-service analysis, and budget variance reporting.']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the District Metered Area where the break occurred. Used for NRW (Non-Revenue Water) and UFW (Unaccounted-for Water) analysis.']
  encumbrance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.encumbrance. Business justification: Emergency repair purchase orders create encumbrances against maintenance budgets for budget control, expenditure tracking, and fiscal year-end reporting.']
  failure_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.failure_record. Business justification: Main breaks are asset failure events requiring root cause analysis, MTBF/MTTR tracking, failure mode classification, and reliability analysis. Links distribution failures to enterprise failure trackin']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_sample. Business justification: Main breaks trigger mandatory bacteriological sampling for regulatory compliance before system restoration per SDWA requirements. Break records must reference associated samples to document compliance']
  pipe_main_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution main pipe asset where the break occurred. Links to the distribution main asset registry.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pressure zone where the break occurred. Critical for hydraulic modeling and pressure management analysis.']
  repair_crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew or team that performed the repair work.']
  repair_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Break repairs require specific materials (pipe sections, fittings, clamps). Essential for emergency inventory management, material failure analysis, procurement planning, and break pattern analysis fo']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Main breaks trigger mandatory bacteriological resampling per Revised Total Coliform Rule (RTCR). Boil water advisories and repeat sampling directly tied to break events. Essential for tracking complia']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order created in the CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) for the repair activity. Links to IBM Maximo Asset Management work order.']
  boil_water_advisory_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a boil water advisory was issued to affected customers due to potential water quality compromise. True if advisory was issued, False otherwise.']
  break_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the main break event, typically formatted as MB-YYYYNNNNNN for external reference and reporting.']
  break_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the main break event: reported, dispatched, in progress, repaired, closed, or deferred.']
  break_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the main break was first detected or reported. Principal business event timestamp for the break occurrence.']
  break_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the main break failure mode: circumferential crack, longitudinal crack, blowout, joint failure, service line break, or corrosion pinhole.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the main break record was first created in the system. Audit trail timestamp for record creation.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts impacted by service disruption due to the main break.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when field crew was dispatched to the main break location.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the GIS feature identifier in Esri ArcGIS for the main pipe segment where the break occurred.']
  hydraulic_model_node_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the node identifier in Innovyze InfoWater hydraulic model for network analysis and pressure simulation.']
  installation_year varchar [note: 'Year the pipe was originally installed in the distribution network.']
  location_address varchar [note: 'Street address or nearest intersection where the main break occurred. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the main break location for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the main break location for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, special circumstances, or detailed notes about the main break event and repair.']
  operating_pressure_psi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Normal operating pressure in the main at the time of break, measured in PSI (Pounds per Square Inch).']
  pipe_age_years varchar [note: 'Estimated age of the pipe at the time of break, calculated from installation date to break date, measured in years.']
  pipe_diameter_inches decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal diameter of the failed pipe in inches.']
  pipe_material varchar [note: 'Material composition of the failed pipe: cast iron, ductile iron, PVC (polyvinyl chloride), HDPE (high-density polyethylene), steel, concrete, asbestos cement, or copper. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cast_iron|ductile_iron|pvc|hdpe|steel|concrete|asbestos_cement|copper — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the main break based on severity, customer impact, and safety considerations: emergency, urgent, high, medium, or low.']
  regulatory_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the main break requires regulatory reporting to EPA, state primacy agency, or Public Utilities Commission. True if reporting is required, False otherwise.']
  repair_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when repair work was completed and the main was returned to service.']
  repair_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the repair activity from start to completion, measured in hours.']
  repair_method varchar [note: 'Method used to repair the main break: clamp, sleeve, pipe replacement, joint repair, valve replacement, or temporary bypass.']
  repair_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when repair work commenced on the main break.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Source of the main break report: customer, field crew, SCADA alert, patrol, third party, or internal inspection.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the main break was officially reported to the utility operations center or SCADA system.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the main break: corrosion, age deterioration, soil movement, freeze-thaw cycle, pressure surge, third-party damage, manufacturing defect, or unknown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrosion|age_deterioration|soil_movement|freeze_thaw|pressure_surge|third_party_damage|manufacturing_defect|unknown — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  soil_condition varchar [note: 'Soil condition at the break location: clay, sand, gravel, rock, mixed, corrosive, saturated, or unknown. Influences corrosion rates and pipe stability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clay|sand|gravel|rock|mixed|corrosive|saturated|unknown — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  traffic_impact varchar [note: 'Impact of the main break on traffic and road access: none, lane closure, road closure, detour required, or emergency access restricted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the main break record was last modified. Audit trail timestamp for record updates.']
  water_lost_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of water lost during the break event, measured in gallons. Critical for NRW (Non-Revenue Water) and UFW (Unaccounted-for Water) reporting.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time of the break: normal, freezing, extreme cold, heavy rain, drought, snow, or extreme heat. Relevant for freeze-thaw and soil movement analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|freezing|extreme_cold|heavy_rain|drought|snow|extreme_heat — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of distribution network incidents including main breaks (pipe bursts, joint failures), planned shutdowns, and emergency/planned isolation events. Captures incident type, date/time, location (GIS coordinates, address, pipe main reference), failure mode (circumferential, longitudinal, blowout, joint) for breaks, valves operated with sequence for isolations, customers affected count and service addresses, estimated volume lost (gallons), repair method, repair duration, root cause classification, pressure zone impact, crew supervisor, and restoration confirmation timestamp. SSOT for outage management, customer notification, asset renewal prioritization, and regulatory reporting of service interruptions.'
}

Table distribution.valve_exercise {
  valve_exercise_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the valve exercise transaction record.']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Preventive maintenance costs are allocated across service areas for cost recovery in maintenance cost tracking, rate case O&M justification, and budget planning.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew or team assigned to the valve exercise work order.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field technician or crew member who performed the valve exercise.']
  inspection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection_event. Business justification: Valve exercising is a regulatory inspection activity (AWWA M44) with pass/fail outcomes, deficiency tracking, and compliance reporting. Natural mapping to enterprise inspection framework for audit tra']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Valve maintenance may identify parts needs (operating nuts, stems, seals). Links exercise program findings to preventive maintenance parts inventory and valve rebuild material tracking for maintenance']
  network_valve_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution network valve asset that was exercised.']
  primary_valve_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent preventive maintenance work order under which this valve exercise was performed.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field technician or crew member who performed the valve exercise.']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Overall condition assessment result from the valve exercise: pass indicates normal operation, fail or needs repair indicates deficiency requiring follow-up.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this valve exercise record was first created in the system.']
  deficiency_code varchar [note: 'Standardized deficiency or failure code from the CMMS failure classification taxonomy.']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of any deficiencies, damage, leaks, or operational issues observed during the valve exercise.']
  deficiency_noted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether any deficiency, damage, or maintenance need was identified during the valve exercise.']
  dma_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the District Metered Area in which the valve is located, used for NRW and UFW analysis.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes required to complete the valve exercise activity, used for labor planning and efficiency analysis.']
  exercise_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the valve exercise activity was performed.']
  exercise_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of valve operation during the exercise activity: opened, closed, or full open-close cycle.']
  exercise_method varchar [note: 'Method used to exercise the valve: manual wrench, powered actuator, or hydraulic tool.']
  exercise_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the valve exercise activity in the maintenance workflow.']
  exercise_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the valve exercise activity was completed, recorded from field device or CMMS.']
  final_position varchar [note: 'Confirmed position of the valve at the completion of the exercise activity.']
  final_position_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of valve opening at completion of exercise, where 0% is fully closed and 100% is fully open.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether follow-up maintenance or repair work order is required based on exercise findings.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the valve location in decimal degrees, captured from GIS or field GPS device.']
  gis_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the valve location in decimal degrees, captured from GIS or field GPS device.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this valve exercise record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  leak_detected boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a water leak was detected at the valve during exercise.']
  leak_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of any detected leak: none, minor seepage, moderate leak, or severe leak requiring immediate repair.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional observations, comments, or context related to the valve exercise activity.']
  operability_status varchar [note: 'Functional operability status of the valve following exercise: operable, inoperable, or restricted operation.']
  operating_nut_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the valve operating nut or stem: good, worn, damaged, or missing.']
  pressure_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the hydraulic pressure zone in which the valve is located, used for network modeling and operational planning.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the field technician who performed the valve exercise, captured for audit and accountability.']
  torque_reading decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured torque in foot-pounds required to operate the valve, used to assess mechanical condition and operability.']
  torque_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the torque reading: foot-pounds or newton-meters.']
  turns_to_close varchar [note: 'Number of complete rotations required to fully close the valve from its open position, recorded during exercise.']
  valve_box_condition varchar [note: 'Condition assessment of the valve box or vault structure: good, fair, poor, or missing.']
  valve_box_depth_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured depth in inches from ground surface to the valve operating nut, recorded during exercise for access planning.']
  valve_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the valve asset, typically displayed on field maps and work orders.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of valve exercise, recorded for context on field work conditions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of valve exercising activities performed as part of the annual valve maintenance program. Captures exercise date, valve reference, technician, number of turns completed, direction (open/close), final position confirmed, torque reading, valve condition assessment (pass/fail/needs repair), and any deficiencies noted. Supports CMMS integration with IBM Maximo and regulatory compliance for system operability.'
}

Table distribution.hydrant_flow_test {
  hydrant_flow_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hydrant flow test record. Primary key.']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Testing program costs are allocated to distribution cost centers for rate recovery in O&M expense allocation and regulatory compliance cost tracking.']
  inspection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection_event. Business justification: Flow testing is a regulatory inspection (NFPA 291, ISO rating) with documented outcomes, deficiency tracking, and compliance reporting. Links distribution testing to enterprise inspection management f']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Testing may reveal need for hydrant parts/replacement (nozzles, caps, valves). Links testing program to maintenance parts procurement and hydrant replacement planning for fire protection system reliab']
  primary_hydrant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fire hydrant asset that was tested. Links to the hydrant asset registry for location, installation date, and maintenance history.']
  tertiary_residual_hydrant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hydrant asset used as the residual hydrant (where pressure is monitored). Typically the hydrant being tested or a nearby hydrant on the same main.']
  test_crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew or work order team that performed the flow test. Used for quality assurance and crew productivity tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order under which the flow test was performed. Links the test to maintenance scheduling, crew assignment, and cost tracking systems.']
  available_flow_at_20psi_gpm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Calculated available fire flow at 20 PSI residual pressure in gallons per minute, derived using the NFPA 291 formula. This is the key metric for fire suppression adequacy and ISO rating.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the test equipment (pitot gauge, pressure gauges) was last calibrated. Ensures measurement accuracy and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the flow test record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dma_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the district metered area containing the tested hydrant. Used for NRW analysis and network performance monitoring.']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured flow rate during the test in gallons per minute, calculated from pitot pressure and outlet diameter. Represents the actual flow delivered during the test.']
  flushing_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration in minutes that the hydrant was flushed before or during the test to clear sediment and ensure representative flow measurements.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the hydrant in the GIS system. Used to link flow test results to spatial analysis, network modeling, and map visualization.']
  hydrant_condition_observed varchar [note: 'Field technician notes on the physical condition of the hydrant during testing, including leaks, corrosion, operability issues, or damage. Used to trigger maintenance work orders.']
  hydraulic_model_updated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the flow test results have been incorporated into the hydraulic model (e.g., Innovyze InfoWater) for calibration. True if updated, false if pending.']
  iso_fire_flow_adequacy varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the tested flow meets ISO Public Protection Classification requirements for the area. Adequate flow supports better ISO ratings and lower insurance premiums.']
  iso_rating_submitted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flow test result has been submitted to ISO as part of the Public Protection Classification rating process. True if submitted, false if not.']
  iso_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the flow test data was submitted to ISO for fire suppression rating purposes. Used for compliance tracking and rating cycle management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the flow test record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  model_update_date date [note: 'Date when the flow test data was incorporated into the hydraulic model. Used to track model calibration currency and data lineage.']
  nfpa_color_classification varchar [note: 'NFPA 291 color code classification based on available fire flow: Class AA (blue) >= 1500 GPM, Class A (green) 1000-1499 GPM, Class B (orange) 500-999 GPM, Class C (red) < 500 GPM, Inadequate for areas with insufficient flow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, anomalies, or context recorded by the field technician during the flow test. May include traffic conditions, customer interactions, or equipment issues.']
  number_of_outlets_flowed varchar [note: 'Count of hydrant outlets opened during the test. Multiple outlets may be flowed simultaneously to achieve higher flow rates for testing.']
  outlet_diameter_inches decimal(4,2) [note: 'Diameter of the hydrant outlet used for the flow test, measured in inches. Typically 2.5 inches for standard outlets. Required for accurate flow calculation from pitot pressure.']
  pitot_pressure_psi decimal(6,2) [note: 'Pressure reading from the pitot gauge placed in the discharge stream of the flow hydrant, used to calculate flow rate in gallons per minute.']
  pressure_zone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the pressure zone in which the tested hydrant is located. Used to correlate test results with hydraulic model zones and SCADA pressure monitoring.']
  residual_pressure_psi decimal(6,2) [note: 'Pressure measured at the residual hydrant while flow hydrants are open, recorded in pounds per square inch. Used to calculate available fire flow at 20 PSI residual per NFPA 291.']
  static_pressure_psi decimal(6,2) [note: 'Pressure measured at the residual hydrant before any flow is initiated, recorded in pounds per square inch. Baseline pressure used to calculate available fire flow.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead technician who conducted the test. Captured for certification tracking and quality control purposes.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient air temperature during the test in degrees Fahrenheit. Extreme temperatures may affect equipment accuracy and water viscosity.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date when the hydrant flow test was conducted. Used for compliance tracking, ISO rating cycles, and hydraulic model calibration schedules.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Method used to measure flow during the test. Pitot gauge is the standard NFPA 291 method; flow meters and pressure differential methods are alternatives.']
  test_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the flow test, typically formatted as a sequential or location-based test reference number used for external reporting and field crew coordination.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the flow test. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  test_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day when the test was performed, recorded in HH:MM format. Important for correlating with demand patterns and SCADA pressure data.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for conducting the flow test. Routine tests are scheduled, complaint tests respond to customer concerns, post-repair tests verify work, new installation tests commission assets, model calibration tests support hydraulic modeling, and ISO rating tests support fire insurance ratings.']
  water_clarity varchar [note: 'Visual assessment of water clarity during the flow test. Turbidity or discoloration may indicate main breaks, sediment accumulation, or water quality issues requiring investigation.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions during the test (e.g., clear, rain, snow, temperature). Recorded for quality control and to identify potential impacts on test accuracy.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all scheduled preventive maintenance activities performed on distribution network assets including hydrant flow tests, valve exercising, and unidirectional/conventional flushing. Captures activity type, date, asset reference (hydrant, valve, pipe segment), technician/crew, activity-specific measurements (static/residual pressure and flow rate for hydrant tests; turns completed, torque, and condition for valve exercises; volume discharged, duration, and pre/post turbidity/chlorine for flushing), outcome status (pass/fail/needs repair), deficiencies noted, and CMMS work order reference. Supports NFPA 291 compliance, ISO fire suppression rating, valve operability programs, water quality maintenance, and system turnover management.'
}

Table distribution.flushing_event {
  flushing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the flushing event record. Primary key.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Post-construction flushing and disinfection are commissioning activities tied to specific CIP projects. Required for regulatory acceptance, bacteriological clearance, asset turnover to operations, and']
  compliance_public_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.public_notification. Business justification: Flushing events that affect water quality or discolor water may require public notification under state regulations. Customer advisories for planned flushing and water quality impacts must link to spe']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Water quality maintenance costs are allocated across zones for cost-of-service analysis in O&M expense tracking, water loss accounting, and rate case cost justification.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field operations crew that performed the flushing activity.']
  crew_lead_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who served as the crew lead or supervisor for this flushing activity.']
  customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating customer service request or complaint that triggered this flushing event, if applicable.']
  hydrant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fire hydrant or blow-off valve used as the discharge point for the flushing activity.']
  pipe_main_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary distribution main segment that was flushed, linked to the GIS network topology.']
  turbidity_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.turbidity_reading. Business justification: Flushing events generate turbidity spikes monitored via online instruments or grab samples. Links distribution maintenance activity to water quality response. Enables tracking flushing effectiveness a']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water quality sample record in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) if a sample was collected.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order in the asset management system (CMMS) under which this flushing activity was scheduled and executed.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the flushing activity occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this flushing event record was first created in the database.']
  discharge_point_type varchar [note: 'The type of infrastructure asset used as the discharge point for flushed water.']
  dma_code varchar [note: 'The District Metered Area code in which the flushing activity took place, used for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) and Unaccounted-for Water (UFW) analysis.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the flushing activity in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamps or manually recorded by field crew.']
  equipment_used varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of specialized equipment used during the flushing activity (e.g., flow meters, turbidity meters, diffusers, flushing trailers).']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average flow rate during the flushing activity measured in Gallons Per Minute (GPM), used to assess flushing effectiveness and velocity.']
  flush_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the flushing activity was performed or is scheduled to be performed.']
  flush_effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the flushing effectiveness based on turbidity reduction, chlorine residual improvement, and visual water quality.']
  flush_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the flushing activity was completed and the system was returned to normal operation.']
  flush_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the flushing event, typically formatted as FLU-YYYY-NNNNNN for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  flush_reason varchar [note: 'The primary business driver or trigger for performing the flushing activity.']
  flush_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the flushing activity commenced, captured from field crew mobile devices or SCADA systems.']
  flush_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the flushing event in the operational workflow.']
  flushing_method varchar [note: 'The technique used to perform the flushing activity. Unidirectional Flushing (UDF) is the preferred method for sediment removal; conventional flushing is used for routine maintenance.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional flushing or corrective action is required based on post-flush water quality results.']
  gis_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the primary flushing location in decimal degrees, captured from GIS or mobile field devices.']
  gis_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the primary flushing location in decimal degrees, captured from GIS or mobile field devices.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this flushing event record was last updated, used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for crew observations, operational challenges, unusual conditions, or additional context not captured in structured fields.']
  post_flush_chlorine_residual_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Free chlorine residual concentration measured after flushing, in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Confirms restoration of adequate disinfection levels.']
  pre_flush_chlorine_residual_mg_l decimal(6,3) [note: 'Free chlorine residual concentration measured before flushing, in milligrams per liter (mg/L). Indicates disinfection adequacy and water age.']
  pressure_zone_code varchar [note: 'The pressure zone identifier where the flushing occurred, used for hydraulic modeling and pressure management.']
  public_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance public notification was sent to affected customers regarding potential water discoloration or service disruption.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the flushing activity occurred.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Street address or nearest intersection where the flushing activity took place, used for public communication and record-keeping.']
  traffic_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether traffic control measures were required for safe execution of the flushing activity.']
  volume_discharged_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume of water discharged during the flushing event, measured in gallons. Critical for water loss accounting and NRW analysis.']
  water_quality_sample_collected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal water quality sample was collected during or after the flushing event for laboratory analysis.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions during the flushing activity, relevant for operational safety and scheduling.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of unidirectional flushing (UDF) and conventional flushing activities performed on distribution mains to remove sediment, biofilm, and stale water. Captures flush date, pipe segment(s) flushed, flushing method (conventional vs. UDF), volume discharged (gallons), duration, pre/post turbidity (NTU) and chlorine residual readings, crew, and discharge point. Supports water quality maintenance and system turnover management.'
}

Table distribution.hydraulic_model_run {
  hydraulic_model_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hydraulic model simulation run. Primary key.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this hydraulic model run to a specific capital project, infrastructure study, or operational initiative for which the simulation was performed.']
  consumption_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to metering.consumption_profile. Business justification: Hydraulic model calibration requires actual consumption data from billing periods to validate demand patterns and loading assumptions. Engineers reference specific consumption profiles to adjust model']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Modeling scenarios support CIP project budgeting and capital planning for capital budget justification, project feasibility analysis, and rate case capital planning.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Hydraulic model runs are often scoped to specific pressure zones for network analysis and scenario planning. The hydraulic_model_run table currently has pressure_zone_code as a STRING, which should be']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Hydraulic model results support permit applications, capacity demonstrations, and system adequacy reports submitted to regulators. Model runs demonstrating fire flow capacity, pressure compliance, and']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or analyst who executed and is responsible for this hydraulic model run, used for accountability and technical review.']
  average_pressure_psi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean nodal pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) across all nodes in the distribution network during the simulation, providing an overall system pressure indicator.']
  boundary_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of hydraulic boundary conditions applied to the model, including source pressures, tank levels, pump operating rules, and valve settings that define the system state for this simulation.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of calibration and validation applied to the hydraulic model for this run: not calibrated (theoretical), preliminary (initial tuning), calibrated (field-verified), or validated (peer-reviewed and accepted).']
  convergence_achieved boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the hydraulic solver achieved numerical convergence within tolerance limits, ensuring the simulation results are mathematically valid and stable.']
  convergence_iterations varchar [note: 'Number of solver iterations required to achieve hydraulic convergence, used to assess model complexity and computational efficiency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hydraulic model run record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  demand_multiplier decimal(5,3) [note: 'Scaling factor applied to baseline water demand values to simulate different loading conditions (e.g., 1.0 for average day, 1.5 for peak day, 2.5 for maximum day plus fire flow). Used for capacity planning and stress testing.']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.dma. Business justification: Hydraulic model runs are often scoped to specific District Metered Areas (DMAs) for calibration and analysis. The hydraulic_model_run table currently has dma_code as a STRING, which should be normaliz']
  fire_flow_available_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum fire flow capacity in gallons per minute (GPM) available at critical nodes while maintaining minimum residual pressure, used for fire protection adequacy assessment and Insurance Services Office (ISO) rating.']
  gis_model_sync_date date [note: 'Date when the hydraulic model network topology was last synchronized with the authoritative Esri ArcGIS geographic information system (GIS) database, ensuring spatial accuracy and asset alignment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hydraulic model run record was last updated, supporting change tracking and data governance.']
  maximum_pressure_psi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Highest nodal pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) observed across the entire distribution network during the simulation, used to identify areas at risk of pipe bursts or excessive pressure.']
  maximum_velocity_fps decimal(8,3) [note: 'Highest water velocity in feet per second (fps) observed in any pipe segment during the simulation, used to identify areas at risk of excessive head loss, water hammer, or pipe erosion.']
  minimum_pressure_psi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Lowest nodal pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) observed across the entire distribution network during the simulation, critical for identifying areas at risk of low pressure or negative pressure events.']
  minimum_velocity_fps decimal(8,3) [note: 'Lowest water velocity in feet per second (fps) observed in any pipe segment during the simulation, used to identify areas with stagnant water or inadequate flushing velocity.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the hydraulic model used for this simulation run, tracking model evolution and configuration changes over time.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for analyst comments, observations, assumptions, or special conditions related to this hydraulic model run, supporting documentation and knowledge transfer.']
  output_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage location where detailed simulation output files (node results, pipe results, time-series data) are stored for this hydraulic model run.']
  pressure_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of nodes where pressure fell below the minimum regulatory threshold (typically 20 PSI under normal conditions or 30 PSI during fire flow) during the simulation, indicating potential compliance issues.']
  pump_energy_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by all pump stations in kilowatt-hours (kWh) during the simulation period, used for operational cost analysis and energy efficiency optimization.']
  run_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hydraulic model simulation run completed execution, whether successfully or with errors.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing identifier or reference number for the hydraulic model run, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  run_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose or objective for executing this hydraulic model run, such as Capital Improvement Program (CIP) planning, regulatory compliance analysis, operational troubleshooting, or infrastructure design validation.']
  run_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hydraulic model simulation run was initiated by the analyst or automated scheduler.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the hydraulic model run, indicating whether the simulation is pending, in progress, successfully completed with convergence, failed due to errors, or cancelled by the user. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: queued|running|completed|failed|cancelled|converged|not_converged — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scada_data_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA system or OSIsoft PI Historian data source used to calibrate or validate this hydraulic model run with real-time operational data.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Type of hydraulic simulation scenario executed: steady-state for snapshot conditions, extended period simulation (EPS) for time-series analysis, fire flow for emergency capacity, water quality for contaminant tracking, emergency response for outage planning, or capacity planning for infrastructure investment analysis.']
  simulation_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds for the hydraulic model simulation to execute, used for performance monitoring and optimization.']
  simulation_end_time timestamp [note: 'Simulated real-world end date and time for extended period simulation (EPS) scenarios, representing the conclusion of the modeled time window.']
  simulation_start_time timestamp [note: 'Simulated real-world start date and time for extended period simulation (EPS) scenarios, representing the beginning of the modeled time window.']
  system_demand_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total water demand across the entire distribution system in million gallons per day (MGD) for the simulation scenario, representing aggregate customer consumption and system losses.']
  tank_level_variation_feet decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum range of water level fluctuation in feet across all storage tanks during the simulation, indicating storage utilization and operational cycling.']
  time_step_minutes varchar [note: 'Temporal resolution in minutes for extended period simulation, defining the interval at which hydraulic conditions are calculated (e.g., 15, 30, or 60 minutes).']
  total_head_loss_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative hydraulic head loss in feet across the distribution network from source to the most remote node, indicating overall system friction and energy dissipation.']
  velocity_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of pipe segments where velocity exceeded recommended maximum thresholds (typically 5-8 fps) during the simulation, indicating potential water quality or infrastructure integrity concerns.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of Innovyze InfoWater (or equivalent) hydraulic model simulation runs for network analysis and operational decision support. Captures run date, model version/calibration date, scenario type (steady-state, extended period simulation, fire flow, water quality decay, energy optimization), demand conditions, boundary conditions, convergence status, key output metrics (min/max pressure PSI, velocity fps, headloss, system demand MGD), and modeler. Used for CIP planning, fire flow certification, pressure zone boundary changes, and regulatory pressure compliance demonstrations.'
}

Table distribution.network_isolation_event {
  network_isolation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network isolation event record.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Planned isolations for construction work must be linked to the driving CIP project for construction coordination, customer communication planning, project schedule tracking, and post-project water qua']
  compliance_public_notification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.public_notification. Business justification: Isolation events affecting large customer populations trigger public notification requirements for service disruptions. Regulatory requirements mandate advance notice for planned outages and emergency']
  contractor_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external contractor performing the work, if applicable.']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Planned outage costs (labor, water loss) are allocated to maintenance cost centers for O&M cost tracking and service reliability cost analysis.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crew assigned to perform the isolation and restoration work.']
  crew_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crew supervisor responsible for managing the isolation event.']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the District Metered Area (DMA) where the isolation event occurred.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crew supervisor responsible for managing the isolation event.']
  encumbrance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.encumbrance. Business justification: Planned work requiring contractor services creates budget encumbrances for budget control and contractor payment tracking.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_sample. Business justification: Isolation events requiring system depressurization trigger mandatory bacteriological sampling before restoration per regulatory requirements. Event records must reference samples to document complianc']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary pressure zone affected by this isolation event.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external contractor performing the work, if applicable.']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Isolation events require pre- and post-restoration water quality sampling to verify system integrity and bacteriological compliance before returning to service. Standard operating procedure for planne']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order that triggered or documented this isolation event.']
  actual_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the network isolation was completed and service was restored.']
  actual_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the network isolation began.']
  boil_water_advisory_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a boil water advisory was issued to affected customers due to the isolation event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this isolation event record was first created in the system.']
  critical_customers_affected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether critical customers (hospitals, fire stations, nursing homes) were affected by the isolation.']
  customer_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advance notification was sent to affected customers before the isolation event.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer accounts impacted by the service interruption during this isolation event.']
  estimated_water_loss_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of water lost during the isolation event due to draining, flushing, or leakage.']
  flushing_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether system flushing was performed after restoration to ensure water quality.']
  gis_isolation_boundary varchar [note: 'GIS polygon or geometry defining the geographic boundary of the isolated area.']
  hydraulic_model_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the isolation plan was verified using hydraulic modeling software before execution.']
  isolation_area_length_feet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total length in feet of the distribution network isolated during this event.']
  isolation_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the isolation event in hours from start to restoration.']
  isolation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the isolation event, used for tracking and reporting.']
  isolation_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the network isolation was necessary, including specific work to be performed.']
  isolation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the isolation event from planning through restoration.']
  isolation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the isolation event based on the reason for network shutdown. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned_maintenance|emergency_repair|main_break|construction|leak_repair|valve_replacement|hydrant_repair — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this isolation event record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or special circumstances related to the isolation event.']
  notification_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer notifications were sent for this isolation event.']
  pipe_segments_isolated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of pipe segment identifiers that were isolated during this event.']
  premises_affected_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical premises (addresses) impacted by the service interruption.']
  pressure_impact_description varchar [note: 'Description of how the isolation affected water pressure in adjacent zones or areas.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the isolation event based on urgency and impact.']
  restoration_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether full service restoration has been confirmed and all valves returned to normal operating position.']
  restoration_confirmed_by varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who confirmed successful restoration of service.']
  restoration_confirmed_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when service restoration was confirmed and documented.']
  scada_monitoring_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SCADA monitoring was active during the isolation event to track pressure and flow impacts.']
  scheduled_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the network isolation is scheduled to be completed and service restored.']
  scheduled_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the network isolation is scheduled to begin.']
  valve_count varchar [note: 'Total number of valves operated during the isolation event.']
  valves_operated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of valve identifiers that were operated (closed or opened) to achieve isolation.']
  water_quality_testing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether water quality testing is required before restoring service to customers.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of planned and emergency network isolation events where distribution pipe segments are shut down via valve operations. Captures isolation start/end datetime, isolated pipe segment(s), valves operated with sequence, customers affected count and service addresses, pressure zone impact, isolation reason (main break repair, planned maintenance, new connection, construction), notification status, crew supervisor, and restoration confirmation timestamp. Core to outage management, customer notification, and regulatory reporting of service interruptions.'
}

Table distribution.pipe_condition_assessment {
  pipe_condition_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pipe condition assessment record. Primary key.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CIP project created or updated based on this assessment, if applicable.']
  compliance_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action. Business justification: Condition assessments identify pipe deficiencies requiring corrective action under regulatory compliance schedules, enforcement agreements, or infrastructure improvement mandates. Assessment findings ']
  contractor_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractor or vendor who performed the assessment, if outsourced.']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Assessment program costs are allocated to asset management cost centers for capital planning cost tracking and condition assessment program budgeting.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal crew or team that performed the assessment.']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the District Metered Area (DMA) in which the assessed pipe main is located.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead technician or inspector who conducted the assessment.']
  encumbrance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.encumbrance. Business justification: Assessment contracts create encumbrances against capital or O&M budgets for contract commitment tracking and budget availability verification.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Assessment identifies pipe materials needing replacement, linking condition findings to procurement specifications for rehabilitation projects. Essential for capital planning, material standardization']
  pipe_main_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution main that was assessed.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pressure zone in which the assessed pipe main is located.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead technician or inspector who conducted the assessment.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractor or vendor who performed the assessment, if outsourced.']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Pipe rehabilitation activities (CIPP lining, coating) trigger water quality sampling to verify no contamination from construction materials or activities. Required for NSF/ANSI 61 compliance verificat']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order under which this condition assessment was performed.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the supervisor or engineer who reviewed and approved the assessment findings.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment findings were reviewed and approved.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the condition assessment was performed.']
  assessment_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition assessment activity was completed.']
