  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Technology or technique used to perform the condition assessment (e.g., CCTV inspection, acoustic pipe assessment, electromagnetic inspection, visual inspection, ultrasonic testing).']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the condition assessment activity.']
  assessment_report_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed condition assessment report, including photos, videos, and technical findings.']
  assessment_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the condition assessment activity began.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition assessment activity.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor or vendor who performed the assessment, denormalized for reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this condition assessment record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the assessment data meets quality standards (True=acceptable quality, False=quality issues noted).']
  data_quality_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing any data quality issues, limitations, or caveats related to the assessment findings.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete defects or anomalies identified during the assessment.']
  defect_types_identified varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of defect types found during the assessment (e.g., corrosion, cracks, joint separation, tuberculation, lining failure).']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the GIS feature identifier for the assessed pipe main in Esri ArcGIS.']
  internal_condition_grade varchar [note: 'Internal surface condition rating on a 1-5 scale, assessing corrosion, tuberculation, and lining integrity.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this condition assessment record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the condition assessment activity, findings, or follow-up actions.']
  overall_condition_grade varchar [note: 'Overall condition rating assigned to the pipe main on a 1-5 scale (1=excellent, 5=critical/failed), based on assessment findings.']
  pipe_length_assessed_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total length of pipe main inspected during this assessment, measured in feet.']
  pipe_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the pipe main assessed, denormalized for reporting convenience.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Recommended next step based on assessment findings (e.g., monitor, rehabilitate, replace, urgent repair, no action required).']
  recommended_action_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the recommended action for Capital Improvement Program (CIP) planning and asset renewal prioritization.']
  remaining_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated remaining useful life of the pipe main in years, based on condition assessment findings and deterioration modeling.']
  structural_condition_grade varchar [note: 'Structural integrity rating on a 1-5 scale, assessing the pipes ability to withstand loads and pressure.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead technician or inspector, denormalized for reporting.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of the assessment, which may affect inspection quality or findings.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of condition assessment activities performed on distribution mains using CCTV inspection, acoustic pipe assessment, electromagnetic inspection, or visual inspection. Captures assessment date, pipe main reference, assessment method, contractor, pipe length assessed (feet), defect types identified, overall condition grade (1-5 scale), remaining useful life estimate (years), and recommended action (monitor, rehabilitate, replace). Feeds CIP prioritization and asset renewal planning.'
}

Table distribution.dma_crew_coverage {
  dma_crew_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this DMA-crew coverage assignment record. Primary key.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the field service crew assigned to cover this DMA']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the District Metered Area being covered by this crew assignment']
  after_hours_responsibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew has after-hours (night/weekend/holiday) emergency response responsibility for this DMA. Used for on-call scheduling and emergency dispatch routing.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this crew coverage assignment ended or is scheduled to end. Null for current active assignments. Supports crew rotation and reassignment tracking.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when this crew began coverage responsibility for this DMA. Used to track historical crew assignments and support crew rotation analysis.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coverage responsibility: routine (scheduled maintenance and monitoring), emergency (main break and leak response), after_hours (night/weekend coverage), on_call (standby), primary (first responder), backup (secondary responder). Determines dispatch priority and labor costing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coverage assignment record was created in the system.']
  dma_crew_coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this coverage assignment: active (currently in effect), inactive (ended), suspended (temporarily paused), seasonal (active only during specific periods). Used to filter current assignments for dispatch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special instructions, geographic boundaries within the DMA, equipment requirements, access restrictions, or coordination notes for this crew-DMA assignment.']
  response_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank for this crew when multiple crews cover the same DMA (1=primary responder, 2=backup, etc.). Used by dispatch systems to route emergency work orders.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coverage assignment record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of field service crews to District Metered Areas for leak detection, valve exercising, emergency response, and NRW management. Each record links one DMA to one crew with coverage period, coverage type (routine vs emergency), response priority, and after-hours responsibility flag. Supports crew dispatch planning, emergency response routing, and labor allocation for water loss control programs.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, DMAs require coverage by multiple crews for different functions (leak detection crews, valve crews, emergency response crews) and shift periods (day shift, night shift, on-call). Simultaneously, field service crews are assigned to cover multiple DMAs across their service territory, with different coverage types (routine maintenance vs emergency response) and priority levels. The business actively manages these coverage assignments with start/end dates, coverage types, response priorities, and after-hours responsibility flags.'
}

Table distribution.pipe_procurement {
  pipe_procurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this pipe procurement line item record. Primary key.']
  pipe_main_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific pipe main segment that was procured under this contract line item']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the blanket procurement contract under which this pipe main was sourced']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date the pipe material was delivered to the job site or utility warehouse. Used for vendor performance measurement and project schedule variance analysis.']
  contract_line_item_number varchar [note: 'The specific line item number within the procurement contract that covers this pipe main procurement. Allows traceability to contract terms, pricing schedules, and delivery conditions specific to this material specification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pipe procurement record was created in the system. Audit trail for procurement planning and execution.']
  delivery_schedule date [note: 'The scheduled delivery date for this pipe main material as coordinated between procurement and the construction project schedule. Used for project planning and vendor performance tracking.']
  installation_specification varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical specification or standard that governs the installation of this pipe main under the contract terms. May reference AWWA standards, utility-specific installation procedures, or project-specific engineering drawings. Ensures contract compliance and warranty validity.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this pipe procurement record. Audit trail for status changes and data corrections.']
  material_certification_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s material certification or mill test report number for this specific pipe main batch. Required for quality assurance, regulatory compliance, and warranty claims. Links physical asset to material traceability documentation.']
  procurement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this procurement line item in the procurement-to-installation lifecycle. Tracks progression from planning through final installation.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The specific purchase order or contract release number issued against the blanket contract for this pipe main procurement. Links to accounts payable and financial systems for payment processing.']
  quantity_allocated decimal(12,2) [note: 'The quantity of pipe material allocated from the contract for this specific pipe main installation, typically measured in linear feet or number of pipe segments. Used for contract consumption tracking and remaining balance calculation.']
  total_line_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of this procurement line item (quantity_allocated × unit_price). Used for contract consumption tracking, budget management, and asset capitalization.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit price paid for this pipe main procurement under the contract terms at the time of release. May vary from the base contract price due to escalation clauses, volume discounts, or material specification adjustments. Critical for asset capitalization and lifecycle cost analysis.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the manufacturer or contractor warranty period begins for this pipe main procurement. May differ from installation date based on contract terms. Used for warranty claim eligibility determination.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the procurement line item relationship between pipe_main and procurement_contract. It captures the specific procurement details for each pipe main segment sourced under a blanket contract. Each record links one pipe main installation to one procurement contract with attributes that exist only in the context of this procurement transaction, including contract line item reference, allocated quantity, unit pricing, delivery schedule, and installation specifications.. Existence Justification: Water utilities operate multi-year blanket procurement contracts for pipe materials that supply multiple pipe main installations across different construction projects and time periods. A single procurement contract (covering specific materials, diameters, and pressure classes) supplies materials for many pipe main installations, and a large capital improvement project installing multiple pipe mains may draw from multiple procurement contracts due to different material specifications, vendor sources, or contract periods. The procurement line item relationship is actively managed by both procurement and distribution teams for contract consumption tracking, material traceability, warranty management, and asset capitalization.'
}

Table distribution.zone_operator_assignment {
  zone_operator_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each pressure zone operator assignment record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the certified operator assigned to the pressure zone']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the pressure zone requiring operator coverage']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the operator assignment to this pressure zone ended. Nullable for active assignments. Used for historical coverage analysis.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the operator was assigned to this pressure zone for the specified role. Used for coverage tracking and compliance reporting.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the operator assignment. Active for current assignments, Suspended for temporary holds, Inactive for ended assignments, Pending for future-dated assignments.']
  certification_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum operator certification grade or class required for this zone assignment per state regulatory requirements. Varies by zone complexity, population served, and treatment processes.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was created in the system.']
  last_coverage_date date [note: 'Most recent date when this operator provided coverage for this pressure zone. Used for rotation balancing and workload distribution analysis.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this assignment record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assignment record was last modified.']
  on_call_rotation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operator is part of the on-call rotation schedule for this pressure zone. True for operators in rotation, false for fixed assignments.']
  operator_role varchar [note: 'Role of the operator for this specific pressure zone assignment. Defines responsibility level and coverage type (Primary, Backup, On-Call, Relief).']
  primary_backup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operator serves as the primary backup for this pressure zone. True for designated primary backup, false otherwise.']
  rotation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number for on-call rotation scheduling. Defines the order in which operators are called for this pressure zone. Nullable for non-rotation assignments.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this assignment record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment between pressure zones and certified operators. It captures the 24/7 coverage model where multiple operators are assigned to each pressure zone with specific roles (primary, backup, on-call rotation) and tracks the required certification levels, assignment dates, and rotation status per state operator licensing requirements. Each record links one pressure zone to one operator with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, pressure zones require 24/7 certified operator coverage with multiple operators assigned to each zone in different roles (primary, backup, on-call rotation). Simultaneously, operators are cross-trained and assigned to multiple pressure zones to provide operational flexibility, coverage during absences, and compliance with state licensing requirements. This is an operational assignment relationship that the business actively manages with specific role designations, certification requirements, rotation schedules, and assignment dates.'
}

Table distribution.maintenance_zone {
  maintenance_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for maintenance_zone']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the District Metered Area that contains this maintenance zone.']
  hydraulic_model_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the hydraulic model used for simulation of the zone.']
  parent_maintenance_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_zone (parent_maintenance_zone_id)']
  area_sq_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Geographic area covered by the maintenance zone in square kilometres.']
  average_flow_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical water flow rate in the zone measured in gallons per minute.']
  average_pressure_psi decimal(6,2) [note: 'Typical operating pressure within the zone measured in pounds per square inch.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the zone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the maintenance zone record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance zone is scheduled to be retired or become inactive (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance zone became effective for operational use.']
  is_critical_infrastructure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone contains assets classified as critical infrastructure (true/false).']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity performed in the zone.']
  maintenance_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for scheduled maintenance activities in the zone.']
  maintenance_window_hours varchar [note: 'Typical duration in hours allocated for maintenance activities within the zone.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next routine maintenance in the zone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments, observations, or special instructions.']
  population_served varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers or residents served by the zone.']
  pressure_zone varchar [note: 'Pressure zone classification used for regulatory reporting and operational planning.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the regulatory jurisdiction for the zone.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the maintenance zone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the maintenance zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Official alphanumeric code used to reference the maintenance zone in operational systems.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the maintenance zone.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the zone based on service profile or land use.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for maintenance_zone. Referenced by maintenance_zone_id.'
}

Table distribution.nrw_program {
  nrw_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for nrw_program']
  predecessor_nrw_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on nrw_program (predecessor_nrw_program_id)']
  actual_nrw_reduction_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured percentage reduction in non‑revenue water realized to date.']
  actual_nrw_reduction_volume_mgd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured volume of water (million gallons per day) saved to date.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the program received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or authority that approved the program.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary budget allocated to the program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget amount.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or dataset providing the measurement data (e.g., SCADA, InfoWater).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the program objectives, scope, and approach.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date of the program; may be null for open‑ended initiatives.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent program performance review.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to measure NRW reduction outcomes.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next program performance review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the program.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the program based on strategic importance.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the program in reports and external systems.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the NRW reduction program.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category of the program indicating the primary focus area for water loss reduction.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or service area where the program is applied.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Internal department accountable for execution of the program.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating for the program.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the program officially begins.']
  target_nrw_reduction_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned percentage reduction in non‑revenue water achieved by the program.']
  target_nrw_reduction_volume_mgd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target volume of water (million gallons per day) to be saved through the program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for nrw_program. Referenced by nrw_program_id.'
}

Table distribution.network_node {
  network_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for network_node']

  upstream_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate upstream node in the hydraulic flow direction.']
  asset_condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing overall condition derived from inspections.']
  asset_lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the asset within its lifecycle.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Physical tag or barcode assigned to the node for inventory.']
  asset_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current book value of the node in US dollars.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the node entered service after testing and acceptance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the node.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the node\'s physical condition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the node record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the node was permanently removed from service, if applicable.']
  diameter_in decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the pipe or conduit at the node, inches.']
  elevation_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ground elevation of the node above sea level, meters.']
  flow_capacity_gpm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum design flow capacity for the node, gallons per minute.']
  flow_gpm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average water flow through the node, gallons per minute.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the node was physically installed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is considered critical for service continuity.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inspection of the node.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the node most recently underwent scheduled maintenance.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the node in decimal degrees.']
  length_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length of pipe segment represented by the node, meters.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the node in decimal degrees.']
  maintenance_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for scheduling maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Standard maintenance frequency for the node.']
  material varchar [note: 'Construction material of the node (e.g., ductile iron, PVC).']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'External or legacy code that uniquely identifies the node within legacy GIS or SCADA systems.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the node used in operations and reporting.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Category of the node describing its functional role in the distribution network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the node.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Internal department responsible for the node\'s operation and maintenance.']
  pressure_psi decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured hydraulic pressure at the node, pounds per square inch.']
  pressure_rating_psi decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum design pressure rating for the node, psi.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor attached to the node, if any.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the node.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the node record.']
  water_quality_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates if the node includes a water‑quality sensor.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for network_node. Referenced by upstream_node_id.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the invoice record. Primary key for the invoice entity.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Invoice belongs to a billing cycle; adding billing_cycle_id FK eliminates the redundant billing_cycle_code column and enables proper cycle lookup.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Capital projects generate invoices for construction work, design services, and developer-funded infrastructure. Essential for project cost recovery billing and capital accounting. Water utilities rout']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Invoices must link directly to customer accounts for customer service operations, billing inquiries, dispute resolution, and account history reporting. Customer service representatives need direct acc']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Invoices are issued against specific funds (water enterprise fund, wastewater fund, stormwater fund). Fund accounting and GASB reporting require tracking invoice-to-fund attribution for revenue recogn']
  point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service point (meter location) associated with this invoice. May be null for account-level charges not tied to a specific service point.']
  service_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.service_line. Business justification: LCRR compliance billing requires direct service line material reference on invoices for lead service line notices, leak adjustment workflows need service line condition metadata, and high-bill investi']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Water quality events (boil water advisories, contamination, taste/odor issues) trigger billing adjustments and credits. Utilities must link invoices to the specific sample event that justified the bil']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.facility. Business justification: Invoices must reference source treatment facility for cost-of-service allocation, rate case analysis, and facility-specific revenue tracking. Essential for regulatory rate design and capital cost reco']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net adjustment amount applied to the invoice, including credits for billing errors, leak adjustments, service quality issues, or promotional discounts. Positive values increase invoice total; negative values decrease it.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the consumption period covered by this invoice. Typically aligns with the current meter read date or service period end.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the consumption period covered by this invoice. Typically aligns with the prior meter read date or service period start.']
  ccr_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the annual Consumer Confidence Report (water quality report) was included with this invoice mailing. Required annually under Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA).']
  conservation_message varchar [note: 'Optional water conservation message or tip printed on the invoice to encourage efficient water use. May vary seasonally or based on drought conditions.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system process name that created this invoice record. For automated cycle billing, typically a batch process identifier; for manual invoices, the billing staff user ID.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the billing system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice. Typically USD for U.S. water utilities.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the invoice was delivered to the customer. Options include postal mail (paper bill), email (electronic bill), customer web portal, or SMS notification with portal link.']
  disconnection_date date [note: 'Date on which service may be disconnected if payment is not received. Typically set 10-15 days after due date, subject to regulatory notice requirements and customer protections.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has formally disputed charges on this invoice. Triggers dispute resolution workflow and may suspend collection activities.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received to avoid late fees or service disconnection. Calculated based on invoice date plus payment terms (typically 15-30 days).']
  generation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the invoice was generated. Automated cycle invoices are produced by scheduled batch billing runs; manual invoices are created by billing staff; off-cycle invoices are generated outside normal schedules; estimated invoices use projected consumption; corrected invoices replace prior errors.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was generated and issued to the customer. This is the official invoice date printed on the bill and used for aging calculations.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable invoice number printed on customer bills and used for customer service inquiries. Must be unique across all invoices.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the invoice. Draft invoices are pending finalization; issued invoices have been sent to customers; paid invoices are fully settled; partial_paid invoices have received some payment; overdue invoices are past due date; cancelled invoices are nullified before payment; void invoices are nullified after issuance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|paid|partial_paid|overdue|cancelled|void — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice based on billing circumstances. Regular cycle invoices are standard periodic bills; final invoices are issued upon service termination; estimated invoices use projected consumption when actual reads are unavailable; corrected invoices replace erroneous prior bills; off-cycle invoices are generated outside normal billing cycles; adjustment invoices correct prior billing errors.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether consumption values on this invoice are estimated rather than based on actual meter readings. True when meter is inaccessible or malfunctioning.']
  is_final boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a final invoice issued upon service termination or account closure. Final invoices settle all outstanding charges and close the billing relationship.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Late payment penalty assessed if payment is not received by due date. Calculated as a percentage of outstanding balance or flat fee per utility policy and regulatory limits.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system process name that last modified this invoice record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Updated whenever invoice status, amounts, or other attributes change.']
  other_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Miscellaneous charges not categorized as water, wastewater, or stormwater services. May include meter reading fees, service connection charges, late fees, returned payment fees, or special assessments.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Standard terms are typically 15, 21, or 30 days depending on utility policy and customer class.']
  previous_balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding balance carried forward from prior billing periods. Represents unpaid or partially paid amounts from previous invoices.']
  print_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was physically printed or electronically rendered for delivery to the customer. May differ from invoice date for batch processing.']
  rate_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tariff rate schedule applied to calculate charges on this invoice. Rate schedules vary by customer class (residential, commercial, industrial) and service type.']
  stormwater_area decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total impervious surface area (in square feet or square meters) used to calculate stormwater management fees. Applicable for properties subject to stormwater utility charges.']
  stormwater_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Charges for stormwater management services, typically calculated based on impervious surface area. Supports stormwater infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax charges applied to the invoice, including sales tax, utility tax, franchise fees, or other regulatory taxes as required by jurisdiction.']
  total_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount the customer must pay, calculated as sum of current period charges (water, wastewater, stormwater, other), taxes, adjustments, and previous balance. This is the headline amount printed on the invoice.']
  wastewater_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total charges for wastewater collection and treatment services during the billing period. May include volumetric charges, fixed fees, and strength-of-waste surcharges for industrial users.']
  wastewater_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of wastewater discharged during the billing period, typically calculated as a percentage of water consumption or measured separately for industrial customers. Measured in same unit as water consumption.']
  water_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total charges for potable water service during the billing period, including volumetric consumption charges and fixed service fees. Calculated based on applicable rate schedule and tier structure.']
  water_consumption_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for water consumption volume. Common units include gallons, cubic meters (m³), hundred cubic feet (CCF), or thousand gallons (kgal).']
  water_consumption_volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of potable water consumed during the billing period, measured in gallons or cubic meters depending on utility standard. Derived from meter readings or estimated when actual reads unavailable.']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a periodic, on-demand, or final statement of charges issued to a customer billing account for water, wastewater, stormwater, and other utility services. Captures billing period, due date, total amount due, invoice status (draft, issued, paid, overdue, cancelled), bill type (regular cycle, final, estimated, corrected, off-cycle), generation method, closing meter read reference (for final bills), deposit application amount, and forwarding address (for final bill refunds). SSOT for all customer-facing billing documents including final bills upon service termination.'
}

  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Individual invoice line items must reference specific CIP projects for detailed cost allocation, project accounting, and grant/bond compliance reporting. Required for tracking which project costs are ']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Invoice lines post to specific GL accounts based on charge type (water, wastewater, stormwater). Revenue reporting, rate case cost-of-service analysis, and regulatory compliance require GL account att']
