  enforcement_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.enforcement_action. Business justification: Enforcement actions (consent orders, administrative orders) mandate specific remediation work with deadlines. Work orders must reference enforcement actions to track compliance schedule milestones, re']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Work orders post labor, material, and overhead costs to specific GL accounts for financial reporting, rate case cost-of-service studies, and GASB compliance. Essential for integrating maintenance cost']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Work orders for customer-requested services (meter replacement, service line repair, reconnection after disconnection) generate billable charges appearing on invoices. Required for billable work order']
  job_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.job_plan. Business justification: Work orders frequently execute standardized job plans that define task sequences, labor requirements, materials, and safety procedures. This is a standard CMMS pattern (IBM Maximo, SAP PM). Job plan p']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_sample. Business justification: Work orders trigger water quality sampling for post-repair verification, commissioning tests, incident response, and regulatory compliance. Standard operational practice in water utilities to document']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location or site where the work is performed (Water Treatment Plant (WTP), Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP), pump station, District Metered Area (DMA)).']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule or route that generated this work order, if applicable.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Capital work orders and major repairs have associated POs for materials/equipment procurement. Enables three-way match (work order-PO-invoice), project cost reconciliation, budget tracking, and financ']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical asset (pipe, pump, valve, treatment equipment, vehicle) that is the subject of this work order.']
  reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Work order origination tracking distinguishes internal employee-reported issues from customer complaints, critical for root cause analysis and employee engagement metrics. Removes denormalized reporte']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_address. Business justification: Work orders require service address for crew dispatch, customer contact, access instructions, and completion documentation. Essential for field operations, customer communication, and GIS-based work o']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Work orders for main breaks, repairs, flushing, and tank maintenance trigger mandatory water quality sampling per regulatory requirements (e.g., bacteriological sampling after distribution system repa']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred for the work order, including labor, materials, contractor services, and overhead allocations.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when work execution was completed in the field.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours actually expended on the work order, captured from time sheets and labor transactions.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when work execution began in the field.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the supervisor or manager who approved the work order for execution or closure.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technician, crew, or contractor assigned to execute the work order.']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the failure (e.g., corrosion, age, improper operation, design defect).']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was formally closed and moved to historical status.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative entered by the technician upon work completion, documenting findings, actions taken, parts replaced, and any follow-up recommendations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was first created in the Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS).']
  downtime_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was out of service or unavailable due to this maintenance activity, critical for calculating Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).']
  estimated_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned total cost for the work order including labor, materials, and services, used for budgeting and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) / Operating Expenditure (OPEX) planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned total labor hours required to complete the work order, used for resource planning and scheduling.']
  failure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of failure or problem that triggered the work order (e.g., leak, blockage, electrical fault, mechanical wear).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was last updated or modified.']
  permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory permits (confined space entry, hot work, excavation, discharge) are required before work can commence.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency and impact of the work order on operations, safety, and regulatory compliance.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is driven by regulatory compliance requirements (Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA), Clean Water Act (CWA), National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES), Lead and Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR)).']
  remedy_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the corrective action taken to resolve the failure (e.g., repair, replace, adjust, clean).']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date when the work need was first reported or identified.']
  safety_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal safety plan, Job Safety Analysis (JSA), or Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) compliance documentation is required for this work order.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned date when work is scheduled to be completed.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when work is scheduled to begin.']
  supervisor_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the work order was reviewed and approved by the maintenance supervisor or manager.']
  warranty_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is associated with a warranty claim against a vendor or contractor for defective equipment or workmanship.']
  work_order_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the work to be performed, including scope, objectives, and any special instructions or safety considerations.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the work order, typically human-readable and used in field operations and reporting.']
  work_order_source varchar [note: 'Origin or trigger that created the work order (preventive maintenance schedule, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) alarm, customer complaint, inspection finding, operator observation, predictive analytics model, regulatory mandate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pm_schedule|scada_alarm|customer_complaint|inspection|operator_round|predictive_analytics|regulatory_requirement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order in the maintenance workflow, from creation through completion and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|scheduled|in_progress|on_hold|completed|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order by maintenance strategy: preventive maintenance (PM), corrective maintenance (CM), predictive maintenance (PdM), emergency, inspection, calibration, or capital project work. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preventive_maintenance|corrective_maintenance|predictive_maintenance|emergency|inspection|calibration|project — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for all maintenance activities — preventive (PM), corrective (CM), predictive (PdM), and emergency work orders. Captures work type, priority, originating asset, assigned crew/technician, scheduled and actual start/finish dates, labor hours, failure code, cause code, remedy code, downtime duration, and work order cost. Includes material line items: parts consumed or reserved with quantity, unit cost, issue date, storeroom, and reservation status. Enables actual vs. planned cost tracking (labor, material, contractor/service, overhead) per work order with GL account code and cost center. The authoritative SSOT for maintenance execution and O&M cost accounting, synchronized with IBM Maximo Work Order and SAP PM Order. Supports rate case cost justification and AWWA benchmarking.'
}

Table asset.pm_schedule {
  pm_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water treatment plant (WTP), wastewater treatment plant (WWTP), pumping station, or other facility where the asset is located and the preventive maintenance will be performed.']
  job_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized job plan that defines the detailed work instructions, materials, tools, and safety procedures for this maintenance task.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical asset (equipment, pipe, pump, valve, vehicle) to which this preventive maintenance schedule applies.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory requirements drive PM schedules (monthly chlorine residual checks per permit, quarterly backwash inspections per state rules). Links maintenance planning to compliance obligations, ensures ']
  asset_criticality_rating varchar [note: 'Criticality classification of the asset to which this schedule applies, indicating the impact of asset failure on operations, safety, regulatory compliance, or customer service.']
  auto_generate_work_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders should be automatically generated by the CMMS system when the schedule becomes due, or whether manual review and approval is required before work order creation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which preventive maintenance costs should be allocated for accounting and budgeting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this preventive maintenance schedule record was first created in the CMMS system.']
  downtime_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset must be taken out of service (downtime) to perform the preventive maintenance task, requiring operational planning and backup capacity arrangements.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this preventive maintenance schedule expires or is deactivated, after which no further work orders will be generated. Null indicates an open-ended schedule.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this preventive maintenance schedule becomes active and begins generating work orders.']
  estimated_downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated duration in hours that the asset will be out of service during preventive maintenance, used for operational planning and redundancy management.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of labor required to complete the preventive maintenance task based on estimated hours and labor rates, used for budgeting and OPEX planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete the preventive maintenance task, used for workforce planning and scheduling.']
  estimated_material_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of materials, parts, and consumables required to complete the preventive maintenance task, used for budgeting and OPEX planning.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric value representing the interval between preventive maintenance occurrences, interpreted based on the frequency unit (e.g., 30 for days, 500 for hours, 1000 for gallons).']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the frequency interval, defining whether the schedule is based on time periods (days, weeks, months, years), operating hours, operational cycles, or volume processed (gallons, cubic meters). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: days|weeks|months|years|hours|cycles|gallons|cubic_meters — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for recording preventive maintenance expenses in the financial system, distinguishing between OPEX maintenance and CAPEX capital improvements.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this preventive maintenance schedule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this preventive maintenance schedule record was last modified in the CMMS system.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Date when the preventive maintenance task was last completed, used to calculate the next due date for calendar-based schedules.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the next due date that the work order should be generated to allow for planning, scheduling, and resource allocation.']
  maintenance_task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the preventive maintenance task to be performed, including specific procedures, inspections, or servicing activities required.']
  meter_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Threshold value for meter-based preventive maintenance triggers; when the asset meter reading reaches or exceeds this value, a work order is automatically generated.']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Type of meter or measurement used for meter-based preventive maintenance triggers, such as operating hours, flow volume, pressure cycles, or other asset-specific usage metrics.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next preventive maintenance work order should be generated based on the schedule frequency and last performed date.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the preventive maintenance schedule based on asset criticality, regulatory requirements, or operational impact, used to sequence work order execution.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance schedule is required to meet regulatory compliance obligations such as EPA Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA), Clean Water Act (CWA), NPDES permit conditions, or state-level inspection requirements.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the specific regulatory requirement, permit condition, or compliance standard that mandates this preventive maintenance activity (e.g., SDWA Section 1412, NPDES Permit Condition 3.2).']
  required_skill_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required skill certifications or qualifications that technicians must possess to perform this preventive maintenance task safely and effectively (e.g., electrical license, confined space entry, SCADA operator certification).']
  safety_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of safety precautions, personal protective equipment (PPE), lockout/tagout procedures, confined space entry protocols, or other safety measures required for this preventive maintenance task.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the preventive maintenance schedule for easy identification by operations and maintenance staff.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule, typically assigned by the CMMS system for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the preventive maintenance schedule indicating whether it is actively generating work orders or has been suspended or deactivated.']
  seasonal_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal preventive maintenance schedule becomes inactive, applicable only when seasonal_schedule_flag is true.']
  seasonal_schedule_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance schedule is seasonal in nature, applicable only during specific months or seasons (e.g., winterization of outdoor equipment, summer peak demand preparation).']
  seasonal_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal preventive maintenance schedule becomes active, applicable only when seasonal_schedule_flag is true.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'Type of trigger mechanism that determines when preventive maintenance is due: calendar-based (time intervals), meter-based (usage thresholds), condition-based (sensor readings), runtime-based (operating hours), or cycle-based (number of operations).']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order that will be generated from this schedule, indicating the nature of the maintenance activity (preventive maintenance, inspection, calibration, lubrication, cleaning).']
  work_zone varchar [note: 'Specific work zone, area, or department within the facility where the preventive maintenance activity will take place, used for crew assignment and access control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this preventive maintenance schedule record in the CMMS system.']

  Note: 'Defines preventive maintenance schedules for assets, including maintenance task description, trigger type (calendar-based, meter-based, condition-based), frequency or interval, last performed date, next due date, estimated labor hours, required skill certifications, and associated job plan. Drives automatic work order generation in IBM Maximo. Supports AWWA O&M best practices and regulatory inspection compliance requirements.'
}

Table asset.job_plan {
  job_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job plan. Primary key.']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Job plans are standardized maintenance procedures defined for specific asset classes. Currently asset_class is stored as STRING code; should be FK to asset_class.asset_class_id for referential integri']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Job plans define standard maintenance tasks with estimated costs allocated to cost centers for annual O&M budgeting. Supports bottom-up budget development and cost center planning.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center or crew responsible for executing this job plan.']
  work_center_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work center or crew responsible for executing this job plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the supervisor or manager who approved this job plan for active use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job plan was approved and activated for use in work orders and preventive maintenance schedules.']
  confined_space_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job plan involves work in a confined space (e.g., wet well, tank, vault) requiring permit and atmospheric monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job plan record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job plan requires taking the asset or system offline, impacting operations and requiring coordination.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job plan version is superseded or retired. Null indicates the job plan is currently active with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job plan version becomes effective and available for use in work order generation.']
  environmental_impact_notes varchar [note: 'Description of potential environmental impacts and mitigation measures for this job plan (e.g., chemical spill containment, waste disposal procedures).']
  estimated_downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated duration in hours that the asset or system will be out of service during execution of this job plan.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor hours required to complete all tasks in this job plan. Used for work order scheduling and resource planning.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor cost in USD to execute this job plan, calculated from labor craft rates and estimated hours.']
  estimated_material_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total material and parts cost in USD required to execute this job plan, based on the materials list.']
  estimated_tool_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in USD for specialized tools, equipment rental, or contractor services required for this job plan.']
  frequency_code varchar [note: 'Standard frequency code indicating how often this job plan should be executed when attached to a preventive maintenance schedule. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|as_needed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_points varchar [note: 'List of quality control and inspection checkpoints to be verified during or after job execution, ensuring work meets standards.']
  job_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the job plan purpose, scope, and overview of the maintenance activity to be performed.']
  job_plan_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this job plan template across the organization. Used for referencing in work orders and preventive maintenance schedules.']
  job_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job plan. Only active job plans can be attached to work orders or preventive maintenance schedules.']
  job_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity type that this job plan supports. Determines scheduling and priority rules.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who most recently updated this job plan record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job plan record was most recently updated.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout procedures are mandatory for this job plan to ensure worker safety during equipment maintenance.']
  materials_list varchar [note: 'List of materials, parts, and consumables required for this job plan, including part numbers and estimated quantities.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, lessons learned, or special instructions for executing this job plan.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'List of required Personal Protective Equipment for workers executing this job plan (e.g., \'Hard hat, safety glasses, chemical-resistant gloves, steel-toe boots\').']
  priority varchar [note: 'Default priority level assigned to work orders generated from this job plan. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (e.g., 1=Critical, 5=Routine).']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on regulatory requirements, permits, or compliance documentation that must be completed as part of this job plan (e.g., EPA reporting, NPDES permit conditions).']
  required_labor_crafts varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of labor craft skills required to execute this job plan (e.g., \'Electrician, Mechanic, Instrumentation Technician\').']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking changes to the job plan over time. Incremented each time the job plan is updated and re-approved.']
  safety_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed safety procedures and precautions required for this job plan, including Lockout/Tagout (LOTO), confined space entry, Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), and hazard mitigation steps.']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document or standard from which this job plan was derived (e.g., \'AWWA M11 Steel Pipe Manual\', \'Manufacturer Service Bulletin 2023-04\').']
  task_sequence varchar [note: 'Step-by-step instructions detailing the ordered sequence of tasks to be performed. Each step includes action description, safety notes, and quality checkpoints.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the job plan that summarizes the maintenance activity (e.g., \'Pump Impeller Replacement\', \'Chlorine Analyzer Calibration\').']
  tools_list varchar [note: 'List of specialized tools, equipment, and instruments required to perform this job plan (e.g., \'Torque wrench, multimeter, pipe wrench set\').']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who originally created this job plan record.']

  Note: 'Standardized task library defining the step-by-step instructions, required labor crafts, estimated durations, safety procedures (LOTO, confined space, PPE), and materials/tools needed to perform a specific maintenance activity. Job plans are reusable templates attached to PM schedules and work orders. Sourced from IBM Maximo Job Plan module and aligned with AWWA O&M manuals and OSHA safety standards.'
}

Table asset.failure_record {
  failure_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset failure event record. Primary key.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical asset that experienced the failure (equipment, pipe, pump, valve, etc.).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Failure events trigger emergency repairs charged to cost centers for unplanned maintenance budget tracking. Critical for analyzing cost center budget variances and justifying contingency reserves in r']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_sample. Business justification: Failure investigations require water quality sampling to determine if contamination, corrosion, or water chemistry contributed to asset failure. Regulatory requirement for reportable incidents and sta']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Failures of customer-side assets (service line breaks, meter malfunctions, lateral collapses) must be tracked to premises for customer impact analysis, regulatory reporting (e.g., lead service line fa']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical asset that experienced the failure (equipment, pipe, pump, valve, etc.).']
  reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who reported or documented the failure event.']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Asset failures (main breaks, tank overflows, pressure loss events) trigger mandatory water quality sampling per regulatory requirements (e.g., bacteriological sampling after pressure loss per TCR/RTCR']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective maintenance work order generated in response to this failure event.']
  actual_repair_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred to repair or replace the failed asset after work completion.']
  affected_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or process affected by the failure (e.g., high service pump station, chlorination system, SCADA network, distribution main).']
  corrective_actions_recommended varchar [note: 'List of recommended corrective and preventive actions to prevent recurrence of similar failures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this failure record was first created in the system.']
  cso_event_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the failure resulted in a Combined Sewer Overflow event requiring regulatory reporting.']
  customers_affected_count varchar [note: 'Count of customer accounts impacted by the failure event (service interruption, pressure loss, water quality issue).']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the failure was discovered (SCADA alarm, operator inspection, customer complaint, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scada_alarm|operator_inspection|customer_complaint|scheduled_inspection|predictive_maintenance|routine_testing|emergency_response — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  downtime_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration in hours that the asset was out of service due to the failure.']
  emergency_response_actions varchar [note: 'Detailed description of immediate emergency response actions taken to mitigate the failure (e.g., valve isolation, backup system activation, customer notification).']
  environmental_impact_description varchar [note: 'Description of any environmental impact resulting from the failure (e.g., water body contamination, soil contamination, habitat damage).']
  failure_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the failure (e.g., age-related deterioration, improper installation, inadequate maintenance, design deficiency, operational error, external damage).']
  failure_criticality_score varchar [note: 'Calculated criticality score based on failure severity, service impact, and asset importance (typically 1-100 scale).']
  failure_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the asset failure occurred.']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Description of the operational impact and consequences of the failure on system performance and service delivery.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Classification of how the asset failed (e.g., mechanical fracture, electrical short, corrosion perforation, seal leakage, bearing seizure, control malfunction).']
  failure_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the failure event, typically auto-generated by CMMS.']
  failure_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the failure based on service impact, safety risk, and regulatory implications.']
  failure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failure record in the investigation and resolution workflow.']
  failure_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the asset failure was detected or occurred.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this failure record was last updated or modified.']
  mtbf_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this failure event should be included in MTBF reliability calculations for the asset class.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured time in hours from failure detection to restoration of asset to full operational status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, observations, or context related to the failure event.']
  overflow_volume_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated volume of wastewater overflow in gallons resulting from SSO or CSO event, if applicable.']
  pressure_drop_psi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured drop in system pressure (PSI) resulting from the failure event.']
  production_loss_mgd decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated water production capacity lost due to the failure, measured in Million Gallons per Day.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory agencies were notified of the failure event, if applicable.']
  regulatory_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the failure event requires notification to regulatory agencies (EPA, state primacy agency).']
  repair_cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost to repair or replace the failed asset, including labor, materials, and contractor costs.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the failure was fully resolved and the asset was restored to normal operation.']
  root_cause_analysis_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a formal root cause analysis (RCA) has been completed for this failure event.']
  root_cause_analysis_findings varchar [note: 'Detailed findings and conclusions from the root cause analysis investigation, including contributing factors and systemic issues identified.']
  service_interruption_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the failure resulted in a complete service interruption to customers.']
  sso_event_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the failure resulted in a Sanitary Sewer Overflow event requiring regulatory reporting.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the failure is covered under manufacturer or contractor warranty and a claim was filed.']

  Note: 'Documents asset failure events including failure date and time, failure mode, failure cause, failure effect, affected system, downtime duration, service impact (customers affected, MGD lost, PSI drop), emergency response actions taken, and root cause analysis findings. Supports reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis, MTBF/MTTR calculations, and regulatory SSO/CSO incident reporting. Linked to corrective work orders in IBM Maximo.'
}

Table asset.asset_meter {
  asset_meter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the asset meter record in the enterprise data platform. Primary key for the asset_meter product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent physical asset (pump, motor, valve, pipe segment, vehicle, etc.) to which this operational meter or counter is attached. Links to the asset master record in the asset domain.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the functional location or facility location record where the metered asset is installed. Links to the location hierarchy in the asset domain (e.g., WTP, pump station, WWTP). Supports geographic and facility-level aggregation of meter data.']
  metering_meter_id varchar [note: 'The native meter identifier from IBM Maximo Asset Management CMMS (METERNAME or internal Maximo meter key). Used for system-of-record traceability and bidirectional synchronization between the Silver Layer and Maximo.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent physical asset (pump, motor, valve, pipe segment, vehicle, etc.) to which this operational meter or counter is attached. Links to the asset master record in the asset domain.']
  alarm_high_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The upper alarm threshold value for this meter. When the reading exceeds this value, a SCADA or CMMS alarm is triggered. For example, high vibration alarm at 0.5 in/s for a pump motor. Supports integration with SCADA HMI alarm management.']
  alarm_low_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The lower alarm threshold value for this meter. When the reading falls below this value, a SCADA or CMMS alarm is triggered. For example, low flow alarm at 0.1 MGD for a treatment process feed pump.']
  average_reading_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The average rate of change per day for this meter, calculated and stored by the CMMS during reading entry (not a derived KPI — stored as a CMMS field used for PM trigger forecasting). For example, average run-hours per day for a pump. Used by meter-based PM triggers to forecast next maintenance due date.']
  avg_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the CMMS to compute the average_reading_rate. SLIDING_WINDOW: rolling average over a configurable number of recent readings. FIXED_PERIOD: average over a fixed calendar period. LAST_READING: uses only the most recent reading interval.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'The required frequency of calibration expressed in calendar days. Used to compute next_calibration_date from last_calibration_date and to schedule calibration work orders in IBM Maximo.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration status of the meter instrument. CALIBRATED: within valid calibration period. DUE: calibration due within the next calibration_interval_days. OVERDUE: calibration date has passed without recalibration. IN_PROGRESS: currently being calibrated. NOT_REQUIRED: meter type does not require formal calibration (e.g., simple cycle counters).']
  calibration_tolerance decimal(10,4) [note: 'Acceptable measurement tolerance or accuracy specification for this meter expressed in the meter\'s unit of measure. Readings outside this tolerance trigger recalibration. For example, ±0.5 PSI for a pressure transducer or ±0.01 MGD for a flow totalizer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset meter record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  current_reading decimal(18,4) [note: 'The most recent recorded value of the meter or counter as of the last_reading_date. For CONTINUOUS meters this is the cumulative total; for GAUGE meters this is the last observed point-in-time value. Principal quantitative fact for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the meter was permanently removed from service and retired. Null for active meters. Used for lifecycle reporting and asset history.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date on which the meter was physically installed on the asset. Used to calculate meter age, warranty expiry, and to establish the baseline reading at installation.']
  is_regulatory_meter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether readings from this meter are used in regulatory compliance reporting (e.g., EPA NPDES Discharge Monitoring Reports, Safe Drinking Water Act compliance, Monthly Operating Reports). True = regulatory-grade meter subject to calibration and data integrity requirements.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date on which the meter was most recently calibrated and certified as accurate. Critical for regulatory compliance where metered values (e.g., flow, pressure, chemical dosing) are used in EPA/NPDES reporting.']
  last_reading_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent meter reading captured, whether from SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian automated feed or manual entry in IBM Maximo. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the physical location where the meter is installed on the asset, e.g., \'Discharge side of Pump 3 at WTP Building A\', \'Inlet valve actuator shaft\'. Supplements the parent asset location for field technician navigation.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer of the meter instrument or sensor hardware, e.g., Endress+Hauser, Yokogawa, Emerson, Siemens. Used for procurement, warranty tracking, and spare parts management.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the meter reading, e.g., HOURS (run-hours), STARTS (motor starts), CYCLES (valve cycles), MGD (Million Gallons per Day), GPM (Gallons per Minute), PSI (Pounds per Square Inch), NTU (Nephelometric Turbidity Units), RPM, DEGREES_F, INCHES. Aligns with OSIsoft PI Historian tag engineering units.']
  meter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the meter or counter, e.g., \'Pump 3 Run Hours\', \'Valve 12 Cycle Count\', \'Influent Flow Totalizer\'. Used for display in CMMS work orders and PM triggers.']
  meter_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier for the meter as assigned in IBM Maximo CMMS or SAP PM. Used for cross-system reference and field identification. Distinct from customer billing meter numbers managed by the Metering domain.']
  meter_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the meter. ACTIVE: in service and collecting readings. INACTIVE: temporarily not in use. RETIRED: permanently decommissioned. SUSPENDED: readings paused (e.g., asset out of service). PENDING_CALIBRATION: due for or undergoing calibration.']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the meter per IBM Maximo meter type taxonomy. CONTINUOUS: accumulating counter that never resets (e.g., run-hours, cycle counts, flow totalizer). GAUGE: point-in-time measurement that can go up or down (e.g., PSI, vibration amplitude, temperature). CHARACTERISTIC: qualitative or categorical observation (e.g., condition rating, oil color).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model or part number for the meter instrument. Used for procurement, spare parts identification, and technical documentation lookup.']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the meter must next be calibrated to maintain measurement accuracy and regulatory compliance. Drives calibration work order generation in IBM Maximo.']
  pi_tag_name varchar [note: 'The OSIsoft PI Historian tag identifier associated with this meter for automated SCADA data ingestion. Enables direct linkage between the CMMS meter record and the PI Historian time-series data stream. Null if the meter is not integrated with PI Historian.']
  pm_trigger_interval decimal(18,4) [note: 'The recurring interval of meter units between successive PM work order generations. For example, every 500 run-hours or every 1000 valve cycles. Works in conjunction with pm_trigger_threshold for ongoing PM scheduling.']
  pm_trigger_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The meter reading value at which a Preventive Maintenance (PM) work order is automatically generated. For example, a pump PM triggered every 2000 run-hours. Core field enabling meter-based PM scheduling in IBM Maximo.']
  previous_reading decimal(18,4) [note: 'The meter reading value recorded immediately prior to the current reading. Used to calculate consumption delta between readings and to detect anomalous jumps or rollover events.']
  previous_reading_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the meter reading that preceded the current reading. Used in conjunction with previous_reading to compute usage rate and validate reading intervals.']
  reading_frequency varchar [note: 'Expected frequency at which meter readings are collected. CONTINUOUS: real-time SCADA/PI Historian streaming. HOURLY/DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY: scheduled batch collection. ON_DEMAND: readings taken only when required (e.g., during inspections or work orders).']
  reading_range_max decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum valid reading value for this meter as defined by the instrument\'s operating range or process design limits. Readings above this value are flagged as suspect or trigger alarms. For example, 150 PSI maximum for a distribution pressure transducer.']
  reading_range_min decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum valid reading value for this meter as defined by the instrument\'s operating range or process design limits. Readings below this value are flagged as suspect. For example, 0 PSI minimum for a pressure transducer.']
  reading_source varchar [note: 'The system or method by which the current meter reading was captured. SCADA: from Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition system. PI_HISTORIAN: from OSIsoft PI Historian automated feed. MANUAL: entered manually by a technician in IBM Maximo. AMI: from Advanced Metering Infrastructure. CMMS_IMPORT: batch import from CMMS. FIELD_DEVICE: direct PLC/RTU feed.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'The regulatory parameter code or identifier associated with this meter for compliance reporting submissions, e.g., EPA DMR parameter code, NPDES permit parameter identifier, or state drinking water program monitoring code. Null if is_regulatory_meter is False.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, technician observations, or special instructions related to this meter. For example, notes about known reading anomalies, temporary bypass conditions, or pending replacement. Sourced from IBM Maximo meter remarks field.']
  rollover_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum value the meter counter can reach before rolling over to zero. Applicable to CONTINUOUS meters with fixed-digit displays (e.g., 99999.9 hours). Used to correctly compute cumulative usage across rollover events. Null if the meter has no rollover limit.']
  sap_measuring_point_code varchar [note: 'The measuring point identifier from SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) module corresponding to this asset meter. Enables reconciliation between IBM Maximo CMMS and SAP PM for assets managed across both systems.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s serial number uniquely identifying the physical meter instrument. Used for warranty claims, calibration certificates, and asset tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset meter record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Tracks operational meters, counters, and their reading history for asset condition and usage monitoring — distinct from customer billing meters (owned by the metering domain). Examples include pump run-hours, motor starts, valve cycle counts, flow totalizers (MGD), pressure transducers (PSI), and vibration sensors. Captures meter type, unit of measure, current reading, rollover value, calibration status, and historical reading records (reading date/time, value, source — manual or SCADA/PI Historian automated, inspector ID, delta from previous reading). Feeds meter-based PM triggers, tracks asset utilization trends, and supports predictive maintenance analytics. Integrates with OSIsoft PI Historian.'
}

Table asset.warranty {
  warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each asset warranty record in the enterprise asset management system. Primary key for the asset_warranty data product.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical infrastructure asset (e.g., pump, valve, pipe, electrical panel, vehicle, WTP/WWTP equipment) covered by this warranty record.']
  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_contract. Business justification: Equipment warranties originate from construction contracts; warranty claim processing, vendor accountability, and performance bond claims require direct reference to the construction contract that spe']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Warranty record creation audit for procurement process tracking, warranty management program accountability, and data quality monitoring.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical infrastructure asset (e.g., pump, valve, pipe, electrical panel, vehicle, WTP/WWTP equipment) covered by this warranty record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or manufacturer who issued and is responsible for honoring this warranty. Used to route warranty claims to the correct counterparty.']
  claim_response_days varchar [note: 'Contractually committed number of calendar days within which the vendor must respond to a submitted warranty claim. Used for SLA monitoring and vendor performance management.']
  claim_submission_process varchar [note: 'Description of the required process for submitting a warranty claim, including required documentation, submission channel (e.g., vendor portal URL, email, phone), and any pre-authorization requirements. Ensures maintenance staff follow correct procedures.']
  claimed_amount_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all warranty claims submitted against this warranty record to date, expressed in USD. Used to track remaining claim capacity against the maximum claim value.']
  component_covered varchar [note: 'Specific sub-component or assembly of the asset covered by this warranty record (e.g., \'motor winding\', \'pump impeller\', \'control panel\', \'membrane module\'). A single asset may have multiple warranty records covering different components.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'The vendor-issued or internally-assigned contract number governing the warranty terms and conditions. Used for legal reference and claim dispute resolution.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'The date on which warranty coverage expires. Proactive monitoring of this date enables timely claim submission and maintenance routing through warranty channels before expiration, reducing OPEX.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of what is covered under this warranty, including specific components, failure modes, and exclusions. For example: \'Covers all mechanical and electrical components of the centrifugal pump excluding wear parts (seals, impellers) and damage caused by cavitation or improper operation.\'']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'The date on which warranty coverage becomes effective. Typically aligned with the asset installation date, commissioning date, or purchase date depending on warranty terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this warranty record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code applicable to all monetary values on this warranty record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency environments for international equipment procurement.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Total length of the warranty coverage period expressed in months. Derived from the vendor contract terms and used for planning and renewal tracking.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Explicit list of conditions, components, or failure modes NOT covered by this warranty. Critical for maintenance routing decisions to avoid voiding coverage through excluded repair activities.']
  expiry_alert_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the coverage end date at which an automated alert should be triggered to notify asset managers and maintenance planners of impending warranty expiration. Enables proactive claim submission and maintenance routing.']
  extended_warranty_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost paid to purchase an extended warranty beyond the standard manufacturer warranty period, expressed in USD. Captured for CAPEX/OPEX planning and ROI analysis of extended warranty investments.']
  installation_date date [note: 'The date the covered asset or component was physically installed and commissioned. Often used as the warranty coverage start trigger and is required for warranty claim substantiation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this warranty record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  max_claim_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum cumulative monetary value of claims that can be submitted under this warranty agreement, expressed in USD. Enables OPEX planning and ensures claims do not exceed contractual limits.']
  maximo_warranty_code varchar [note: 'The native warranty record identifier from IBM Maximo Asset Management (CMMS). Retained as a cross-reference key to enable bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CMMS source system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional warranty-related information not captured in structured fields, such as special claim conditions, vendor negotiation history, or maintenance routing instructions specific to this warranty.']
  performance_guarantee_metric varchar [note: 'For performance-type warranties, the specific operational metric that is guaranteed (e.g., \'pump flow rate >= 500 GPM\', \'motor efficiency >= 92%\', \'membrane rejection rate >= 99%\'). Null for parts/labor-only warranties.']
  performance_guarantee_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum or target numeric value of the performance guarantee metric that the asset must achieve under the warranty. For example, 500 (GPM) or 92 (%). Used to trigger performance warranty claims when asset output falls below the guaranteed level.']
  performance_guarantee_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the performance guarantee threshold value (e.g., GPM, MGD, PSI, %, NTU). Ensures correct interpretation of the numeric threshold in the context of the guaranteed metric.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'The date the asset or component was purchased. Some warranties are triggered from purchase date rather than installation date; retained for accurate coverage period calculation.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The SAP purchase order number associated with the original procurement of the asset or component. Links the warranty to the procurement transaction for audit and claim substantiation.']
  registration_date date [note: 'The date the warranty was formally registered with the vendor or manufacturer. Some warranties require registration within a specified period post-purchase to be valid; failure to register may void coverage.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty agreement includes an option to renew or extend coverage beyond the original expiration date. If True, renewal terms and pricing should be captured in the coverage_scope field.']
  requires_oem_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty terms require that all maintenance and repair work be performed exclusively by the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or an OEM-authorized service provider. If True, routing non-OEM technicians to this asset may void the warranty.']
  sap_pm_order_number varchar [note: 'The SAP PM maintenance order number associated with warranty-covered repair or maintenance work. Links warranty coverage to the SAP PM work order for cost recovery and OPEX tracking.']
  transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this warranty can be transferred to a new owner or operator in the event of asset sale, transfer, or organizational restructuring. Relevant for capital project asset handovers.']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the designated warranty claims contact at the vendor or manufacturer. Used for formal claim submission and written correspondence.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated warranty claims contact person at the vendor or manufacturer organization. Used to initiate and follow up on warranty claims.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the designated warranty claims contact at the vendor or manufacturer. Used to initiate and follow up on warranty claims.']
  warranty_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known warranty certificate or agreement number assigned by the vendor or manufacturer. Used for claim submission and correspondence with the warranty provider. Aligns with IBM Maximo warranty record identifier.']
  warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the warranty record. \'active\' indicates coverage is in force; \'expired\' indicates the coverage period has ended; \'claimed\' indicates a claim has been submitted and is under review; \'voided\' indicates the warranty was invalidated (e.g., unauthorized repair); \'pending_activation\' indicates the warranty has been registered but the coverage period has not yet started.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warranty coverage scope. \'parts\' covers replacement components only; \'labor\' covers repair labor costs; \'performance\' guarantees operational output levels (e.g., pump flow rate, motor efficiency); \'comprehensive\' covers both parts and labor; \'extended\' is a purchased extension beyond the original term; \'manufacturer\' is the standard OEM warranty.']

  Note: 'Tracks warranty coverage for assets and components, including warranty type (parts, labor, performance), vendor/manufacturer, warranty start and expiration dates, coverage terms, claim submission process, maximum claim value, and warranty status. Enables proactive warranty claim management before expiration and ensures maintenance work is routed through warranty channels when applicable, reducing OPEX.'
}

Table asset.depreciation_schedule {
  depreciation_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the depreciation schedule record. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the capital asset for which this depreciation schedule is maintained. Links to the enterprise asset registry.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Capitalized assets from CIP projects require depreciation schedules linked to originating project for GASB 34 compliance, rate case justification, and audit trail connecting capital expenditure author']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Depreciation schedules calculate periodic depreciation expense for fixed assets. Direct link supports automated depreciation posting, GASB 34 infrastructure reporting, and rate base calculations for u']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the capital asset for which this depreciation schedule is maintained. Links to the enterprise asset registry.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized from the depreciation start date through the end of the current fiscal period. Represents the contra-asset balance on the balance sheet. Expressed in USD.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capitalized cost of the asset at acquisition, including purchase price, installation, freight, and any directly attributable costs required to bring the asset to its intended use. Expressed in USD.']
  annual_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total depreciation expense allocated for the full fiscal year for this asset. For straight-line method: depreciable_base / useful_life_years. Expressed in USD.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA asset class code categorizing the type of capital asset for depreciation area assignment and useful life defaults (e.g., \'PUMP\', \'PIPE\', \'BLDG\', \'VEHICLE\', \'ELEC\', \'METER\').']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the capital asset being depreciated (e.g., \'WTP High-Service Pump Unit 3\', \'WWTP Aeration Blower\', \'12-inch Ductile Iron Main - Oak Street\').']
  asset_location_code varchar [note: 'Functional location or facility code where the asset is installed (e.g., WTP facility code, WWTP unit, pump station ID, distribution zone). Aligns with IBM Maximo functional location hierarchy and Esri ArcGIS GIS asset registry.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known fixed asset number as assigned in SAP FI/CO Fixed Asset Accounting (FI-AA) or IBM Maximo. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trails.']
  capex_project_number varchar [note: 'The Capital Improvement Program (CIP) project number associated with the asset\'s acquisition or construction. Links depreciation schedules to CAPEX planning and project close-out reporting.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'The date the asset was formally capitalized and transferred from construction-in-progress (CIP) to fixed assets in SAP FI-AA. May differ from depreciation start date for assets with a commissioning lag.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI company code representing the legal entity or municipal utility enterprise to which this depreciation schedule belongs. Supports multi-entity financial reporting and GASB fund accounting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center to which depreciation charges are posted, enabling OPEX/CAPEX allocation by operational unit (e.g., WTP operations, distribution, wastewater).']
  declining_balance_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage rate applied per period for declining balance or double declining balance depreciation methods. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2000 = 20%). Null for straight-line method.']
  depreciable_base decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount subject to depreciation, calculated as acquisition cost minus salvage value. This is the total amount to be allocated over the asset\'s useful life. Expressed in USD.']
  depreciation_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation area identifier representing the valuation purpose (e.g., \'01\' for book depreciation per GAAP/GASB, \'15\' for tax depreciation, \'20\' for cost accounting). Supports parallel valuation for regulatory and tax reporting.']
  depreciation_convention varchar [note: 'The period convention applied to determine depreciation in the first and last year of the asset\'s life (e.g., full_month, half_year, pro_rata). Aligns with SAP FI-AA period control settings.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'The projected or actual date on which depreciation ceases, corresponding to the end of the asset\'s useful life or full depreciation of the depreciable base.']
  depreciation_key varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation key code that defines the calculation method, period control, and base value for depreciation posting (e.g., \'LINR\' for straight-line, \'0000\' for no depreciation). Enables direct reconciliation with SAP Fixed Asset Accounting.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to allocate the depreciable base over the asset\'s useful life. Straight-line is most common for municipal water utility infrastructure per GASB. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: straight_line|declining_balance|double_declining_balance|units_of_production|sum_of_years_digits — promote to reference product]']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'The date on which depreciation begins for the asset, typically the in-service date or acquisition date per GAAP/GASB capitalization policy.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'The date the asset was retired, disposed of, or decommissioned. Triggers cessation of depreciation and recognition of any gain or loss on disposal in SAP FI-AA.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash or fair value received upon disposal or sale of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal: disposal_proceeds minus net_book_value at disposal date. Expressed in USD.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (month 1–12) within the fiscal year for which this depreciation record applies. Enables monthly depreciation posting and period-end close reconciliation in SAP FI/CO.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this depreciation schedule record applies. Supports annual depreciation posting, CAPEX/OPEX reporting, and GASB financial statement preparation.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Governmental fund code (e.g., Enterprise Fund, Capital Projects Fund) to which the asset and its depreciation are attributed, per GASB fund accounting requirements for municipal utilities.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI General Ledger account code for the depreciation expense posting (e.g., the depreciation expense account and accumulated depreciation contra-asset account). Enables financial statement mapping.']
  impairment_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative impairment charges recognized against the asset when its carrying value exceeds its recoverable amount, per GAAP ASC 360 impairment testing. Reduces net book value beyond scheduled depreciation. Expressed in USD.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'The date the asset was placed into operational service. This is the trigger date for depreciation commencement per GAAP/GASB policy and aligns with IBM Maximo asset activation.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date the depreciation schedule was last reviewed and validated by the asset accounting team, supporting annual audit requirements and GASB financial statement preparation.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the asset on the balance sheet, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Key metric for CAPEX/OPEX planning and asset renewal decisions. Expressed in USD.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording significant accounting judgments, useful life revision rationale, impairment triggers, regulatory directives, or other material information relevant to this depreciation schedule.']
  period_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The depreciation expense allocated for the specific fiscal period (month). Used for monthly journal entry posting to the depreciation expense account in SAP FI/CO. Expressed in USD.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this depreciation schedule record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this depreciation schedule record was last modified, supporting change tracking, audit trail, and period-end reconciliation.']
  remaining_useful_life_years decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated remaining useful life of the asset in years as of the last assessment date. Used for condition-based depreciation adjustments and asset renewal planning under CIP.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the asset accountant or finance officer who last reviewed and approved this depreciation schedule record, supporting audit trail and SOX/GASB compliance.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life, used to calculate the depreciable base. For most water utility infrastructure, this is often zero. Expressed in USD.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the depreciation schedule record. \'active\' = depreciation ongoing; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted (e.g., asset taken out of service); \'fully_depreciated\' = net book value at or near salvage value; \'disposed\' = asset retired; \'impaired\' = impairment recognized; \'draft\' = pending approval.']
  units_of_production_period decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual production units consumed during the current fiscal period, used to calculate period depreciation under the units-of-production method (e.g., gallons pumped, operating hours logged via SCADA/OSIsoft PI Historian).']
  units_of_production_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated production units over the asset\'s useful life, used as the denominator for units-of-production depreciation method (e.g., total gallons pumped, total operating hours). Applicable only when depreciation_method = \'units_of_production\'.']
  useful_life_revised_years varchar [note: 'Updated estimated useful life in years following a formal engineering reassessment or condition assessment. Replaces original useful_life_years for prospective depreciation calculation.']
  useful_life_revision_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset\'s estimated useful life was last revised, triggering a prospective change in depreciation calculation per GAAP ASC 250 change in accounting estimate.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years, as determined by engineering assessment and aligned with AWWA asset life guidelines and GASB/GAAP standards (e.g., 50 years for water mains, 20 years for pumps, 10 years for meters).']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure element linking the asset to its originating Capital Improvement Program (CIP) project for CAPEX tracking and project settlement.']

  Note: 'Financial depreciation schedule for capital assets, capturing acquisition cost, salvage value, useful life (years), depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance, units of production), annual depreciation amount, accumulated depreciation, net book value, and depreciation start date. Aligned with GAAP/GASB standards for municipal utility accounting. Integrates with SAP FI/CO Fixed Asset module and supports CAPEX/OPEX reporting.'
}

Table asset.acquisition {
  acquisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset acquisition record. Primary key for the asset acquisition data product.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset being acquired in the asset registry. Links this acquisition to the corresponding asset_registry record.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Capital Improvement Program project identifier if the asset was acquired as part of a capital project. Links to project management and budget tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Acquisitions are charged to departmental cost centers for capital budget tracking, CIP program management, and accountability. Supports budget-to-actual variance reporting for capital projects.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the Water Treatment Plant (WTP), Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP), pump station, or other facility where the asset is located.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Capital asset acquisitions post to specific GL accounts for capitalization, depreciation tracking, and GASB 34 infrastructure reporting. Critical for financial statement preparation and regulatory com']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset being acquired in the asset registry. Links this acquisition to the corresponding asset_registry record.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor in the procurement or Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was legally acquired or title transferred to the utility. Used for depreciation start date calculation and asset lifecycle tracking.']
  acquisition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the acquisition record in the workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion and asset registry creation.']
  acquisition_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was acquired into the utility\'s asset base. Determines accounting treatment and capitalization rules.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset acquisition was formally approved by the designated authority. Part of the audit trail.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or authority who approved the asset acquisition. Required for audit trail and governance.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Detailed categorization within the asset class such as pump, valve, pipe, tank, or vehicle. Supports asset hierarchy and reporting.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the asset type such as treatment equipment, distribution infrastructure, collection system, or vehicle. Used for depreciation schedule assignment.']
  capitalization_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum dollar amount threshold for capitalizing an asset as defined by the utility\'s accounting policy. Typically ranges from $1,000 to $5,000 for water utilities.']
  capitalization_threshold_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the acquisition cost meets the utility\'s capitalization threshold for fixed asset recording. Determines if asset is capitalized or expensed.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset was placed into service and became operational. Marks the start of depreciation for accounting purposes.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the construction contractor who built or installed the asset, applicable for construction acquisition type.']
  cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of acquiring the asset including purchase price, installation, freight, and other capitalizable costs. Basis for depreciation calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset acquisition record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost. Typically USD for United States water utilities.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the asset over its useful life. Straight-line is most common for water utility infrastructure.']
  functional_location varchar [note: 'SAP PM functional location code representing the hierarchical location of the asset within the utility\'s infrastructure. Supports Geographic Information System (GIS) integration.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funds used to finance the asset acquisition. Critical for Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) tracking and regulatory rate case filings.']
  grant_award_num varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the grant award that funded the acquisition, required for grant compliance reporting.']
  grant_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the federal, state, or local grant program that funded the acquisition, if applicable. Examples include EPA Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (WIFIA) or State Revolving Fund (SRF).']
  initial_condition_grade varchar [note: 'Condition assessment grade assigned at the time of acquisition. Baseline for future condition monitoring and asset health tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset acquisition record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  maximo_asset_num varchar [note: 'IBM Maximo Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) asset number assigned at acquisition. External system identifier for cross-reference.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, conditions, or details about the asset acquisition. Supports audit trail and historical context.']
  purchase_order_num varchar [note: 'Purchase order number from SAP Materials Management (MM) or procurement system authorizing the asset acquisition. Links to procurement transaction.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Used in depreciation calculation for certain methods.']
  sap_equipment_num varchar [note: 'SAP Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Plant Maintenance (PM) equipment number assigned at acquisition. External system identifier for cross-reference.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in years for depreciation calculation. Varies by asset class per Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and Governmental Accounting Standards Board (GASB) guidelines.']
  warranty_duration_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the warranty period in months. Calculated from warranty start and expiry dates for reporting purposes.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or contractor warranty period ends. Critical for maintenance planning and defect liability tracking.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or contractor warranty period begins for the acquired asset.']
  work_order_num varchar [note: 'Work order number from IBM Maximo or SAP PM for construction or installation activities related to the acquisition.']

  Note: 'Records the acquisition of new assets into the utility\'s asset base, capturing acquisition type (purchase, construction, donation, transfer), acquisition date, purchase order reference, vendor/contractor, acquisition cost, funding source (CAPEX, grant, developer contribution), commissioning date, and initial condition grade. Triggers creation of the asset_registry record and depreciation_schedule. Integrates with SAP MM purchase orders and SAP FI asset capitalization.'
}

Table asset.disposal {
  disposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset disposal record. Primary key for the asset disposal transaction.']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CIP project that triggered the asset replacement and disposal, if applicable. Links disposal to capital planning and renewal programs.']
  disposal_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset being disposed from the asset registry. Links to the physical infrastructure asset (pipe, pump, valve, equipment, vehicle) that is being retired or decommissioned.']
  disposal_replacement_asset_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new asset that replaced the disposed asset, if disposal reason was replacement. Links retired asset to its successor for lifecycle tracking.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Asset disposals generate gain/loss transactions posted to specific GL accounts for financial reporting. Required for accurate net book value adjustments and GASB compliance in financial statements.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset being disposed from the asset registry. Links to the physical infrastructure asset (pipe, pump, valve, equipment, vehicle) that is being retired or decommissioned.']
  replacement_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new asset that replaced the disposed asset, if disposal reason was replacement. Links retired asset to its successor for lifecycle tracking.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or contractor who performed the disposal, demolition, or removal services. Links to vendor master data in SAP MM or procurement system.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total depreciation expense accumulated on the asset from installation date through disposal date. Used in calculating book value and gain/loss on disposal.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the disposal transaction was formally approved by the authorizing approver. Required before disposal can proceed.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorizing approver who approved the asset disposal. Typically a senior manager, director, or capital planning committee member with authority over asset retirement decisions.']
  book_value_at_disposal decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net book value (original acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation) of the asset at the time of disposal. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal for financial reporting.']
  buyer_contact_info varchar [note: 'Contact information (phone, email, or address) for the buyer of the asset. Used for transfer documentation and warranty transfer. Confidential business contact data.']
  buyer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that purchased the asset, if disposal method was sale or auction. Null for other disposal methods.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the physical disposal activity was completed. May differ from disposal effective date if removal or demolition took multiple days.']
  cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred to dispose of the asset. Includes demolition costs, removal costs, environmental remediation, transportation, and disposal fees.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The cost center responsible for the asset at the time of disposal. Used for departmental cost allocation and budget tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this disposal record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this disposal record. Typically USD for U.S. water utilities.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'The date on which the asset was officially retired or disposed. This is the principal business event date that triggers SAP FI-AA asset retirement posting and updates the asset registry operational status to Retired.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the asset was disposed. Indicates whether the asset was sold, scrapped, transferred to another entity, donated, demolished, or salvaged for parts.
