[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salvage|demolition|sale|donation|transfer|scrap|auction — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposal_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the disposal transaction. Externally-known unique number used for tracking and audit purposes across SAP FI-AA and Maximo.']
  disposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disposal transaction. Tracks workflow from initiation through final completion and posting to financial systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|reversed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  environmental_clearance_date date [note: 'The date on which environmental clearance was obtained, if required. Null if no clearance was required.']
  environmental_clearance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting environmental clearance process, remediation actions taken, or hazardous material handling procedures followed during disposal.']
  environmental_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether environmental clearance or remediation was required before disposal. True for assets containing hazardous materials, lead pipes, asbestos insulation, or contaminated equipment.']
  gain_loss_on_disposal decimal(15,2) [note: 'The accounting gain or loss recognized on the disposal transaction, calculated as net disposal value minus book value at disposal. Positive values represent gains, negative values represent losses.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the employee or department that initiated the disposal request. Typically an asset manager, operations supervisor, or maintenance planner.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who last modified this disposal record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this disposal record was last updated. Audit trail for record modification.']
  location varchar [note: 'The physical location or facility where the asset was disposed, demolished, or removed from. May be a treatment plant, pump station, or field location.']
  maximo_disposal_code varchar [note: 'The disposal transaction identifier in IBM Maximo CMMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail between Maximo and SAP FI-AA.']
  net_disposal_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net financial result of the disposal transaction, calculated as disposal proceeds minus disposal costs. May be positive (gain) or negative (loss).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special handling instructions, lessons learned, or other relevant information about the disposal transaction.']
  proceeds decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount received from the disposal of the asset, if any. Represents sale price, salvage value, or auction proceeds. Zero for demolition or scrap with no recovery value.']
  retirement_reason varchar [note: 'The primary business or operational reason for retiring the asset. Captures whether disposal was due to end of useful life, failure, regulatory mandate, planned replacement, or other strategic decision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: end_of_life|catastrophic_failure|obsolescence|regulatory_requirement|replacement|consolidation|capacity_reduction|safety_hazard|environmental_compliance — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retirement_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context and details about the reason for asset retirement. May include failure analysis, condition assessment summary, or regulatory citation.']
  sap_asset_retirement_doc varchar [note: 'The SAP FI-AA asset retirement document number generated when the disposal transaction was posted to the financial system. Used for financial audit and reconciliation.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the asset retirement transaction was posted to SAP FI-AA general ledger. May differ from disposal date due to period-end closing rules.']
  transfer_to_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the entity (department, municipality, or organization) to which the asset was transferred, if disposal method was transfer. Null for other disposal methods.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'The Maximo work order number associated with the physical removal, demolition, or disposal activity. Links disposal transaction to field execution.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who created this disposal record. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Records the retirement, disposal, or decommissioning of assets from the utility\'s active inventory. Captures disposal date, disposal method (salvage, demolition, sale, transfer), disposal proceeds, reason for retirement (end of life, failure, regulatory, replacement), authorizing approver, and final book value at disposal. Triggers SAP FI-AA asset retirement posting and updates asset_registry operational status to Retired.'
}

Table asset.inspection_event {
  inspection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection event record. Primary key.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset or infrastructure component that was inspected.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water treatment plant (WTP), wastewater treatment plant (WWTP), or other facility where the inspection occurred.']
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized inspection checklist or form template used during the inspection.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who performed the inspection.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_sample. Business justification: Inspections trigger compliance and investigative sampling: tank inspections require bacteriological verification, cross-connection inspections require water quality testing, valve inspections may trig']
  premise_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Inspections of backflow prevention devices, service laterals, and customer-owned infrastructure occur at premises. Required for cross-connection control programs, LCRR compliance, and regulatory repor']
  primary_inspection_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved the inspection results.']
  project_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_permit. Business justification: Operational compliance inspections (NPDES, drinking water, dam safety) reference permits issued during project phase; regulatory reporting requires linking inspection findings to permit conditions, de']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the asset or infrastructure component that was inspected.']
  water_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_sample. Business justification: Regulatory inspections (sanitary surveys, LCRR system assessments, tank inspections) include water quality sampling as part of the inspection protocol. Real business need: compliance documentation lin']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order under which this inspection was performed, if applicable.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspection results were formally approved by the reviewing authority.']
  checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the inspection checklist used, ensuring traceability to specific regulatory or operational standards.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which identified deficiencies must be remediated, based on regulatory or operational requirements.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action work orders or follow-up activities are required as a result of this inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection event record was first created in the system.']
  critical_deficiency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any critical or high-severity deficiencies were identified that require immediate corrective action.']
  deficiencies_identified_count varchar [note: 'Total number of deficiencies, non-conformances, or issues identified during the inspection.']
  deficiency_summary varchar [note: 'Summary description of all deficiencies identified during the inspection, including severity and recommended actions.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time spent performing the inspection, measured in minutes.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection identified any environmental compliance issues or potential environmental impacts.']
  functional_location varchar [note: 'SAP PM functional location code representing the hierarchical location of the inspected asset within the facility.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical inspection was performed.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection activity was completed.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required frequency of this inspection type, measured in days, as mandated by regulatory or operational policy.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for inspector observations, comments, and detailed findings from the inspection.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection activity commenced.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_progress|completed|cancelled|failed|pending_review|approved — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection activity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine|regulatory|post_incident|pre_commissioning|condition_assessment|safety|environmental|quality|preventive|corrective — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_certification_num varchar [note: 'Professional certification or license number of the inspector, if required by regulatory authority.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector who conducted the inspection.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection event record was most recently updated.']
  maximo_inspection_num varchar [note: 'External inspection identifier from IBM Maximo CMMS system.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection of this asset or component.']
  pass_fail_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the inspection based on checklist criteria and regulatory requirements.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'External permit identifier issued by the regulatory authority, if this inspection is tied to permit compliance.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority that mandated or oversees this inspection (e.g., EPA, state primacy agency, OSHA).']
  regulatory_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection was mandated by a regulatory authority (EPA, state primacy agency, OSHA) or was voluntary/internal.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspection report was submitted to the regulatory authority.']
  report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection report has been submitted to the regulatory authority, if required.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety incident or near-miss occurred during the inspection activity.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of inspection, relevant for outdoor infrastructure inspections (pipes, valves, tanks).']

  Note: 'Operational record of a physical inspection performed on an asset or infrastructure component, including inspection type (routine, regulatory, post-incident, pre-commissioning), inspector identity, inspection date, checklist used, pass/fail outcome, deficiencies identified, corrective action required flag, and regulatory permit reference. Distinct from condition_assessment (which produces a scored condition grade) — inspection_event captures compliance-driven inspection activities required by EPA, state primacy agencies, and OSHA.'
}

Table asset.document {
  document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset document record. Primary key for the asset document registry.']
  asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical asset this document is associated with. Links to the asset registry.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Asset files contain certificates of analysis for treatment chemicals, pipe materials, coatings, and construction materials. Standard procurement documentation and regulatory compliance requirement for']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: As-built drawings, O&M manuals, warranty certificates, and technical specifications generated during capital projects must reference the originating CIP project for asset lifecycle documentation, regu']
  design_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.design_contract. Business justification: Design deliverables (calculations, specifications, technical memoranda, basis-of-design reports) are design contract work products; professional liability claims, design standard compliance verificati']
  document_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the internal staff member who approved the document.']
  document_author_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the internal staff member who authored the document, if applicable.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the water treatment plant (WTP), wastewater treatment plant (WWTP), or other facility this document pertains to.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory permit this document is associated with, if applicable.']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical asset this document is associated with. Links to the asset registry for equipment, pipes, pumps, valves, and other infrastructure.']
  related_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a work order that generated or is associated with this document, such as inspection reports or maintenance records.']
  superseded_by_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer document that replaces this document. Maintains document version lineage and ensures users access the current revision.']
  superseded_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version or document that this document replaces or supersedes.']
  test_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.test_result. Business justification: Asset documentation packages include laboratory test results for commissioning reports, material certifications, water quality compliance records, and coating/lining verification. Required for regulat']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier who provided the document, applicable for vendor-supplied documentation such as manuals and specifications.']
  access_restriction varchar [note: 'Description of who may access the document. Defines user roles, departments, or security clearances required for document access.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the document received official approval for release and use.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the document for use. Establishes document authority and accountability.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the document was formally approved. Marks the transition to active status.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that created the document. Identifies the document originator for accountability.']
  author_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or company that produced the document. Relevant for vendor-supplied documentation and external engineering firms.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Industry or regulatory standard that the document complies with. Examples include AWWA standards, ISO 55001, SDWA, CWA, or OSHA regulations.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the document content. Determines access controls and distribution restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage.']
  destruction_eligible_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the document becomes eligible for destruction based on retention policies.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date when the document is eligible for destruction or archival per retention policy. Supports records lifecycle management.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the document by business function. Supports document organization and retrieval.']
  document_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the document content, scope, and purpose. Supports document search and classification.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the document. Used for tracking and retrieval in document management systems and regulatory audits.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document. Tracks document progression through review, approval, and archival processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|active|superseded|archived|obsolete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document type. Categorizes documents by their purpose and content to support ISO 55001 documentation requirements and regulatory audit readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: as_built_drawing|om_manual|equipment_datasheet|commissioning_report|test_certificate|regulatory_permit|warranty_document|inspection_report|maintenance_procedure|safety_data_sheet — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the document becomes effective and applicable for use. Critical for regulatory permits and compliance documentation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the document expires or is no longer valid. Applicable to permits, certifications, and time-limited approvals.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the document. Identifies the software and tools required to view and edit the document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|dwg|dxf|docx|xlsx|jpg|png|tif — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the digital file as stored in the document management system. Includes file extension for format identification.']
  file_size_kb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the electronic document file measured in kilobytes.']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the document file in megabytes. Used for storage management and download time estimation.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the associated GIS feature in Esri ArcGIS. Links documents to spatial assets such as pipes, valves, and facilities.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the document was officially issued or published for use.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags for document search and classification. Enhances document discoverability in the document management system.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the primary language of the document. Supports multilingual document management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to the document metadata.']
  maximo_document_code varchar [note: 'Document identifier in IBM Maximo CMMS. Provides traceability to the source system for asset management integration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, instructions, or context about the document. Provides supplementary information not captured in other fields.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency that requires or issued the document. Examples include EPA, state drinking water programs, OSHA, or local health departments.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the regulatory requirement, permit, or compliance obligation that this document supports or fulfills.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document is required by regulatory agencies such as EPA, state primacy agencies, or OSHA. Critical for compliance audit readiness.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per regulatory or business policy requirements. Supports records management and compliance.']
  retention_trigger_date date [note: 'Date from which the retention period begins, typically the document effective date or superseded date.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the document to ensure continued accuracy and relevance.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Standard interval in months between scheduled reviews of the document.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who reviewed the document for technical accuracy and completeness. Part of document quality control process.']
  reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the document review was completed. Tracks document quality assurance timeline.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the document was created or last modified. Supports version tracking and audit trails.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier for the document. Tracks document changes and updates over time to maintain version control.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number in SAP ERP system. Links to SAP PM or MM modules for equipment documentation and procurement records.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic location where the document is stored, such as file path, cabinet number, or repository identifier.']
  storage_location_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the document in the document management system. Provides direct access to the digital document file.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the document. Provides human-readable identification of the document content and purpose.']
  url varchar [note: 'Web address or hyperlink to access the electronic document in the document management system or external repository.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for asset_document. '
}

Table asset.procurement_mapping {
  procurement_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the asset class to procurement category mapping record']
  asset_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the asset class being sourced']
  procurement_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the procurement category used for sourcing']
  updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the procurement professional who last updated this mapping']
  approved_vendor_count varchar [note: 'Number of approved vendors for this asset class within this procurement category. Used for supplier diversity analysis and sourcing risk assessment.']
  average_unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Benchmark average unit cost in USD for items of this asset class when procured through this category. Used for budgeting, cost variance analysis, and procurement performance tracking.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mapping was established in the system']
  last_procurement_date date [note: 'Most recent date when an item of this asset class was procured through this procurement category. Used to identify inactive mappings and refresh cost benchmarks.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this mapping was last modified, including updates to sourcing strategy, lead times, or cost benchmarks']
  mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this asset class to procurement category mapping. Active mappings are used in procurement workflows and materials planning.']
  preferred_sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Recommended procurement approach for this specific asset class when sourced through this procurement category. Drives purchasing workflow and approval routing.']
  standard_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical procurement lead time in days for items of this asset class when sourced through this procurement category. Used for materials planning and inventory reorder point calculations.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the strategic sourcing configuration between asset classes and procurement categories. It captures class-specific procurement strategies, lead times, cost benchmarks, and approved vendor counts used for materials planning, strategic sourcing decisions, and inventory optimization. Each record links one asset class to one procurement category with sourcing parameters that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, asset classes (pumps, valves, meters, SCADA equipment) are sourced through multiple procurement categories (mechanical parts, electrical components, instrumentation supplies), and each procurement category serves multiple asset classes. The procurement function actively manages these mappings to maintain class-specific sourcing strategies, lead time benchmarks, approved vendor pools, and cost standards for strategic sourcing, materials planning, and inventory optimization.'
}

Table asset.asset_sampling_point {
  asset_sampling_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for asset_sampling_point']
  quality_sampling_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the approved water quality sampling location where samples are collected']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical infrastructure asset being monitored (tank, pump station, treatment unit, pipe segment, valve, etc.)']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this asset-sampling point configuration. Active = in use; suspended = temporarily inactive; under_review = pending validation; decommissioned = permanently retired.']
  last_configuration_review_date date [note: 'Date when this asset-sampling point configuration was last reviewed and validated as part of monitoring plan audits or regulatory compliance reviews.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sampling at this specific asset-sampling point combination is mandated by regulatory permit conditions (NPDES, SDWA, state requirements) versus operational/voluntary monitoring.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when this asset-sampling point configuration was deactivated (e.g., asset decommissioned, sampling point relocated, monitoring requirements changed). Null if currently active.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when this asset-sampling point configuration became active and sampling began for this specific asset at this location. Used to track historical changes in monitoring configurations.']
  sample_collection_responsibility varchar [note: 'Identifies which team or entity is responsible for collecting samples at this asset-sampling point combination (may vary by asset even at the same sampling location based on access requirements or operational constraints).']
  sampling_access_notes varchar [note: 'Asset-specific access instructions, safety requirements, or logistical notes for sampling personnel collecting samples at this location for this particular asset (e.g., \'Requires confined space entry permit for tank interior sampling\', \'Access via north valve vault only\', \'Coordinate with operations for pump shutdown\').']
  sampling_frequency_override varchar [note: 'Asset-specific override of the default sampling frequency for this sampling point. Allows different sampling cadences for different assets monitored at the same location (e.g., critical assets sampled daily while standard assets sampled weekly at the same point).']
  sampling_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking (1=highest) for this asset-sampling point combination used to sequence sampling activities when multiple assets are monitored at the same location. Based on asset criticality, regulatory requirements, and operational risk.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational configuration between physical infrastructure assets and water quality sampling points. It captures the many-to-many relationship where a single asset (e.g., storage tank, pump station) can have multiple designated sampling locations (inlet, outlet, overflow, different depths), and a single sampling point can monitor water quality across multiple interconnected assets (e.g., a distribution system sampling point representing multiple mains). Each record defines the sampling configuration, frequency overrides, priority ranking, and access logistics specific to each asset-sampling point pair.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, the relationship between physical assets and sampling points is genuinely many-to-many. A single asset such as a storage tank has multiple designated sampling locations (inlet, outlet, overflow, different depths for stratification monitoring), and a single sampling point in the distribution system can monitor water quality across multiple interconnected assets (mains, valves, service connections). The utility actively manages these sampling configurations as part of regulatory compliance and operational monitoring programs, with each asset-sampling point pair having specific frequency overrides, priority rankings, and access logistics.'
}

Table asset.grant_funding {
  grant_funding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this asset-grant funding relationship record. Primary key.']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the grant providing funding for the asset']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical infrastructure asset being funded by the grant']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of grant funds allocated to this specific asset from this specific grant. Used for reimbursement tracking and audit reporting.']
  amount_reimbursed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount that has been reimbursed by the grantor for this asset under this grant. Used to calculate remaining funding balance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this asset-grant funding relationship with respect to grantor requirements. Values: compliant (meets all conditions), non_compliant (violation identified), under_review (audit in progress), pending_documentation (awaiting supporting docs), closed (funding complete and closed out).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset-grant funding record was created in the system.']
  eligible_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of costs incurred for this asset that have been determined eligible for reimbursement under this grant\'s allowable cost categories.']
  funding_end_date date [note: 'Date on which funding for this asset under this grant ends, typically aligned with the grant\'s period of performance end date or asset completion date.']
  funding_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset became eligible for funding under this grant, typically aligned with the grant\'s period of performance start date or asset installation date.']
  grant_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the asset\'s total cost that is funded by this grant (e.g., 75.00 means 75% grant-funded, 25% utility match). Used for matching requirement calculations.']
  ineligible_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of costs incurred for this asset that have been determined ineligible for reimbursement under this grant (e.g., costs incurred outside period of performance, non-allowable cost categories).']
  last_drawdown_request_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent drawdown request submitted to the grantor for reimbursement of costs associated with this asset under this grant.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset-grant funding record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special conditions, compliance issues, or other relevant information about this asset-grant funding relationship.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the capital project for this asset under this grant. Used to track project milestones and align with grant drawdown schedules.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the funding relationship between physical infrastructure assets and grants. It captures the allocation of grant funds to specific assets, tracking the funding amount, percentage, compliance status, and funding period for each asset-grant combination. Each record links one asset to one grant with attributes that exist only in the context of this funding relationship, supporting audit trails, reimbursement tracking, and grantor compliance reporting.. Existence Justification: In water utility capital finance, individual infrastructure assets (treatment plant upgrades, main replacements, pump stations) are routinely funded through multiple concurrent grants (DWSRF, CWSRF, EPA WIFIA, state programs, ARPA), and each grant program funds multiple assets across capital improvement projects. The utility must track the specific funding amount, percentage, compliance status, and reimbursement details for each asset-grant combination to support grantor reporting, single audit requirements (SEFA schedules), and drawdown requests.'
}

Table asset.compliance_requirement {
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each asset-requirement compliance relationship record']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the physical infrastructure asset subject to the regulatory requirement']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory requirement that applies to the asset']
  compliance_method varchar [note: 'The specific method, control, or operational practice by which this asset achieves compliance with this requirement (e.g., \'continuous monitoring\', \'quarterly sampling\', \'treatment technique\', \'operational control\'). Varies by asset-requirement combination.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting compliance issues, corrective actions, assessment findings, or special considerations specific to this asset-requirement relationship.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this specific asset with respect to this specific regulatory requirement. Tracks whether the asset meets the requirement, is out of compliance, is pending assessment, or has been granted a variance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset-requirement compliance relationship record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory requirement became applicable to this specific asset. May differ from the requirement\'s general effective date based on asset installation date, facility type, or system size.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment performed for this asset against this specific regulatory requirement. Used to track assessment currency and trigger next assessment scheduling.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance assessment of this asset against this regulatory requirement. Calculated based on the requirement\'s monitoring frequency and the last assessment date.']
  responsible_person varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual responsible for ensuring this specific asset maintains compliance with this specific requirement. May differ from the general requirement owner based on asset location or operational area.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset-requirement compliance relationship record.']
  variance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the granted variance or exemption for this asset-requirement pair. Null if no variance is granted or if variance is permanent.']
  variance_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory variance, exemption, or waiver has been granted for this specific asset-requirement combination. True if variance is active, false otherwise.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between physical infrastructure assets and regulatory requirements. It captures the specific compliance obligations, assessment schedules, and compliance status for each asset-requirement pair. Each record links one asset to one regulatory requirement with attributes that track compliance method, assessment dates, variance status, and compliance state specific to that asset-requirement combination.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, a single physical asset (e.g., a chlorine contact basin) must comply with multiple overlapping regulatory requirements from different regulatory programs (SDWA Surface Water Treatment Rule for CT requirements, state disinfection rules, EPA DBP rules for chlorine residual limits, NPDES for discharge). Conversely, a single regulatory requirement (e.g., SDWA Lead and Copper Rule) applies to multiple different asset types across the utility (service lines, treatment plant corrosion control systems, distribution system storage tanks, sampling stations). The compliance relationship itself carries operational data including compliance status, assessment schedules, compliance methods, and variance status that are specific to each asset-requirement pair.'
}

Table asset.inspection_checklist {
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inspection_checklist']
  parent_inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection_checklist (parent_inspection_checklist_id)']
  applicable_asset_category varchar [note: 'Asset category for which this checklist is intended.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the checklist.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the person who authored the checklist.']
  checklist_type varchar [note: 'Category of the checklist indicating its primary focus.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the checklist record was created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and scope of the checklist.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the checklist becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the checklist is no longer valid (nullable).']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time required to complete the checklist, in minutes.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric interval defining how often the inspection should occur.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the inspection frequency interval.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist must be performed for the asset.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent modification.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the checklist was last applied to an inspection.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the checklist used to identify it in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the checklist.']
  number_of_items varchar [note: 'Total count of individual inspection items in the checklist.']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulation that mandates this checklist.']
  required_by_regulation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist is mandated by regulation.']
  reviewer varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reviewed and approved the checklist.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the checklist.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the checklist record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the checklist, e.g., v1.0.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for inspection_checklist. Referenced by inspection_checklist_id.'
}

Project closeout requires documenting new sampling points per regulatory permit conditi']

  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent facility (WTP, WWTP, pump station, reservoir) where this sampling point is located, if applicable.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Permanent sampling points (stations, buildings, infrastructure) are capital assets requiring depreciation and inclusion in utility rate base for rate case proceedings—essential for capital asset track']
  registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific physical asset (pipe segment, valve, tank, treatment unit) at which sampling occurs, if applicable. Links to asset registry for maintenance correlation.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_address. Business justification: Sampling points are physically located at customer service addresses for distribution system monitoring, lead/copper compliance sampling, and RTCR investigations. SDWA regulatory requirement for site-']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_territory. Business justification: Sampling points are geographically located within service territories for regulatory zone compliance reporting, CCR preparation, and operational monitoring jurisdiction. Water utilities organize sampl']
  water_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.water_system. Business justification: A sampling point belongs to a single water system; linking provides hierarchy for reporting and compliance tracking.']
  access_type varchar [note: 'Classification of physical access requirements for sampling personnel. Public = open access; restricted = requires authorization; private property = requires owner permission; confined space = requires confined space entry procedures; remote = difficult to reach, may require special equipment.']
  ccr_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether water quality results from this sampling point must be included in the annual Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) to customers. True = included in CCR; False = not included.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, historical context, or operational observations about this sampling point that do not fit other structured fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this sampling point record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'The date when this sampling point was permanently retired from service. Null if still active or temporarily suspended.']
  dma_code varchar [note: 'The District Metered Area (DMA) code to which this sampling point belongs, used for Non-Revenue Water (NRW) analysis and pressure zone management.']
  dmr_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether effluent quality results from this sampling point must be included in monthly Discharge Monitoring Reports (DMR) submitted to EPA or state agencies. True = included in DMR; False = not included.']
  elevation_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elevation of the sampling point above sea level in feet. Used for hydraulic gradient analysis and pressure zone validation.']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'The typical or design flow rate in gallons per minute (GPM) at this sampling location. Used for composite sampling calculations and hydraulic modeling correlation.']
  gis_feature_code varchar [note: 'The unique feature identifier in the Esri ArcGIS or other GIS system that represents this sampling point. Used for spatial analysis, network tracing, and map visualization.']
  installation_date date [note: 'The date when this sampling point was first established and became operational for water quality monitoring.']
  last_sample_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which a sample was collected from this sampling point. Used to track compliance with sampling schedules and identify overdue sampling locations.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the sampling point location for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sampling point by its position in the water system lifecycle. Entry point = water entering distribution; distribution system = points within the network; source water = raw intake; WTP/WWTP process = treatment plant intermediate stages; effluent discharge = final discharge point; customer tap = end-user location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry_point|distribution_system|source_water|wtp_process|wwtp_process|effluent_discharge|customer_tap — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the sampling point location for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the person who last modified this sampling point record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this sampling point record was last updated or modified.']
  next_scheduled_sample_date date [note: 'The next planned date for sample collection at this location based on the regulatory or operational sampling schedule.']
  pressure_zone varchar [note: 'The hydraulic pressure zone within the distribution network where this sampling point is located. Used for hydraulic modeling and water quality correlation analysis.']
  primary_contaminant_group varchar [note: 'The primary category of contaminants monitored at this sampling point (e.g., \'Microbiological\', \'Disinfection Byproducts\', \'Inorganic Chemicals\', \'Organic Chemicals\', \'Radionuclides\', \'Nutrients\', \'Metals\'). Used to organize sampling schedules and laboratory workflows.']
  regulatory_zone varchar [note: 'The regulatory monitoring zone or district to which this sampling point is assigned for compliance reporting purposes. Used to aggregate results for Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) and Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR).']
  residence_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The estimated water residence time in hours from the treatment plant to this sampling point in the distribution system. Critical for disinfection Contact Time (CT) calculations and Disinfection Byproduct (DBP) formation analysis.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The internal department or division responsible for sampling at this location (e.g., \'Water Quality Lab\', \'Distribution Operations\', \'Wastewater Operations\', \'Compliance\').']
  safety_notes varchar [note: 'Safety considerations and hazards specific to this sampling location (e.g., confined space, traffic hazards, chemical exposure, biological hazards, electrical equipment).']
  sample_collection_method varchar [note: 'The method by which samples are collected at this point. Grab = single point-in-time sample; composite = time- or flow-weighted composite over a period; continuous monitor = automated real-time sensor; passive sampler = diffusion-based accumulation.']
  sampler_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary field technician or automated sampler equipment responsible for routine sample collection at this location. Used for quality assurance and chain-of-custody tracking.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'The regulatory or operational frequency at which samples must be collected from this location. Continuous = real-time monitoring (e.g., SCADA); event-based = triggered by specific conditions (e.g., storm events, process upsets). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|event_based — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sampling_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed field instructions for sample collection at this location, including access directions, safety precautions, flushing procedures, bottle types, preservation requirements, and any site-specific protocols.']
  sampling_point_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the sampling location, used in regulatory reporting and laboratory requisitions. Typically follows EPA or state-specific coding conventions.']
  sampling_point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the sampling location (e.g., \'Main Street Entry Point\', \'WTP Clearwell Outlet\', \'WWTP Final Effluent\').']
  sampling_point_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sampling point. Active = in regular use; inactive = not currently sampled but may be reactivated; temporarily suspended = short-term pause (e.g., construction); decommissioned = permanently retired.']
  scada_tag varchar [note: 'The SCADA system tag or point identifier for continuous monitoring instruments at this sampling location. Used to link laboratory results with real-time process data from OSIsoft PI Historian or similar systems.']
  treatment_stage varchar [note: 'For WTP or WWTP process sampling points, the specific treatment stage being monitored (e.g., \'Raw Intake\', \'Coagulation\', \'Sedimentation\', \'Filtration\', \'Disinfection\', \'Clearwell\', \'Primary Treatment\', \'Secondary Treatment\', \'Tertiary Treatment\', \'UV Disinfection\', \'Reverse Osmosis\'). Null for distribution or source sampling points.']
  water_source_type varchar [note: 'The type of water source from which this sampling point draws or monitors. Surface water = rivers, lakes, reservoirs; groundwater = wells, aquifers; groundwater under influence = groundwater influenced by surface water; blended = mix of sources; purchased = water bought from another utility; recycled = reclaimed wastewater.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the person who created this sampling point record.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all approved water quality sampling locations across the utility\'s infrastructure including distribution system sites, source water intakes, WTP/WWTP process points, and wastewater effluent discharge outfalls. Captures location type (entry point, distribution, source, effluent, customer tap), GIS coordinates, regulatory monitoring zone classification, DMA assignment, pressure zone, LCRR tier classification for tap sites, associated permit or CCR reporting requirements, and activation/deactivation status. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for where samples are collected and links to sampling_schedule for monitoring requirements.'
}

  sampling_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sampling schedule record. Primary key.']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sampling schedules track annual_budget_allocation and cost_per_sample; linking to cost_center enables budget variance analysis and cost allocation for regulatory monitoring programs—essential for comp']
  monitoring_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where samples are collected (e.g., treatment plant, distribution point, discharge point).']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Sampling schedules operationalize compliance obligations (permit monitoring requirements, consent order milestones). This link connects operational monitoring plans to formal compliance commitments, e']
  quality_sampling_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where samples are collected (e.g., treatment plant, distribution point, discharge point).']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Sampling schedules are driven by specific regulatory requirements (SDWA monitoring frequencies, RTCR rules, LCR sampling). This link documents the regulatory basis for each monitoring schedule, essent']
  responsible_crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crew or team responsible for collecting samples under this schedule.']
  sampling_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.sampling_plan. Business justification: Quality\'s sampling schedules implement laboratory\'s approved sampling plans. Compliance monitoring requires linking schedule execution to approved plans for UCMR, RTCR, LCR, and NPDES permit complianc']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory responsible for analyzing samples collected under this schedule (internal lab or contracted external lab).']
  annual_budget_allocation decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated in USD for this sampling schedule for the fiscal year.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this sampling schedule (e.g., Compliance Manager, Lab Director).']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this sampling schedule was formally approved for execution.']
  compliance_deadline_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which all samples for this schedule must be collected and analyzed to avoid violation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this schedule: compliant (all samples collected on time), at risk (approaching deadline or missed samples), non-compliant (violation occurred), pending review (awaiting regulatory determination).']
  cost_per_sample decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated or contracted cost in USD for each sample collection and analysis event under this schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sampling schedule record was first created in the system.']
  holding_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable time in hours between sample collection and analysis, per regulatory or method requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sampling schedule record was last updated.']
  last_sample_collected_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent sample was collected under this schedule. Used to track compliance gaps.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this record.']
  monitoring_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the monitoring period covered by this schedule. Null for ongoing/perpetual schedules.']
  monitoring_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the monitoring period covered by this schedule.']
  next_scheduled_sample_date date [note: 'Date when the next sample is scheduled to be collected under this schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or operational notes related to this sampling schedule.']
  notification_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance that field crews and labs should be notified before scheduled sampling events.']
  preservation_method varchar [note: 'Required preservation technique for samples (e.g., \'refrigerate to 4°C\', \'acidify to pH<2 with HNO3\', \'add sodium thiosulfate\', \'no preservation required\').']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of this schedule: critical (regulatory mandate with enforcement risk), high (compliance-sensitive), medium (operational importance), low (discretionary monitoring).']
  regulatory_rule varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory rule or permit condition driving this schedule (e.g., \'LCRR\', \'DBP Stage 2 Rule\', \'NPDES Permit CA0012345\', \'PFAS Monitoring Advisory\', \'SDWA Coliform Rule\').']
  reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of the reporting obligation associated with this schedule (e.g., \'Monthly Operating Report (MOR)\', \'Discharge Monitoring Report (DMR)\', \'Consumer Confidence Report (CCR)\', \'quarterly summary to state primacy agency\').']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Type of sample to be collected: grab (single point-in-time), composite (time- or flow-weighted over period), continuous (automated real-time), or integrated (multiple grabs combined).']
  sample_volume_ml decimal(10,2) [note: 'Required sample volume in milliliters for each collection event.']
  samples_collected_ytd varchar [note: 'Count of samples collected under this schedule in the current calendar or fiscal year.']
  samples_per_period varchar [note: 'Number of samples required per monitoring period (e.g., 4 samples per month for DBP monitoring in a large system).']
  samples_required_ytd varchar [note: 'Count of samples required to be collected under this schedule by this point in the calendar or fiscal year.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of sample collection: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, semiannual, annual, on-demand (event-driven), or continuous (automated monitoring). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|semiannual|annual|on_demand|continuous — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Standard method or protocol for sample collection (e.g., \'EPA Method 1694\', \'Standard Methods 9060A\', \'grab sample\', \'composite 24-hour\').']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or identifier for the sampling schedule (e.g., \'Q1 2024 DBP Monitoring - Plant A\').']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sampling schedule: active (in effect), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (monitoring period ended), cancelled (no longer required), pending approval (awaiting regulatory or management sign-off), or expired (past end date).']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sampling schedule purpose: regulatory (mandated by permit/rule), operational (internal monitoring), investigational (response to incident), special study (research/optimization), compliance verification, or routine.']
  seasonal_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule has seasonal variations in frequency or parameters (e.g., increased DBP monitoring in summer months).']
  violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule has triggered a monitoring or reporting violation.']

  Note: 'Defines the regulatory and operational sampling schedules for each monitoring location and contaminant group. Captures required sampling frequency (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual), applicable rule (LCRR, DBP Stage 2, PFAS, NPDES), monitoring period start/end dates, responsible lab or field crew, and schedule status. Drives compliance calendar and ensures no monitoring gaps that could trigger violations.'
}
