Table service.sla_definition {
  sla_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service level agreement definition record.']
  billing_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: Service level agreements define performance commitments that may be tied to premium rate schedules (guaranteed pressure, priority response). Tiered service pricing requires linking SLA definitions to ']
  service_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_class. Business justification: Service level agreements vary by customer service class, with different response time targets and compliance requirements for residential vs. commercial vs. industrial customers. The service_class STR']
  service_offering_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service offering or service class to which this SLA applies.']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.tariff. Business justification: SLA definitions are documented in tariff filings and are subject to regulatory approval. The tariff_reference STRING attribute should be normalized to a FK to tariff, allowing JOIN to retrieve tariff_']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory body or internal authority that approved this SLA definition, such as Public Utilities Commission, State Primacy Agency, or internal executive committee.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA definition was approved by the Public Utilities Commission or internal governance authority.']
  compliance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required percentage of time or occurrences that the SLA target must be met to maintain compliance, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.5% for high-availability commitments).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA definition record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA definition expires or is superseded by a new version. Null indicates the SLA is currently active with no defined end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA definition becomes effective and enforceable, typically aligned with rate case approval or tariff filing effective date.']
  escalation_procedure varchar [note: 'Defined escalation path or procedure to be followed when the SLA is breached, including management notification, regulatory reporting, and customer communication steps.']
  exclusion_conditions varchar [note: 'Conditions or circumstances under which the SLA does not apply or is temporarily suspended, such as force majeure events, natural disasters, regulatory-mandated shutdowns, or customer-requested service interruptions.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic area or service territory to which this SLA applies: system-wide across all service areas, specific district, pressure zone, District Metered Area (DMA), or specific customer location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA definition record was last updated or modified.']
  maximum_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the SLA metric, above which the SLA is considered breached. Applicable for metrics with upper bounds such as Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL), turbidity (NTU), or response time.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the SLA metric is measured or evaluated: continuous monitoring via SCADA, periodic sampling, or event-based measurement triggered by specific conditions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|event_based — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Specific metric or parameter being measured for this SLA, such as \'Outage Response Time\', \'Service Restoration Time\', \'Minimum Water Pressure (PSI)\', \'Maximum Turbidity (NTU)\', \'pH Range\', \'Maximum Contaminant Level (MCL)\', or \'Notification Time\'.']
  minimum_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the SLA metric, below which the SLA is considered breached. Applicable for metrics with lower bounds such as minimum water pressure (PSI) or minimum pH.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or clarifications regarding the SLA definition, including historical context, implementation guidance, or special considerations.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Communication channels required for customer or regulatory notification, such as email, SMS, phone call, postal mail, public notice, website posting, or media announcement. Multiple methods may be specified.']
  notification_requirement varchar [note: 'Timeframe within which customers or regulatory agencies must be notified of an SLA breach or service issue, such as immediate notification for water quality violations or 24-hour notice for planned outages.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the penalty, credit, or rebate applied per SLA breach, expressed in the utility\'s operating currency.']
  penalty_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the penalty or credit: fixed amount per breach, per occurrence, per hour of non-compliance, per day, percentage of customer bill, or tiered based on severity or duration.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty or consequence applied when the SLA is breached: customer credit, service rebate, regulatory fine, escalation to management, or no penalty.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Legal or regulatory framework that mandates or informs this SLA commitment, such as Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA), state primacy agency rules, Public Utilities Commission orders, or American Water Works Association (AWWA) standards.']
  reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Frequency and type of reporting required for SLA compliance monitoring, such as monthly performance reports to the Public Utilities Commission, quarterly customer reports, or annual Consumer Confidence Report (CCR) inclusion.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the SLA definition, used for external reference and regulatory tariff filings.']
  sla_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the SLA commitment, including the business purpose, customer benefit, and operational context.']
  sla_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA definition: active and enforceable, inactive, pending regulatory approval, superseded by a newer version, or temporarily suspended.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA definition, describing the commitment or guarantee.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Category of SLA commitment: response time for outage acknowledgment, restoration time for service recovery, water quality guarantee parameters, pressure guarantee thresholds, customer notification requirements, or system availability targets.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric target or threshold value for the SLA metric, representing the committed performance level or guarantee.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the SLA metric, such as \'minutes\', \'hours\', \'PSI (Pounds per Square Inch)\', \'NTU (Nephelometric Turbidity Units)\', \'mg/L\', \'pH units\', or \'percent\'.']

  Note: 'Defines the service level agreements applicable to each service offering or service class, including response time commitments for outages, restoration time targets, water quality guarantee parameters, pressure guarantee thresholds (PSI), and customer notification requirements. Captures SLA type, metric name, target value, measurement unit, regulatory basis (SDWA, state rules), and penalty or credit terms for non-compliance. Supports regulatory tariff compliance and customer service commitments.'
}

Table service.tariff {
  tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory tariff filing record. Primary key.']
  billing_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: Tariffs are regulatory filings that authorize specific rate schedules. Regulatory compliance and rate case tracking require linking the approved tariff document to the rate schedules it authorizes for']
  finance_rate_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rate_case. Business justification: Tariffs are the direct regulatory output of rate case proceedings. Every tariff must reference the rate case docket that established it. Core regulatory compliance relationship for PUC/PSC reporting a']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Tariffs must comply with regulatory requirements (rate structure mandates, affordability requirements, conservation pricing rules). Tariff filings reference compliance with state/federal mandates. Lin']
  service_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_class. Business justification: Tariff filings are class-specific, with separate rate schedules and regulatory approvals for each customer class. The customer_class STRING attribute should be normalized to a FK to service_class, all']
  superseded_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior tariff that this tariff replaces. Establishes the lineage of rate changes and enables historical rate analysis.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_territory. Business justification: Tariffs apply to specific service territories and franchise areas. Currently service_territory is a STRING field describing where the tariff applies. Should be FK to service_territory for referential ']
  adjustment_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tariff includes automatic adjustment clauses or riders that allow for rate changes without a full rate case. Common adjustment clauses include purchased water adjustment clauses (PWAC), infrastructure surcharges, and cost recovery mechanisms for specific capital programs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority formally approved the tariff filing. This date may differ from the effective date due to statutory notice periods or phased implementation.']
  approval_order_number varchar [note: 'The official order or docket number issued by the regulatory authority approving this tariff. This reference links the tariff to the formal regulatory decision and provides traceability to the rate case proceeding.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'The foundational rate component approved by the regulatory authority, typically expressed as a fixed monthly charge or per-unit volumetric rate. This is the core revenue component before adjustments, surcharges, or riders.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The standard billing cycle for customers under this tariff. Defines how often charges are calculated and invoices are issued.']
  conservation_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tariff incorporates conservation-oriented rate design features such as increasing block rates, seasonal pricing, or water budget-based rates intended to promote efficient water use.']
  cost_of_service_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost-of-service study or rate design analysis that supports the tariff rates. This study allocates utility costs to customer classes and justifies the rate structure to the regulatory authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tariff record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which tariff rates and charges are denominated. For U.S. water utilities, this is typically USD.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'The web address or document management system link to the official tariff filing document, including all schedules, exhibits, and supporting materials. Provides direct access to the complete regulatory record.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the tariff becomes binding and enforceable. Rates and terms specified in the tariff apply to all qualifying service from this date forward.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the tariff ceases to be in force, if applicable. Null for tariffs that remain active until superseded by a subsequent filing.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the tariff was officially filed with the Public Utilities Commission or state regulatory authority. This date triggers statutory review periods and notice requirements.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'The date of the public hearing or evidentiary proceeding held by the regulatory authority to review the tariff filing. Null if no hearing was required or held.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tariff record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  low_income_assistance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tariff includes provisions for low-income customer assistance programs, such as lifeline rates, bill discounts, or affordability programs mandated or approved by the regulatory authority.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,4) [note: 'The minimum monthly or periodic charge that applies regardless of consumption. Ensures cost recovery for fixed infrastructure and customer service costs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, clarifications, or special conditions related to the tariff. May include notes on implementation, transition provisions, or regulatory stipulations.']
  public_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which public notice of the tariff filing was provided to customers and stakeholders, as required by regulatory statute. Public notice periods allow for customer comment and intervention in rate proceedings.']
  rate_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net investment in utility plant and working capital upon which the utility is allowed to earn a return. Rate base is calculated as gross plant in service less accumulated depreciation plus working capital allowances.']
  rate_of_return_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The allowed rate of return on rate base approved by the regulatory authority. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0725 for 7.25%). This is the weighted average cost of capital (WACC) the utility is permitted to earn.']
  rate_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed rate schedule document or section number within the tariff filing that specifies the complete pricing structure, including block tiers, surcharges, and adjustments.']
  rate_structure_type varchar [note: 'The pricing methodology employed by the tariff. Flat rates charge a fixed amount regardless of usage, uniform rates charge a constant per-unit price, declining block rates decrease with higher usage tiers, increasing block rates increase with higher usage tiers to promote conservation, seasonal rates vary by time of year, and time-of-use rates vary by time of day or demand period.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The name of the Public Utilities Commission, state regulatory agency, or other governing body that has jurisdiction over this tariff filing. Examples include State Public Service Commission, Public Utilities Commission, or Department of Public Utilities.']
  revenue_requirement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total annual revenue requirement approved by the regulatory authority for the utility under this tariff. This is the aggregate revenue the utility is authorized to collect to cover operating expenses, capital costs, and allowed return on investment.']
  service_rules_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the service rules, terms, and conditions document or section number within the tariff filing that governs service delivery, customer obligations, disconnection policies, and dispute resolution procedures.']
  tariff_description varchar [note: 'A detailed narrative description of the tariff, including its purpose, scope, applicability, and key terms. This field captures the regulatory intent and business context of the tariff.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the tariff as it appears in regulatory filings and customer communications.']
  tariff_number varchar [note: 'The official regulatory tariff number assigned by the Public Utilities Commission or state regulatory authority. This is the externally-known identifier used in all regulatory filings and rate case proceedings.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tariff filing. Draft indicates internal preparation, filed means submitted to the regulator, pending approval is under regulatory review, approved means accepted by the commission, active is currently in force, suspended is temporarily halted, superseded is replaced by a newer tariff, and withdrawn is retracted before approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|filed|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|superseded|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tariff_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tariff based on the customer segment and service arrangement it governs. General service tariffs apply to standard retail customers, special contracts cover negotiated agreements with large users, wholesale tariffs govern bulk water sales to other utilities, and industrial/municipal/residential/commercial designate specific customer classes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general_service|special_contract|wholesale|industrial|municipal|residential|commercial — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record of regulatory tariff filings and associated rate case proceedings with the Public Utilities Commission or state regulatory authority. Captures the full lifecycle from rate case filing (test year, requested revenue requirement, hearing dates, intervenor parties) through approval (final order, approved revenue requirement) to the effective tariff (tariff number, effective date, tariff type, incorporated rate schedules and service rules). This is the regulatory anchor for all rate and service term compliance, linking rate case proceedings to their resulting approved pricing structures. Supports financial planning, regulatory compliance, and rate modeling.'
}

Table service.service_rate_case {
  service_rate_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service_rate_case data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  service_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_class. Business justification: Rate case proceedings can be class-specific (e.g., residential rate case, industrial rate case) or apply to all classes. This FK allows rate cases to be scoped to specific customer service classes whe']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.territory. Business justification: Rate case proceedings can be territory-specific or system-wide. Water utilities often file rate cases for specific franchise areas or service territories. This FK allows rate cases to be scoped to the']

  Note: 'Tracks the lifecycle of regulatory rate case proceedings filed with the Public Utilities Commission or state regulatory authority to adjust water and wastewater service rates. Captures filing date, test year, requested revenue requirement, approved revenue requirement, rate case status, hearing dates, intervenor parties, final order date, and the resulting approved tariff reference. Supports financial planning, regulatory compliance, and rate modeling.'
}

Table service.special_contract {
  special_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the special contract record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_agreement. Business justification: Special contracts are negotiated agreements that extend or modify standard service agreements. Currently has customer_account_id but missing the service_agreement link. Special contracts should refere']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Special contracts (large industrial users, negotiated rates) require dedicated billing accounts with custom rate structures. Contract billing administration depends on linking the contract to the bill']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Large industrial or municipal customers under special contracts often have dedicated infrastructure projects (dedicated treatment capacity, private mains, interconnections). Contract compliance, capit']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the large industrial, municipal, or wholesale customer party under this special contract.']
  finance_rate_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rate_case. Business justification: Special contracts require rate case approval or tariff deviation justification. Links negotiated rates to regulatory proceedings for PUC compliance, cost-of-service justification, and audit trails. Re']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Special contracts (large industrial users) require dedicated fund accounting for revenue tracking, regulatory reporting, and rate case revenue requirement analysis. Essential for wholesale/industrial ']
  pretreatment_iup_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pretreatment_iup. Business justification: Special contracts for industrial users reference their IUP (Industrial User Permit). Contract terms (volume, discharge limits, pricing) must align with pretreatment permit conditions. Links billing, s']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments or modifications made to the original contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at the end of its term unless either party provides notice of termination.']
  base_rate_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Negotiated base rate per unit of water (typically per thousand gallons or per cubic meter) under the special contract.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the customer is billed under the special contract: monthly, quarterly, annual, or custom.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the signed contract document stored in the document management system.']
  contract_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the contract term expressed in months, used for renewal planning and rate case modeling.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the special contract for business reference and reporting.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the special contract, used in regulatory filings and customer correspondence.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was executed and signed by all parties.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the special contract indicating its operational state and regulatory approval status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|terminated|expired|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the special contract indicating the nature of the agreement: industrial user, wholesale, intergovernmental, municipal, bulk supply, or special rate.']
  contract_value_total decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total monetary value of the contract over its full term, used for financial planning and revenue forecasting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this special contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this contract. Typically USD for U.S. water utilities.']
  demand_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly or periodic demand charge assessed to reserve capacity for the customer, independent of actual consumption.']
  discharge_limit_bod_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable BOD concentration in wastewater discharge under the special contract, measured in mg/L.']
  discharge_limit_tss_mg_per_l decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable TSS concentration in wastewater discharge under the special contract, measured in mg/L.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the special contract terms expire or terminate. Nullable for open-ended contracts subject to renewal.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the special contract terms become binding and enforceable.']
  iup_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is required to maintain an Industrial User Permit (IUP) and comply with pretreatment requirements as a condition of the contract.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the contract terms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this special contract record was last updated in the system.']
  maximum_volume_limit_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum daily water volume (in MGD) that the utility agrees to supply under the contract, defining capacity allocation limits.']
  minimum_volume_commitment_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum daily water volume (in MGD) that the customer commits to purchase under the contract, used for capacity planning and revenue forecasting.']
  negotiated_rate_structure varchar [note: 'Type of rate structure negotiated in the special contract: flat rate, tiered volume, demand charge, time-of-use, seasonal, or custom.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate or modify the contract.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due under the contract terms.']
  penalty_for_over_consumption decimal(12,2) [note: 'Financial penalty or surcharge assessed if the customer exceeds the maximum volume limit, typically expressed as a per-unit overage charge.']
  penalty_for_under_consumption decimal(12,2) [note: 'Financial penalty assessed if the customer fails to meet the minimum volume commitment, typically expressed as a fixed amount or per-unit shortfall charge.']
  pretreatment_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of wastewater pretreatment requirements the customer must meet, including discharge limits for BOD, COD, TSS, TDS, pH, FOG, and other pollutants.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority approved the special contract for execution.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory approval from the Public Utilities Commission or other governing body for this special contract.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or docket number assigned by the regulatory authority for the special contract filing.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of contract renewal terms, including automatic renewal provisions, notice periods, and renegotiation conditions.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized representative who signed the contract on behalf of the customer.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position of the authorized representative who signed the contract on behalf of the customer.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum response time in hours guaranteed under the SLA for service requests or outage notifications.']
  sla_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Guaranteed service uptime percentage under the SLA, typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.50 for 99.5% uptime).']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any additional special conditions, covenants, or obligations unique to this contract, such as infrastructure contributions, service level guarantees, or operational constraints.']
  tariff_deviation_justification varchar [note: 'Business and regulatory justification for deviating from standard published tariff terms, required for regulatory approval.']

  Note: 'Records negotiated special service contracts with large industrial, municipal, or wholesale customers that deviate from standard tariff terms. Captures contract type (industrial user, wholesale, intergovernmental), negotiated rate terms, minimum volume commitments (MGD), demand charge structure, contract duration, renewal terms, special conditions (IUP compliance, pretreatment requirements), and regulatory approval status. Distinct from standard service agreements governed by published tariffs.'
}

Table service.conservation_program {
  conservation_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the water conservation program record. Primary key.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Conservation programs are funded by designated utility funds (often rate-funded conservation funds or restricted funds). Essential for budget tracking, expenditure authorization, and regulatory report']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.grant. Business justification: Many conservation programs are grant-funded (state/federal water efficiency grants, EPA WaterSense). Grant expenditure tracking required for compliance, drawdown requests, and single audit reporting. ']
  program_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Conservation programs in water utilities have designated employee program managers responsible for execution, budget, enrollment, and regulatory reporting. Links employee accountability to program per']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Conservation programs are often mandated by regulatory requirements (drought response orders, demand management mandates, water use efficiency standards). Program design, targets, and reporting tied t']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_territory. Business justification: Conservation programs are often territory-specific with territory-level water savings targets and regulatory mandates. Adding this link enables territory-based program management and reporting.']
  actual_participant_count varchar [note: 'Current count of customers or accounts enrolled and participating in the conservation program.']
  actual_water_savings_gallons varchar [note: 'Measured or estimated cumulative water savings achieved by the program to date, in gallons.']
  budget_expended_to_date decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount of program budget spent as of the last reporting period, in US dollars.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for customer inquiries and program communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for customer inquiries and program support.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created the conservation program record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the conservation program record was first created in the system.']
  customer_class_applicability varchar [note: 'Customer class segments eligible to participate in this conservation program.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed requirements and conditions that customers must meet to participate in the conservation program, including customer class, service type, meter size, and usage thresholds.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date when customer enrollment or application for the program closes. Null for open enrollment programs.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'Date when customer enrollment or application for the program opens.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funds supporting the conservation program, such as operating budget, capital improvement program, state grants, or federal funding.']
  incentive_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary value of the incentive provided per qualifying customer or installation, in US dollars.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial or non-financial incentive offered to customers participating in the program.']
  incentive_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for calculating and distributing the incentive to program participants.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the conservation program record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the conservation program record was last updated.']
  last_reported_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance report submitted for this conservation program.']
  marketing_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the conservation program to specific marketing and outreach campaigns for tracking effectiveness.']
  maximum_incentive_per_customer decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cap on the total incentive amount a single customer can receive under this program during the program period, in US dollars.']
  performance_metric_definition varchar [note: 'Description of the key performance indicators (KPIs) used to measure program success, such as gallons saved per dollar spent or participation rate.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'Target sector or application area for the conservation program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the conservation program, used for external reference and reporting.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the conservation program objectives, activities, and benefits to customers and the utility.']
  program_end_date date [note: 'Date when the conservation program concludes and no new participants are accepted. Null for ongoing programs.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the conservation program as presented to customers and in regulatory filings.']
  program_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to the conservation program administration and operations.']
  program_start_date date [note: 'Date when the conservation program becomes active and customers can begin participating.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the conservation program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the conservation program by delivery mechanism and approach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rebate|tiered_pricing|water_audit|drought_surcharge|education|restriction|incentive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  program_website_url varchar [note: 'Web address where customers can find detailed program information, eligibility requirements, and application forms.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the conservation program received regulatory approval from the governing authority.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Approval or permit number issued by the state Public Utilities Commission or environmental agency authorizing the program.']
  regulatory_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the conservation program is required by state or federal regulation (True) or is voluntary (False).']
  regulatory_mandate_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the specific regulation, statute, or executive order requiring or authorizing the conservation program.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which program performance and results are reported to regulatory agencies and utility management.']
  target_participant_count varchar [note: 'Goal for the number of customers or accounts participating in the conservation program.']
  target_water_savings_gallons varchar [note: 'Projected total water savings goal for the program over its duration, measured in gallons.']
  tariff_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the utility tariff schedule or rate case filing that includes this conservation program.']
  total_program_budget decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for the conservation program over its entire duration, in US dollars.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of customer programs including water conservation programs (rebates, tiered pricing incentives, water audits, drought surcharges), demand management programs, and customer affordability/assistance programs (lifeline rates, PIPP, bill discounts, emergency assistance). Captures program name, program type, eligibility criteria (including income thresholds for affordability programs), incentive or benefit structure, funding source, program period, target outcomes, enrollment tracking, and regulatory mandate reference. Supports AWWA water efficiency goals, state conservation mandates, and regulatory affordability requirements.'
}

Table service.affordability_plan {
  affordability_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the affordability plan. Primary key.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Low-income assistance programs are funded by designated funds (rate-funded assistance funds or restricted funds). Essential for subsidy accounting, budget tracking, and regulatory reporting of afforda']
  service_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_class. Business justification: Affordability plans apply to specific customer service classes. Currently customer_class_applicability is a STRING, but this should be a FK to service_class for referential integrity and to enable cla']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.territory. Business justification: Affordability and low-income assistance programs can be territory-specific, as different jurisdictions may have different regulatory mandates, funding sources, or eligibility criteria. This FK allows ']
  application_process_description varchar [note: 'Description of the process customers must follow to apply for enrollment in this affordability plan, including required documentation and submission channels.']
  auto_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether eligible customers are automatically enrolled in this affordability plan based on participation in other assistance programs (e.g., SNAP, Medicaid, LIHEAP). True if auto-enrollment is enabled, false if customers must apply.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address customers can use to request information about this affordability plan or submit application inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number customers can call for information about this affordability plan, application assistance, or enrollment status inquiries.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this affordability plan record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this affordability plan record was first created in the system.']
  current_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'Current number of customers actively enrolled in this affordability plan. Updated periodically to reflect enrollment changes.']
  discount_fixed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar credit amount applied to the customer\'s bill per billing cycle when the discount type is fixed amount. Null if discount type is not fixed amount.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the customer\'s water and/or wastewater bill when the discount type is percentage-based. Null if discount type is not percentage.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the affordability benefit is applied: percentage (percent off total bill), fixed amount (dollar credit per billing cycle), rate reduction (lower volumetric or base rate), bill cap (maximum bill amount), or usage credit (free gallons or cubic meters).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this affordability plan expires or is discontinued. Null if the plan has no scheduled end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this affordability plan becomes active and available for customer enrollment.']
  eligibility_income_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual household income amount (in local currency) that qualifies a customer for enrollment in this affordability plan. Typically expressed as a percentage of Federal Poverty Level (FPL) or Area Median Income (AMI).']
  eligibility_income_threshold_basis varchar [note: 'The reference standard used to determine income eligibility: federal poverty level (FPL percentage), area median income (AMI percentage), state median income (SMI percentage), or fixed amount (absolute dollar threshold).']
  eligibility_income_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the income threshold basis (e.g., 150% of FPL, 80% of AMI) used to determine customer eligibility for the affordability plan.']
  enrollment_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customers who can be enrolled in this affordability plan at any given time. Null if no enrollment cap applies.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of funding for the affordability plan: utility revenue (general operating funds), rate surcharge (dedicated customer charge), municipal subsidy (city/county budget), state grant, federal grant, private donation, or cross subsidy (higher-income customer rates). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: utility_revenue|rate_surcharge|municipal_subsidy|state_grant|federal_grant|private_donation|cross_subsidy — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this affordability plan record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this affordability plan record was last updated in the system.']
  maximum_benefit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total dollar benefit a customer can receive per billing cycle or per enrollment period under this affordability plan. Null if no cap applies.']
  maximum_benefit_duration_months varchar [note: 'Maximum number of months a customer can remain enrolled in this affordability plan before re-certification or plan expiration. Null if no duration limit applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this affordability plan, including program changes, temporary modifications, or administrative remarks.']
  partner_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of external assistance program(s) used for auto-enrollment eligibility verification (e.g., SNAP, Medicaid, LIHEAP, TANF). Null if auto-enrollment is not enabled.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the affordability plan, used for external reference and reporting.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the affordability plan, including objectives, benefits, and terms of assistance provided to eligible customers.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the affordability plan (e.g., Lifeline Rate Program, Low-Income Assistance Plan, Percentage of Income Payment Plan).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the affordability plan: active (accepting enrollments), inactive (not currently offered), suspended (temporarily paused), pending approval (awaiting regulatory approval), expired (past end date), or discontinued (permanently retired).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the affordability plan structure: lifeline rate (reduced base rate), percentage of income (PIPP - bill capped at income percentage), fixed discount (flat dollar or percentage reduction), tiered discount (graduated by income level), emergency assistance (one-time aid), or crisis voucher (short-term relief).']
  program_website_url varchar [note: 'Web address where customers can find detailed information about this affordability plan, download application forms, and access eligibility guidelines.']
  recertification_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required recertification events for customers enrolled in this affordability plan. Null if recertification is not required.']
  recertification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers must periodically recertify their eligibility to continue receiving benefits under this affordability plan. True if recertification is required, false otherwise.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority approved this affordability plan for implementation. Null if not applicable.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval or docket number issued by the regulatory authority authorizing this affordability plan. Null if not applicable.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or legislative authority that mandates or oversees this affordability plan (e.g., State Public Utilities Commission, City Council Ordinance). Null if plan is voluntary.']
  regulatory_mandate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this affordability plan is mandated by state or local regulation, or is a voluntary utility program. True if mandated, false if voluntary.']
  required_documentation varchar [note: 'List of documents customers must provide to verify eligibility for this affordability plan (e.g., proof of income, tax returns, benefit award letters, utility bills).']
  service_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies which utility services are covered by this affordability plan: water only, wastewater only, water and wastewater combined, stormwater, or all services.']
  waitlist_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waitlist is maintained for customers when enrollment capacity is reached. True if waitlist is enabled, false otherwise.']

  Note: 'Defines the low-income customer assistance and affordability programs available, including lifeline rates, percentage-of-income payment plans (PIPP), bill discount programs, and emergency assistance funds. Captures plan name, eligibility income thresholds, discount percentage or fixed credit amount, maximum benefit duration, funding source, regulatory mandate, and enrollment capacity. Supports equitable access to essential water services and regulatory affordability requirements.'
}

Table service.bulk_water_agreement {
  bulk_water_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bulk water supply agreement. Primary key for the bulk water agreement entity.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Bulk water agreements (wholesale customers, neighboring utilities) require billing accounts for invoicing contracted volumes. Wholesale billing operations depend on linking the agreement to the accoun']
  cip_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.cip_project. Business justification: Bulk water agreements (wholesale supply to neighboring utilities) often require dedicated infrastructure projects (interconnections, booster stations, dedicated pipelines). Contract performance monito']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Bulk water agreements require water withdrawal permits or inter-jurisdictional transfer permits. Utility tracks regulatory approval for wholesale water transactions, ensuring compliance with permitted']
  delivery_point_asset_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_registry. Business justification: Wholesale delivery points are physical assets (interconnection vaults, master meters, pressure control stations) requiring maintenance, inspection, and emergency response planning. Operations teams ne']
  dma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.dma. Business justification: Bulk water delivery points need DMA assignment for wholesale flow monitoring, NRW accounting (separating wholesale from retail losses), zone-level demand forecasting, and contracted_volume_mgd verific']
  finance_rate_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.rate_case. Business justification: Bulk water rates are established through rate case proceedings. Regulatory approval required for wholesale pricing. Links wholesale rate structure to rate case docket for PUC compliance and cost-of-se']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fund. Business justification: Bulk water sales revenue flows to specific utility funds. Essential for wholesale revenue accounting, interfund transfers, and GASB reporting. Water utilities track wholesale vs. retail revenue by fun']
  metering_meter_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the meter installed at the delivery point to measure bulk water volumes for billing and compliance purposes. Links to asset management system.']
  pressure_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.pressure_zone. Business justification: Bulk delivery points must reference pressure zones for hydraulic capacity verification, delivery_pressure_psi guarantee compliance, system impact analysis, and zone-level demand accounting. Critical f']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.procurement_contract. Business justification: Bulk water sale agreements to neighboring utilities often have associated procurement contracts for treatment chemicals, power, or infrastructure maintenance that are cost-allocated to the bulk water ']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_territory. Business justification: Bulk water agreements are delivered within specific service territories. The delivery_point_description and coordinates indicate a geographic location that should be tied to a service territory for fr']
  wtp_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to treatment.facility. Business justification: Wholesale water agreements specify which treatment facility supplies the bulk water - essential for capacity planning, contract compliance verification (volume/quality commitments), and cost allocatio']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the bulk water agreement, used in contracts, invoices, and regulatory filings.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bulk water agreement: draft (under negotiation), pending approval (awaiting regulatory or board approval), active (in force), suspended (temporarily halted), terminated (ended before expiration), or expired (reached end date).']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bulk water agreement based on the nature of service: wholesale supply (ongoing large-volume delivery), bulk purchase (one-time or periodic large purchases), emergency interconnection (backup supply during outages), seasonal supply (peak demand periods), treatment services (wastewater treatment for another utility), or wastewater discharge (acceptance of wastewater flows).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides termination notice. True if auto-renewal applies, False otherwise.']
  base_rate_per_thousand_gallons decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base volumetric rate charged per thousand gallons of water delivered or wastewater treated under the agreement. Null if pricing is not volumetric.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are issued to the counterparty for bulk water supply or treatment services: monthly, quarterly, or annual.']
  capacity_charge_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual capacity reservation charge paid by the purchasing party to reserve a specific volume of supply or treatment capacity, independent of actual usage. Null if no capacity charge applies.']
  contract_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration of the agreement in years from effective start date to effective end date. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  contracted_volume_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contracted daily volume of water supply or wastewater treatment capacity in Million Gallons per Day (MGD). Represents the baseline or maximum daily volume agreed upon in the contract.']
  counterparty_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the counterparty contact person for agreement communications and notifications.']
  counterparty_contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact person name at the counterparty organization for agreement administration and operational coordination.']
  counterparty_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the counterparty contact person for operational coordination and emergency notifications.']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the wholesale customer or supplying utility party to the agreement (e.g., neighboring municipality, regional water authority, industrial facility).']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty organization: municipality, water district, industrial customer, commercial customer, government agency, or neighboring utility.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who created the bulk water agreement record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bulk water agreement record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_point_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the physical location where water is delivered or wastewater is received, including interconnection point, meter location, or facility name.']
  delivery_point_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery or acceptance point for GIS mapping and asset management integration.']
  delivery_point_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery or acceptance point for GIS mapping and asset management integration.']
  delivery_pressure_psi_max decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable delivery pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) at the interconnection point to prevent damage to the receiving utilitys infrastructure.']
  delivery_pressure_psi_min decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum required delivery pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI) at the interconnection point to meet the purchasing utilitys distribution system requirements.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the bulk water agreement expires or terminates. Null for open-ended agreements or those with automatic renewal provisions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the bulk water agreement becomes legally binding and service delivery or acceptance obligations commence.']
  emergency_supply_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes provisions for emergency water supply during outages, droughts, or infrastructure failures. True if emergency provisions exist, False otherwise.']
  emergency_supply_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed description of emergency supply terms, including trigger conditions, maximum emergency volume, duration limits, and emergency pricing. Null if no emergency provisions exist.']
  intergovernmental_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an intergovernmental agreement between two public entities (municipalities, water districts, government agencies), which may have special legal status and exemptions. True if intergovernmental, False otherwise.']
  late_payment_penalty_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage penalty applied to overdue balances for late payment, expressed as an annual percentage rate or per-period rate as specified in the agreement.']
  metering_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for installation, maintenance, and calibration of metering equipment at the delivery point: supplier, purchaser, or shared responsibility.']
  minimum_take_volume_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum daily volume in Million Gallons per Day (MGD) that the purchasing party is obligated to take or pay for under take-or-pay provisions. Null if no minimum take obligation exists.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified the bulk water agreement record in the system.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bulk water agreement record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special provisions, historical context, or operational considerations related to the bulk water agreement.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due from the counterparty.']
  peak_volume_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable daily volume in Million Gallons per Day (MGD) that can be delivered or accepted under the agreement, representing peak capacity limits.']
  pricing_structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing mechanism used in the agreement: flat rate (fixed monthly/annual charge), volumetric (per-gallon charge), tiered (volume-based tiers), demand charge (capacity reservation fee), take-or-pay (minimum volume commitment), or cost-plus (cost recovery plus margin).']
  rate_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Description of the rate escalation mechanism in the agreement, such as annual CPI adjustment, fixed percentage increase, or cost-of-service pass-through provisions.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority formally approved the bulk water agreement and its terms.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or docket number assigned by the regulatory authority when approving the bulk water agreement and its rate structure.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or Public Utilities Commission that has jurisdiction over the agreement and must approve rates and terms (e.g., state PUC, EPA regional office, primacy agency).']
  renewal_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years of each automatic renewal period if auto-renewal provisions apply. Null if no auto-renewal.']
  take_or_pay_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a take-or-pay provision requiring the purchasing party to pay for a minimum volume regardless of actual usage. True if take-or-pay applies, False otherwise.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate or not renew the agreement.']
  water_quality_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the water quality specifications that delivered water must meet, typically citing EPA Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA) standards, state primacy agency requirements, or specific contractual quality parameters (e.g., turbidity NTU limits, disinfectant residual, MCL compliance).']

  Note: 'Records negotiated special service agreements with large industrial, municipal, wholesale, or intergovernmental customers that deviate from standard published tariff terms. Covers wholesale bulk water supply, industrial user contracts, intergovernmental agreements, and special pricing arrangements. Captures agreement parties, contract type, negotiated rate terms, volume commitments (MGD), demand charge structure, delivery specifications (pressure, quality), take-or-pay provisions, contract duration, renewal terms, special conditions (IUP compliance, pretreatment requirements), emergency supply terms, and regulatory approval status. Distinct from standard retail service agreements governed by published tariffs.'
}

Table service.offering_territory_availability {
  offering_territory_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this offering-territory availability record. Primary key.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service offering being made available in this territory']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service territory where this offering is available']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this offering within this territory. Available = open for new enrollments; Suspended = temporarily unavailable due to capacity or regulatory issues; Pending_Approval = awaiting regulatory authorization; Discontinued = no longer offered; Restricted = available only to specific customer classes.']
  capacity_reserved_mgd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Amount of system capacity in million gallons per day reserved or allocated for this offering within this territory. Used for capacity planning and to ensure infrastructure can support the offering\'s demand.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this availability record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this offering was discontinued or became unavailable in this specific territory. Null indicates ongoing availability. Territory-specific discontinuation may occur due to franchise agreement changes or regulatory decisions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this offering became available for customer enrollment within this specific territory. This is territory-specific and may differ from the offering\'s global effective_start_date due to phased rollouts or territory-specific regulatory approvals.']
  franchise_authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority or franchise agreement authorized the utility to provide this specific offering within this territory. Critical for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  marketing_launch_date date [note: 'Date when marketing and customer outreach for this offering began in this territory. May differ from effective_start_date due to phased rollout strategies.']
  priority_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offering receives priority deployment or enhanced service levels in this territory, often due to regulatory mandates, environmental justice requirements, or infrastructure investment priorities.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the territory-specific regulatory approval, rate case docket, or Public Utilities Commission order authorizing this offering in this territory. May differ from the offering\'s global tariff_reference when territory-specific approvals are required.']
  territory_specific_rate_adjustment decimal(15,4) [note: 'Multiplier or adjustment factor applied to the offering\'s base rates for this specific territory. Used when regulatory authorities approve territory-specific rate variations due to infrastructure costs, population density, or other geographic factors. 1.0 = no adjustment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this availability record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the availability and authorization of specific water and wastewater service offerings within defined geographic service territories. It captures the regulatory approval, rate adjustments, and lifecycle management of which offerings are available in which territories. Each record links one offering to one service_territory with attributes that exist only in the context of this territory-specific availability, including territory-specific rate adjustments, franchise authorization dates, and availability status that cannot be stored on either the offering or territory alone.. Existence Justification: Water utilities operate service offerings across multiple geographic territories, each with distinct franchise agreements, regulatory approvals, and rate structures. A single offering (e.g., potable water supply) is made available in multiple territories (Downtown District, North County, etc.), and each territory offers multiple service types (potable, wastewater, recycled water). The utility actively manages which offerings are available in which territories, with territory-specific rate adjustments, regulatory authorizations, and lifecycle states that cannot be stored on either the offering or territory entity alone.'
}

Table service.service_program_enrollment {
  service_program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the program enrollment record. Primary key.']
  account_billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the billing account enrolled in the conservation program']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service agreement associated with this enrollment.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to billing_account']
  primary_service_program_conservation_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the conservation, assistance, or demand management program.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date when the customer submitted their application to enroll in this conservation program.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment application was approved by program administrators.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment automatically renews at the end of the term.']
  baseline_consumption_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Customer water consumption baseline measured in gallons prior to program enrollment. Used to calculate savings.']
  baseline_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the baseline measurement period used to establish pre-program consumption.']
  baseline_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the baseline measurement period used to establish pre-program consumption.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the enrollment was cancelled or terminated.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for enrollment cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|non_compliance|program_ended|moved|ineligible|duplicate|other — promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the enrollment was cancelled.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the customer successfully completed all program requirements.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this enrollment record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the program enrollment ends or is scheduled to end. Nullable for open-ended enrollments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the program enrollment becomes active and benefits begin.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'Date when customer eligibility was verified.']
  eligibility_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the utility verified that this billing account met the eligibility criteria for this conservation program.']
  eligibility_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer eligibility for the program has been verified (e.g., income verification for assistance programs).']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer enrolled in the program (online portal, phone, mail, in-person, mobile app, agent-assisted).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the billing account enrolled in this conservation program. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the billing account completed or withdrew from this conservation program enrollment. Null for active enrollments.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique enrollment reference number for tracking and customer communication.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this enrollment (active, pending, completed, withdrawn, suspended). Identified in detection phase as program_status relationship data.']
  incentive_amount_approved decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary incentive amount approved for this enrollment in USD. Includes rebates, credits, or financial assistance.']
  incentive_amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual monetary incentive amount paid to the customer in USD.']
  incentive_amount_received decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary incentive amount paid to this billing account for participation in this specific conservation program. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  incentive_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the incentive payment was issued to the customer.']
  incentive_payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the incentive payment to the customer.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this enrollment record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special circumstances, or administrative comments about the enrollment.']
  program_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this program enrollment record. Primary key.']
  program_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the program enrollment commitment measured in months.']
  regulatory_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulatory program category for compliance reporting (e.g., state-mandated conservation, drought response).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment must be included in regulatory conservation compliance reporting.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when program compliance or completion was verified by utility staff or third-party auditor.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the verification process for program compliance and savings achievement.']
  verified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the staff member or auditor who performed the verification.']
  water_savings_achieved varchar [note: 'Measured water savings in gallons achieved by this billing account through participation in this specific conservation program. Identified in detection phase as relationship data.']
  water_savings_actual_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual water savings achieved through this enrollment measured in gallons. Calculated from meter reading analysis or engineering estimates.']
  water_savings_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate actual water savings (e.g., meter-based analysis, engineering estimate, deemed savings).']
  water_savings_target_gallons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target water savings goal for this enrollment measured in gallons. Used for conservation program performance tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment relationship between a billing account and a conservation program. It captures the participation of a customer account in a specific water conservation program, tracking enrollment lifecycle, incentive payments received, and water savings achieved. Each record links one billing account to one conservation program with attributes that exist only in the context of this enrollment relationship.. Existence Justification: In water utility operations, billing accounts (customers) can simultaneously enroll in multiple conservation programs (toilet rebate, irrigation audit, rain barrel incentive, drought surcharge programs), and each conservation program serves many billing accounts. The utility actively manages these enrollments as operational business entities, tracking enrollment lifecycle, processing incentive payments, measuring water savings per account-program combination, and verifying eligibility. This is not an analytical correlation but an operational business process where enrollment records are created, updated, and closed by program administrators.'
}

Table service.program_material_eligibility {
  program_material_eligibility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this program-material eligibility record. Primary key.']
  conservation_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the conservation program offering the rebate or incentive']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the eligible material, fixture, or device']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this material was approved for eligibility under this conservation program by program management or regulatory authority.']
  eligibility_end_date date [note: 'Date when rebates for this material under this program expire. May differ from program end date if materials are phased out early.']
  eligibility_start_date date [note: 'Date when rebates for this material under this program become effective. May differ from program start date if materials are added mid-program.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this material\'s eligibility under this program: Active (accepting claims), Suspended (temporarily unavailable), Expired (no longer eligible), Pending (awaiting approval).']
  eligible_quantity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units of this material that a customer can claim rebates for under this program (e.g., max 2 high-efficiency toilets per household).']
  minimum_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Required efficiency certification or rating for this material to qualify (e.g., WaterSense certified, EPA WaterSense, ENERGY STAR). Specific to this program-material combination.']
  program_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when this material became available for customer enrollment and rebate claims under this program.']
  rebate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Specific rebate or incentive amount offered for this material under this program. May differ from the program\'s default incentive_amount based on material type or efficiency rating.']
  total_rebate_amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount paid in rebates for this material under this program. Used for budget tracking against program allocation.']
  total_rebates_issued varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of rebates issued for this material under this program to date. Used for budget tracking and program evaluation.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the eligibility relationship between conservation programs and materials. It captures which materials (fixtures, devices, equipment) are eligible for rebates or incentives under specific conservation programs. Each record links one conservation program to one eligible material with program-specific rebate amounts, quantity limits, and eligibility periods that exist only in the context of this program-material combination.. Existence Justification: Conservation programs in water utilities offer rebates and incentives for multiple eligible materials (high-efficiency toilets, smart irrigation controllers, low-flow fixtures, etc.). Each material can be eligible under multiple programs simultaneously (e.g., a WaterSense toilet may qualify for both a general water efficiency program and a low-income assistance program). The utility actively manages this eligibility catalog, setting program-specific rebate amounts, quantity limits, and eligibility periods for each program-material combination to support customer enrollment and budget tracking.'
}

Ref: service.service_program_enrollment.agreement_id > service.agreement.agreement_id
Ref: service.service_program_enrollment.billing_account_id > billing.billing_account.billing_account_id
Ref: service.service_program_enrollment.primary_service_program_conservation_program_id > service.conservation_program.conservation_program_id
Ref: service.program_material_eligibility.conservation_program_id > service.conservation_program.conservation_program_id
Ref: service.program_material_eligibility.material_master_id > supply.material_master.material_master_id
